## SB 1.17.39 | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | 20-03-2023 | Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse

**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/X_jcFTkll88?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Om Agyanati Miran Dhasya Gyanan Janashalakaya Chakshur Unmilitam Yena Tasmai Shri Gurave Namaha"
**Summary:** The session begins with traditional Vaishnava prayers and mantras, invoking blessings and paying respects to spiritual teachers.

Om Agyanati Miran Dhasya Gyanan Janashalakaya Chakshur Unmilitam Yena Tasmai Shri Gurave Namaha. Nama Om Vishnupadaya Krishna Preshtaya Bhutale Shri Mate Bhakti Vedanta Swamin Nithinamine. Namaste Saraswate Deve Gauravani Pracharine Nirvishesha Shunyavadi Pashchatya Deshatarine. Jaya Shri Krishna Chaitanya Prabhu Nithyananda Shri Advaita Gadadhara Shri Vasadi Gauravakta Vrinda. Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare. Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya Narayanam Namaskritya Naram Chai Vanarottamam Deveem Saraswateem Vyasam Tato Jayam Udi Rayet.

### Kali, sin, governance
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/X_jcFTkll88?t=50
**Verse References:** SB 1.17.39
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So, if you remember the previous verse Kali was given four places by Maharaj Pariksit where gambling goes on, where drinking liquor is indulged in, where there is illicit connection with women, prostitution and where there is animal slaughter."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the context of the 39th verse of the 17th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam, explaining how King Pariksit initially restricted Kali to four places associated with sinful activities.

In this session, we are discussing from the 39th verse of the 17th chapter of the 1st canto of Shri Madbhagavatam. The meaning of this 39th verse is: The personality of Kali asked for something more, and because of his begging, King Pariksit gave him permission to live where there is gold. Because wherever there is gold, there is also falsity, intoxication, lust, envy, and enmity. So, if you remember the previous verse, Kali was given four places by Maharaj Pariksit: where gambling goes on, where drinking liquor is indulged in, where there is illicit connection with women, prostitution, and where there is animal slaughter.

### Kali, gold, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/X_jcFTkll88?t=123
**Verse References:** SB 1.17.39
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "the king gave him permission to live where there is gold where this gold means where people have accumulated gold for purposes other than devotional service other than for serving krishna other than krishna conscious purpose"
**Summary:** The lecture explains that Kali Purusha requested another place to reside because Pariksit's kingdom was free of sinful activities; Pariksit then allowed Kali to reside where gold is accumulated for non-devotional purposes.

Now, Kali Purusha asked something more because at the time Pariksit was ruling, Kali Purusha could not find in Pariksit's kingdom any place where these four activities were going on. So, he asked for some place, some other place other than the place where these four activities, these sinful activities, are indulged in. And because the Kali Purusha had surrendered to Pariksit and he was begging for one more place, some other place, the king gave him permission to live where there is gold. Where this gold means where people have accumulated gold for purposes other than devotional service, other than for serving Krishna, other than Krishna conscious purpose. If they have got gold accumulated in their home or in their state, anywhere, then that place also Pariksit gave Kali Purusha a place to reside. Why? Why was Kali Purusha given a fifth place to reside?

### gold, falsity, devotional service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/X_jcFTkll88?t=202
**Verse References:** SB 1.17.39
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "wherever there is gold which is not utilized for devotional service there is also falsity intoxication lust envy and enmity these five things are there wherever gold is accumulated and not utilized for uh devotional service"
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on why gold accumulated without devotional purpose becomes a dwelling place for Kali, leading to falsity, intoxication, lust, envy, and enmity, referencing Srila Prabhupada's explanation.

Because wherever there is gold which is not utilized for devotional service, there is also falsity, intoxication, lust, envy, and enmity. These five things are there wherever gold is accumulated and not utilized for devotional service. So, in the purport, Srila Prabhupada explains, although Maharaj Pariksit gave Kali permission to live in four places, it was very difficult for Kali Purusha to find the places because during the reign of Maharaj Pariksit, there were no such places. This is called a perfect rule of a perfect king, a pure devotee. So, during Maharaj Pariksit's time, during Yudhishtira Maharaj's time, during the time of Ambarish Maharaj, during the time of all those great kings who were ruling strictly according to the Vedic injunctions and who were themselves pure devotees, then the entire kingdom, there were no places where such sinful activities were indulged in by anybody else. Therefore, Prabhupada says, Kali asked the king to give him something practical which could be utilized for his nefarious purposes.

### adharma, kali, wealth
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/X_jcFTkll88?t=292
**Verse References:** SB 1.17.39
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "What are the four defects of accumulating gold for any purpose other than devotional service? There will be falsity, Andhritam, Madham, intoxication, Kamam, lust. And enviousness, enviousness."
**Summary:** The speaker explains Kali Purusha's role as a friend of Adharma and how Parikshit Maharaj tried to protect his citizens from corruption, ultimately giving Kali a fifth place where gold is accumulated for non-devotional purposes, leading to falsity, intoxication, lust, and envy.

Kali Purusha is described as Adharmabandhu, friend of Adharma, irreligion. So, wherever Kali Purusha resides, he will spread Adharma. And Parikshit Mahana did not want his citizens to get corrupted, to get influenced by Adharma, Adharma influence of Kali. So, therefore, Kali asked the king, you have practically cheated me. You didn't give me any place. So, then Parikshit Maharaj, because Kali had surrendered, he gave him a fifth place, that place where gold is accumulated for non-devotional purposes. So, Prabhupada says in the purport further, Maharaj Pariksit thus gave Kali permission to go. He gave Kali permission to live in a place where there is gold, because wherever there is gold, there are all the above mentioned four things, and over and above them, there is enmity also. What are the four defects of accumulating gold for any purpose other than devotional service? There will be falsity, Andhritam, Madham, intoxication, Kamam, lust, and enviousness, enviousness.

### wealth, devotional service, enmity
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/X_jcFTkll88?t=379
**Verse References:** SB 1.17.39
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "If somebody accumulates gold and does not use gold for devotional service, such gold encourages five things, falsity, intoxication, prostitution, envy and enmity."
**Summary:** The lecture details the five negative consequences of accumulating wealth without using it for devotional service: falsity, intoxication, prostitution, envy, and enmity, which can manifest at various levels of society.

So, these four defects of accumulating wealth or gold for any purpose other than devotional service, these four things are there. And also the fifth thing, over and above these four, there is enmity also that will breed among the family members or among the citizens of the state or among the members of a community or nation or even internationally. These four defects will be seen in the people if there is gold or wealth accumulated without being used for devotional service. So, Srila Prabhupada further says, according to Srimad Bhagavatam, gold encourages falsity, intoxication, prostitution, envy, and enmity. This is what the Bhagavatam teaches. If somebody accumulates gold and does not use gold for devotional service, such gold encourages five things: falsity, intoxication, prostitution, envy, and enmity.

### gold standard, falsity, government
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/X_jcFTkll88?t=472
**Verse References:** SB 1.17.39
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Gold standard currency is based on falsehood because the currency is not on a par with the reserve gold."
**Summary:** The speaker transitions to a practical application of the principle, criticizing the gold standard exchange in currency as inherently flawed due to the disparity between the value of currency notes and the actual gold reserves held by the government, thus promoting falsity.

Even a gold standard exchange in currency is bad. Now, Prabhupada is talking about the government policy. Gold standard currency is based on falsehood because the currency is not on a par with the reserve gold. Now, that is the system most countries have, including India, that they have currency notes issued by the government. Now, what is the value of that currency note, the piece of paper? Now, they say, the government says that this paper has got a value because it is valuable because it is based on the reserve gold that is kept stored in the government safe deposit vaults. But, the reality is the government issues currency notes not exactly based on the value of the gold stored in their vaults. They issue much more currency notes than the gold there. So, Prabhupada says the basic principle is falsity.

---
### inflation, economy, government policy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/X_jcFTkll88?t=555
**Verse References:** SB 1.17.39
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "This artificial inflation of currency that means they are giving issuing more notes than what they should be issuing. This is called artificial inflation. By the authorities encourages prostitution of the state economy."
**Summary:** The lecture continues to critique the issuance of currency notes beyond the value of reserve gold, terming it 'artificial inflation' and arguing that it leads to the 'prostitution' and ruin of the state economy.

How gold encourages falsity is a good example here, right here. The basic principle is falsity because currency notes are issued in value beyond that of the actual reserve gold. For example, if let us say the government has gold worth 100 crores. The government has gold worth let us say 100 million rupees just to give you an example. If the total currency notes in circulation, printed currency notes, is more than 100 million rupees, then that is a falsity, and that is what exactly is happening. So, Prabhupada says currency notes are issued in value beyond that of the actual reserve gold. So, there is falsity right here by the government itself. See, the government should themselves not be practicing falsity, and they should regulate the citizens to avoid falsity. Now, here government itself is not practicing falsity. Government itself is practicing falsity when they issue currency notes beyond the value of the reserve gold. So, what happens if they issue such currency? This artificial inflation of currency, that means they are giving issuing more notes than what they should be issuing. This is called artificial inflation. By the authorities encourages prostitution of the state economy. The state economy is completely spoiled. It is ruined. The state economy is ruined.

### inflation, commodities, economy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/X_jcFTkll88?t=670
**Verse References:** SB 1.17.39
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "The price of commodities becomes artificially inflated because of bad money or artificial currency notes."
**Summary:** The speaker explains how artificial inflation leads to increased commodity prices due to an imbalance between the supply of currency and the availability of goods, ultimately leading to a situation where people have money but goods are scarce.

What happens? How is the economy ruined? Prabhupada says the price of commodities becomes artificially inflated because of bad money or artificial currency notes. See, people have more currency notes in their hand than the actual reserve gold, and the value of the goods that are available. These currency notes are just a medium of exchange. So, if I have currency notes worth let us say 10,000 rupees, if these 10,000 rupees can purchase some goods worth what they say is worth 10,000, but if the equation is not commensurate with actual total value of the goods, then there is inflation, artificial inflation in the price of the commodities. The price of the commodities will go up because the supply of the goods will not correspond with the supply of the currency notes. There will be more currency notes in supply and the goods will be less. So, what happens? People's buying power will exhaust all the goods and people will have money, but there will be no goods. So, the price of the commodities will naturally go up when there is scarcity. Bad money drives away good money. So, this is what is called as bad money.

### gold coins, currency, regulation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/X_jcFTkll88?t=775
**Verse References:** SB 1.17.39
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Instead of paper currency, actual gold coin should be used for exchange."
**Summary:** The speaker advocates for the use of gold coins as currency, suggesting that the government should mint coins from its gold reserves to prevent the misuse of gold and regulate the quantity of gold ornaments women can possess to discourage lust, envy, and enmity.

The currency notes issued in excess of the value of the stored gold. So, Prabhupada says instead of paper currency, actual gold coin should be used for exchange. Whatever the government has got the reserve gold, they should make gold coins and that should be the currency. That should be the medium of exchange, and this will stop the prostitution of gold. Gold will not be misused anymore. Gold ornaments for women may be allowed by control, not by quality, but by quantity. What about women using gold ornaments? That was always there in the Vedic culture. So, now how can government avoid people accumulating too much gold in the form of gold ornaments for women? So, government has to regulate by quantity the gold that people possess. Otherwise, if they possess too much of gold, that will simply lead to lust, envy, enmity, etc. So, by restricting the quantity of gold people can have, this will discourage lust, envy, and enmity. When there is actual gold currency in the form of coins, the influence of gold in producing falsity will automatically cease.

### Kali Yuga, sinful activities, five places
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/eH-jHmYjVs8?t=51
**Verse References:** SB 1.17.40
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Thus the personality of Kali by the directions of Maharaj Pariksit, the son of Uttara was allowed to live in those five places."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces verses 40 and 41 of the 17th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam, focusing on the five places where Kali is allowed to reside: gambling, drinking, prostitution, animal slaughter, and places where gold is accumulated for non-devotional purposes.

In this session we will be discussing from two words. Verses 40 and 41 of the 17th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. The 40th verse is as follows: Amuni Panchasthanani Hyadharma Prabhavak Kadihi Outareyena Dattani Nyavasat Tannideshakrit. So, the meaning of this 40th verse is thus the person, the personality of Kali by the directions of Maharaj Pariksit, the son of Uttara was allowed to live in those five places. So, what are the five places? The four places where the four sinful activities: gambling, drinking, prostitution, animal slaughter. These four places and the fifth place is where gold is accumulated.

Falsity is basically different forms of cheating by gambling. So, this is what we are talking about, by gambling. Otherwise, if the religious principles are not followed, then the, the, the result of illicit sex is Varna Sankara population, Varna Sankara population. ### Kali Yuga, religious principles, counteracting measures, fasting, marriage, charity, Bhagavata Dharma
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/eH-jHmYjVs8?t=283
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy

**Key Quote:** "The counteracting process for these four sinful activities is already suggested by Prabhupada in the purport to 38th verse and the 39th verse."
**Summary:** The speaker notes the increase in irreligious practices in Kali Yuga and mentions Srila Prabhupada's suggested counteracting measures from verses 38 and 39, including compulsory fasting, marriage, and charity for the propagation of Bhagavata Dharma.

So, we can see how the number of people who are, on, not following the religious principles is increasing as Kali Yuga progresses and there is so much of indulgence in intoxication, meat eating, gambling, illicit sex so much so much and there are same people there are sober people who want to drive away corruption but Prabhupada says the counteracting process for these four sinful activities is already suggested by Prabhupada in the purport to 38th verse and the 39th verse so 38th verse Prabhupada suggested two compulsory days of fasting observing the Kali Shivrata and then a compulsory marriage for those who want to associate with a member of the opposite sex then citizens of state must give in charity up to 50 percent of their income for the propagation of Bhagavata Dharma and the five ways in which Bhagavata Dharma should be propagated in the state all these recommendations are meant for actually counteracting the evil influence of kali in this kali yuga.

### brahmanas, sannyasis, varnashrama system, spiritual masters
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/eH-jHmYjVs8?t=491
**Verse References:** —

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Sannyasis are spiritual masters for everyone."
**Summary:** The speaker clarifies the roles of brahmanas and sannyasis within the varnashrama system, explaining that brahmanas are religious preceptors for other castes, while sannyasis are spiritual masters for all castes and orders of society, even brahmanas.

So in the purport Srila Prabhupada says the brahmanas are the religious preceptors for all other castes. In the varnashrama system there are four social divisions brahmana, kshatriya, vaishya, shudra. So of these four divisions the division of brahmanas they are the spiritualists, the spiritual teachers for the kshatriyas, vaishyas and shudras. And the sannyasis are spiritual masters for all castes and orders of society. Sannyasis are among the brahmanas those who are in the renounced order, those who are renunciants. They are completely detached from all major duties. They are completely detached from all major duties, all material connections. So sannyasis are the spiritual masters even of the brahmanas. So sannyasis are spiritual masters for everyone.

**Summary:** The lecture begins with traditional Vaishnava invocations and the recitation of Srimad Bhagavatam 1.17.42, setting the stage for a discussion about Parikshit Maharaj and the restoration of dharma.

Om Agyanati Miran Dhasya Gyananjan Shalakaya Chaksharan Militam Yena Tasmai Shri Gurave Namaha. Nama Om Vishnu Padaya Krishna Preshtaya Bhutale Shri Mate Bhakti Vedanta Swamin Nithinamine. Namaste Saraswate Deve Gauravani Pracharine Nirvishesha Shunyavadi Pashchatya Deshatarine. Jaya Shri Krishna Chaitanya Prabhu Nithyananda Shri Advaita Gadadhara Shiva Sadhi Gauravakta Vrinda. Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare. Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya Narayanam Namaskritya Naram Chai Vanarottamam. Devi Saraswati Vyasam Tato Jayam Udi Rayet. Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya. In this session we will be discussing from the 42nd verse of the 17th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. Vrishasya Nashtam Streen Paadan Tapas Chowcham Dayamiti Pratisandhatha Ashwasya Mahimcha Samavardhayat. The meaning of this verse is: Thereafter the king re-established the lost legs of the personality of religion, the bull, and by encouraging activities, he sufficiently improved the condition of the earth. Vrishasya Nashtam Streen Paadan Tapas Chowcham Dayamiti Pratisandhatha Ashwasya Mahimcha Samavardhayat. So this is Suta Goswami describing the result of Parikshit Maharaj.

### gold, misuse, sankirtan yajna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/OFoMC9EXZo0?t=489
**Verse References:** SB 1.17.42
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Maharaj Parikshit, therefore, like his grandfather Arjuna, collected all illicit gold kept for the propensities of Kali and employed it in the Sankirtan Yajna as per the instruction of the Srimad Bhagavatam."
**Summary:** The discussion centers on the nature of gold as a generator of negative qualities, but also its potential for positive utilization under proper guidance, specifically highlighting Parikshit Maharaj's use of illicit gold in Sankirtan Yajna.

Gold is certainly a generator of falsity, intoxication, prostitution, enmity and violence. But, under the guidance of a proper king or public leader or a Brahmin or Sanyasi, the same gold can be properly utilized to reestablish the lost legs of the bull, the personality of religion. By itself, wealth or gold is not bad. The improper or proper utilization, that matters. How you use that gold is important. If you use it properly, you can use it to maintain or restore the lost legs of Dharma. Or, people may misuse it and further degradation sets in and the Dharma becomes lost. So, Srila Prabhupada says, specifically what did he do? Maharaj Parikshit, therefore, like his grandfather Arjuna, collected all illicit gold kept for the propensities of Kali and employed it for the purpose of and employed it in the Sankirtan Yajna as per the instruction of the Srimad Bhagavatam. Bhagavatam says, in Kali Yuga, other types of Yajna are not practical. In the Bhagavad Gita and all the scriptures it is said, work should be done as Yajna so that people don't become entangled in this material world. So, there are varieties of Yajna that one may perform, but in Kali Yuga, the most practical and feasible type of Yajna is Sankirtan Yajna. So therefore, Parikshit Maharaj, like his grandfather, Arjuna, he employed all the illicit gold kept for propensities of Kali. Illicit gold means gold that has been accumulated for sense enjoyment that he utilized in Sankirtan Yajna. And that is a recommendation of the Bhagavatam.

Lord means the Supreme Lord Krishna. 25% for the family members. And 25% for personal necessities. So, dividing one's wealth. This for whom? For such people, there is no need of any such business division to be made because they don't have that for dividing like this but for those who are having excess wealth than what is minimum necessary there is a a way of dividing the wealth into three parts what is that 50% for service of Krishna 25% for the family members.

### Maharaj Pariksit, Suta Goswami, Naimisharanya, Romaharshana Suta
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/8FfJL5AC2OM?t=120
**Verse References:** SB 1.17.43-44
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The most fortunate emperor Maharaj Pariksit, who was entrusted with the kingdom of Hastinapura by Maharaj Yudhishthira when Maharaj Yudhishthira desired to retire to the forest, is now ruling the world with great success due to his being glorified by the deeds of the kings of the Kuru dynasty."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the meaning of the verses and discusses the context of Suta Goswami speaking in the present tense about Maharaj Pariksit, mentioning the sacrifice at Naimisharanya and the role of Romaharshana Suta.

The meaning of these two verses is given together by Srila Prabhupada: The most fortunate emperor Maharaj Pariksit, who was entrusted with the kingdom of Hastinapura by Maharaj Yudhishthira when Maharaj Yudhishthira desired to retire to the forest, is now ruling the world with great success due to his being glorified by the deeds of the kings of the Kuru dynasty. So in the purport Srila Prabhupada says the prolonged sacrificial ceremonies undertaken by the sages of Naimisharanya were begun shortly after the demise of Maharaj Pariksit. You see there is a very important point, the discussion that is going on which is being narrated here between Suta Goswami and the sages of Naimisharanya, that they began a thousand year sacrifice towards the end of Dwapar Yuga and beginning of Kali Yuga, that period of time. So when did it happen? The prolonged ceremonies undertaken by the sages of Naimisharanya were begun shortly after the demise of Maharaj Pariksit, after the demise of Maharaj Pariksit. So the question will arise, why is Suta Goswami speaking in the present tense? In this verse he is telling Maharaj Pariksit is now ruling the world with great success. Why is he talking in the present tense? So that will be explained by Srila Prabhupada. The sacrifice was to continue for one thousand years. And it is understood that in the beginning, some of the contemporaries of Baladev, the elder brother of Lord Krishna, also visited the sacrificial place. There is a description in the Bhagavatam elsewhere that this thousand year sacrifice was begun by the father of Suta Goswami. And this Suta Goswami referred to in the beginning of this Bhagavatam, who is answering the questions by the Sayyiduna Misharanya, is called Ugrashrava Suta. But this thousand year sacrifice was begun by the Sayyiduna Misharanya under the guidance of Ugrashrava Suta's father, whose name was Romaharshana Suta. So, Romaharshana Suta was actually, he was reciting the Puranas, because he was the disciple of Vyasa Deva, who was expert in the Puranic literature, the fifth Veda, Puranic literature.

---

says you cannot drive out corruption simply by some vigilance department or some anti-corruption ministry etc. By legislation you cannot drive out alone. Legislation alone is not going to work. Ittham bhutanubhavoyam abhimanyo suto nripah yasya palayatakshonim yuyam satraya dikshitaha Maharaj Pariksit, the son of Abhimanyu, is so experienced that by dint of his expert administration, he has been able to do so many things. He has been able to do so many things. He has been able to do so many things. He has been able to do so many things. He has been able to do so many things. and patronage, it has been possible for you to perform a sacrifice such as this. So, Suta Goswami is telling, Pariksit's administration is perfect, was perfect. Now, it's in the past tense because the Bhagavatam recitation began after Pariksit's, this second time Bhagavatam recitation began after Pariksit's, this second time Bhagavatam recitation began after Pariksit's, this second time Bhagavatam recitation began after Pariksit's, this second time began after Pariksit's, this second time Bhagavatam recitation began after Pariksit's, this second the first time it was recited when parikshit was cursed that we explained in the next chapter in more detail so the suta goswami ro sorry uh ugrashrava suta heard this bhagavatam from shukadeva goswami when parikshit heard and now he's reciting this bhagavatam the way he heard it in the past and therefore ugrashrava suta speaking is that this sacrifice thousand year sacrifice was begun when parikshit maharaj is ruling and it has been possible to perform this sacrifice because of the wonderful administration of maharaj parikshit so i'll stop here thank you very much harikrishnan

## SB 1.18.1-2 | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | 25-03-2023 | Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### Srimad Bhagavatam, Krishna Katha, Devotee Eagerness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/CGZkMtd3VI4?t=49
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Their meeting is completely transcendental, completely spiritual, so these topics are also Krishna Katha."
**Summary:** The lecture begins with an introduction to the 18th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam, explaining the context of Suta Goswami reciting the Bhagavatam to the sages of Naimisharanya and their eagerness to hear about Krishna and his devotees.

In this session, we will be discussing from two verses, the first and second verse of the 18th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. We are beginning a new chapter in this session. The 18th chapter is entitled Maharaj Pariksit Cursed by a Brahmana Boy. If you remember, this first canto began by the sage Naimisharanya asking six questions to begin with. This was a print of this discussion from the Ugrashrava Suta, Suta Goswami. Suta Goswami answered these questions, and he saw the sages are very eager to hear about Krishna. So when he saw the eagerness to hear about Krishna, then he said I am going to recite the Srimad Bhagavatam as I heard it from Sukadeva Goswami, when Sukadeva Goswami is speaking Bhagavatam seven days continuously to Parikshit Maharaj when Parikshit Maharaj is about to quit his body by cursed by a brahmana body. So before Suta Goswami could begin reciting the Bhagavatam that he heard from Sukadeva Goswami, the sage Brahmishananya had so many more questions: please tell us before you begin reciting Bhagavatam, please tell us about Parikshit Maharaj, please tell us about Sukadeva Goswami, please tell us how did Parikshit Maharaj meet Sukadeva Goswami, how did they meet and we have heard that Parikshit Maharaj's birth activities and even his death is very very glorious, please describe in detail these topics which pertain to Krishna, Krishna means Krishna and his pure devotees. So since Sukadeva Goswami is a pure devotee of Krishna, Parikshit Maharaj is a pure devotee of Krishna, their meeting is completely transcendental, completely spiritual, so these topics are also Krishna Katha.

### Parikshit Maharaj, Kali, Wonderful Administration
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/CGZkMtd3VI4?t=177
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Suta Goswami is equally anxious to describe Maharaj Parikshit's wonderful birth and death, and this verse is stated by Suta Goswami to increase the interest of the sage tsunami shananya that his birth was wonderful, now hear about his death, even that is very very wonderful."
**Summary:** This segment discusses Suta Goswami's eagerness to describe the birth and death of Emperor Parikshit, highlighting his wonderful administration, particularly his handling of Kali, and setting the stage for the upcoming narration.

So the sages wanted that therefore Suta Goswami began to recite the birth of Emperor Parikshit, how he took charge of ruling from his Suta Goswami and how he took charge of ruling from his Suta Goswami and how he took charge of ruling great from his grandfathers Yudhishtira and Arjuna and how Parikshit Maharaj happened to meet Sukadeva Goswami, that is the last two chapters. Now Maharaj Parikshit cursed by a brahmana boy, the 18th chapter and 19th chapter is the meeting of Parikshit Maharaj and Sukadeva Goswami. So now the first verse of the 18th chapter, Sukadeva Goswami said due to the mercy of the personality of God Shri Krishna who acts wonderfully, Maharaj Parikshit though struck by the weapon of the son of Drona in his mother's womb could not be. So in the purport Srila Prabhupada says the sages of Naimisharanya became struck with wonder after hearing about the wonderful administration of Maharaj Parikshit especially in reference to his punishing the personality of Kali and making him completely unable to do any harm within the kingdom. See Parikshit Maharaj gave shelter to Kali, but who's Kali? He is the friend of irreligion, adharma bandhu, he's got nefarious purpose. So Parikshit Maharaj gave him shelter at the same time he incapacitated Kali Purusha to do any harm within his kingdom. Parikshit protected his citizens against influence of Kali by taking proper steps, so they were amazed what a great and wonderful personality is Parikshit Maharaj. Suta Goswami is equally anxious to describe Maharaj Parikshit's wonderful birth and death, and this verse is stated by Suta Goswami to increase the interest of the sage tsunami shananya that his birth was wonderful, now hear about his death, even that is very very very wonderful.

### Surrender, Fearlessness, Material Existence
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/CGZkMtd3VI4?t=343
**Verse References:** BG 13
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Only a pure devotee of the Supreme Lord is fearless in this material existence."
**Summary:** This section focuses on Maharaj Parikshit's fearlessness due to his surrender to Krishna, contrasting it with the miseries inherent in material existence, such as birth, death, disease, and old age.

Second verse, brahma kopa utthitaad yastu brahma kopa utthitaad yastu brahma kopa utthitaad yastu takshakath phduina prihlavathu nasamuhamoha urubhayat bhagavati arpitashayaha. The meaning of the second verse, furthermore Maharaj Parikshit was always consciously surrounded to the personality of Godhead and therefore he was neither afraid nor overwhelmed by fear due to a snake bird which was to bite him because he was afraid of him and of the fury of a brahmana boy. Before describing the details of Pariksit being cursed by a brahmana boy for an untimely death due to the curse being bitten by a snake bird, Suta Goswami is telling the sage Nami Sharanya that Pariksit Maharaj was not afraid of death itself, what to speak of fear of death due to a curse being bitten by a snake bird, he had no fear at all. Why? Because he is always surrendered to the personality of Godhead Krishna. So in the purport Srila Prabhupada explains this point. A self-surrendered devotee of the Lord is called Narayana, Narayana, Parayana. One is completely surrendered to the Supreme Lord Narayana and is very much attached to the Lord. Such a person is never afraid of any place or person, not even of death. Only a pure devotee of the Supreme Lord is fearless in this material existence. Otherwise in the material existence there are always these four right components, one is a pure devotee of the Lord and one is a pure devotee of the Lord. So Nami Sharanya is telling us that the pure devotee of the Lord is fearlessness in this material existence. Otherwise in the material existence there are always these four fundamental problems which simply are giving so much of miseries to the living entities. Janma, Mrtyu, Jara, Vyadi, Dukha, Dosha, Anudarshanam, Krishna warns about this in the 13th chapter of the Bhagavad Gita. Material existence means these four fundamental Dukha, Dosha. Dukha means misery and Dosha means defect.

---
### Heaven and Hell, Remembrance of Narayana, Devotional Service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/CGZkMtd3VI4?t=481
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "But in all conditions and in all circumstances in this material world, remembrance of Narayana is essential if at all somebody wants to be fearless, somebody wants protection, somebody wants to avoid suffering from these conditions."
**Summary:** The discussion extends to how a surrendered devotee views heaven and hell equally, understanding them as creations of the Lord, and emphasizes the importance of constant remembrance of Narayana for fearlessness and protection in the material world.

So, nobody can avoid these four things. Once they take birth in this material world, they have to face death at a later time. And in between birth and death, there are the miseries of disease and old age. But a person who is completely surrendered to the Supreme Lord, like Maharaj Pariksit, is never afraid of any misery or any person or any situation, not at all. He is fearless. For him, that means for such a surrendered soul, nothing is as important as the Supreme Lord. Thus, he gives equal importance to heaven and hell. Why? How is it possible? That a surrendered devotee of the Supreme Lord, how does he give equal importance to heaven and hell? There is so much difference in the conditions of living in heaven and hell. The reason is, he knows well that both heaven and hell are creations of the Lord. Who is the creator of this material existence? The different planetary systems, the different arrangements, the different facilities. It is Supreme Lord himself. He is the original creator. There are secondary creators, but the original creator is the Supreme Lord. And similarly, life and death are different conditions of existence created by the Lord in this material world. It is an arrangement of the Supreme Lord that those living entities who are rebellious to death, devotional service, who are rebellious and who don't want to live with the Supreme Lord in the spiritual kingdom of God, they are placed in material existence and they are placed in situation of life and death, heaven and hell, etc. But in all conditions and in all circumstances in this material world, remembrance of Narayana is essential if at all somebody wants to be fearless, somebody wants protection, somebody wants to avoid suffering from these conditions. What is most essential and what works really ultimately is the remembrance of Narayana. Constant remembrance of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, Narayana. The Narayana Parayana practices this constantly. A pure devotee like Maharaj Pariksit, he practices remembering Narayana constantly.

---
### Curse as Blessing, Will of the Lord, Pure Devotee
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/CGZkMtd3VI4?t=670
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Anything sent by God is a blessing for the devotee."
**Summary:** The final segment explains how Maharaj Parikshit, as a pure devotee, accepted the curse as the will of the Lord, recalling how Krishna saved him in the womb, and emphasizing the devotee's surrender and fearlessness in all circumstances.

Maharaj Pariksit was such a pure devotee. He was wrongfully cursed by an inexperienced son of a brahmana. He was wrongfully cursed by an inexperienced son of a brahmana. This son of the brahmana was under the influence of Kali. Therefore, he wrongly cursed Parikshit Maharaj. And details will be described in the later part of this chapter by Suta Goswami. But here Prabhupada is just explaining how Parikshit was not afraid of death or the curse or the bite of this snake bird by which he was supposed to die. He was not afraid of that. So, Maharaj Parikshit took this curse to be sent by Narayana. How is it possible for Parikshit to consider like this? He knew that Narayana, Lord Krishna had saved him when he was burned in the womb of his mother. How did Parikshit take birth? When he was in the womb of his mother. At the end of the battle of Kurukshetra, this Dronacharya's son Ashwatthama was fighting on the side of the Kauravas and the Kauravas had lost the battle. So, he wanted to somehow take revenge against the Kauravas losing the battle. So, he tried to kill them. He tried to kill the five Pandavas and by mistake he killed the five sons of the five Pandavas, Upapandavas and by beheading them when they were asleep, fast asleep. And this was not at all appreciated by Duryodhana, his master. Duryodhana condemned this heinous act by Ashwatthama. And then Ashwatthama was actually punished by Arjuna, though his life was spared. And then he still was not, was not still giving up the opportunity to do some harm, some destruction for the Pandavas. So, what did he do? He used a Brahmastra to kill the only surviving descendant of the Pandavas who was Maharaj Parikshit. In the womb of his mother, the grandson of Arjuna, in the womb of his mother, Abhimanyu's wife, Arjuna's daughter-in-law. So, he tried to kill Parikshit in the womb of his mother. But Parikshit was saved by Krishna when this danger was there. So, Parikshit knew that he was saved by the Supreme Lord Narayana or Lord Krishna when he was in the womb of his mother. And similarly, if he were to be killed by a snake bite, it would also take place by the will of the Lord. So, the devotee never goes against the will of the Lord. Anything sent by God is a blessing for the devotee. So, devotee is completely surrendered to the Lord. His life is in the hands of the Lord. Nobody else can do anything without the Lord's sanction in the material world. Therefore, Maharaj Parikshit was neither afraid nor bewildered by such things as a curse or bite of a snake bird or death itself. That is the sign of a pure devotee of the Lord. A pure devotee is always fearless. He is always protected. He is always under the shelter of the Supreme Lord. And in this way, he is actually indifferent to all material situations and circumstances. I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

---

### Srimad Bhagavatam, Immortality, Krishna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/cUhOd7jRUlY?t=57
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "This is just like nectar for mortal beings like us."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the 11th verse of the 18th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam, explaining that hearing about Krishna's activities is like nectar for mortal beings, offering a way to counteract death.

In this session, we will be discussing from the 11th verse of the 18th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. This chapter is entitled Maharaj Pariksit Cursed by a Brahmana Boy. In the beginning of this chapter, there are descriptions of events leading to that particular incident. The 11th verse is as follows: Suta Jeevasamah Saumya Shashwative NFiSth Travisa Vishedam Yashah Astvam Shamsasie Krishnasya Martheanam Amrutham He Nahab. Meaning of this verse: The good sages said, 'Oh, grave Suther has Swami May you live many years and have eternal fame for you are speaking very nicely about the activities of Lord Krishna, the personality of Godhead. This is just like nectar for mortal beings like us. Martyanam Amrutam Hinaha.

---
### Death, Nectar, Krishna Katha
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/cUhOd7jRUlY?t=133
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "It will guarantee eternal life for whoever hears them."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the meaning of 'Martya' as a place of death and 'Amrutam' as nectar that counteracts death, emphasizing that narrations about Krishna are like Amrutam, guaranteeing eternal life to those who hear them.

Everyone in this earth planet is called Martya. Martya means this place is a place of death. Mrutyu, from Mrutyu the word Martya comes. That does not mean that people living on other planets don't die, but here particularly, the death can be perceived or can be perceived as a place of death. It can be understood as one of the fundamental problems that plague our material existence. And for somebody who is certainly going to die, the nectar, Amrutam, Amrutam means just the opposite of death, that which helps one counteract death, that which gives eternal life, Amrutam. So, this narration about Krishna is just like Amrutam. It's like drinking nectar. It will guarantee eternal life for whoever hears them.

---
### Suta Goswami, Srimad Bhagavatam, Eternal Fame
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/cUhOd7jRUlY?t=234
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Simply by speaking about Krishna, Suta Goswami is giving an opportunity for all those assembled sages to hear this nectarian narration and thereby conquer death, which is otherwise impossible."
**Summary:** The speaker describes the setting of the sages of Naimisharanya hearing Srimad Bhagavatam from Suta Goswami, highlighting Suta Goswami's glorious act of speaking about Krishna and giving the sages an opportunity to conquer death.

So, therefore, the description here is given. The sages of Naimisharanya, who assembled for a long sacrifice, they are hearing Srimad Bhagavatam from Suta Goswami. So, Suta Goswami is speaking so nicely that they are perceiving this narration about Krishna as very, very pleasing to the ears, and it also guarantees eternal life. So, therefore, they are addressing Suta Goswami and telling, may you live many years and have eternal fame. Eternal fame because Suta Goswami's action, or Suta Goswami's act, is very, very glorious. Simply by speaking about Krishna, Suta Goswami is giving an opportunity for all those assembled sages to hear this nectarian narration and thereby conquer death, which is otherwise impossible. So, Aushriya Prabhupada explains in the purport.

### Transcendental Activities, Spiritual Energy, Material Energy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/cUhOd7jRUlY?t=321
**Verse References:** BG 4.9
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna's actions are always transcendental because they are always performed under the spiritual energy."
**Summary:** The speaker quotes Srila Prabhupada, explaining why we hear about the transcendental qualities and activities of the Lord, referencing Bhagavad Gita 4.9. He distinguishes between Krishna's spiritual energy and the material energy that binds conditioned souls.

Aushriya Prabhupada explains in the purport, why do we hear about the transcendental qualities and activities of the personality of Godhead? We may always remember what has been spoken by the Lord Himself in the Bhagavad Gita, Fourth Chapter, Ninth Verse of the Bhagavad Gita. What does the Lord say? His acts, even when He acts in human society, are all transcendental, for they are all accentuated by the spiritual energy of the Lord, which is distinguished from its material energy. Krishna has two kinds of energies that are working in this world, the material energy and the spiritual energy. So the spiritual energy is the energy by which Krishna and his pure devotees, they act under that spiritual energy. And we in the material world, the conditioned souls, we are acting under the material energy. Material energy is the cause of bondage. Material energy is the cause of bondage. Material energy is the cause of bondage, contaminated by the three gunas, the three modes of material nature, whereas spiritual energy is pure, it is completely spiritual and it is free from any influence of the gunas. So there is a very, very big difference between spiritual energy and material energy. Krishna's actions are always transcendental because they are always performed under the spiritual energy. So Srila Prabhupada further says, as stated in the Bhagavad Gita, such acts are called divyam, the verse is janma karma chame divyam, transcendent all are transcendental. Krishna's actions, karma called as karma in this verse, and janma, Krishna's birth, are completely transcendental. This means that Krishna does not act or take his birth like an ordinary living being under the custody of material energy. We all take birth under the material energy.

---
### Transcendental Body, Liberation, Krishna Katha
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/cUhOd7jRUlY?t=479
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Simply by hearing Krishna katha from the right source."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts the material body, which is subject to change, with Krishna's transcendental body. He explains that understanding Krishna's transcendental nature leads to liberation and the opportunity to engage in loving devotional service in Krishna's personal abode.

So we get a material body, we have a material mind, we have material ego, all these are covering our real self, that is the spirit soul, and we are covered by these material coverings. When Krishna appears or Krishna takes birth in the material world like he did five thousand years back, he does not take birth like an ordinary living being, nor is his body material or changeable like that of ordinary living beings. Our body is always material, then we take birth in this world, we take a material body, we accept the material body. Krishna's body is not material. Now material body has got a characteristic that it is constantly changing. Body is changing from childhood to old age and the body changes even at the time of death. So we are subject to this changeable body. Now if somebody understands this fact that Krishna's body is completely transcendental and even Krishna's activities are completely spiritual, then such a person, when he understands this particular fact about Krishna, either from the Lord or from authorized sources, directly one may hear from Krishna or one may hear from a representative of Krishna coming in parampara who is a pure devotee, then what happens? Such a person is not reborn after leaving the present material body. Punar janma naiti. Krishna says in Bhagavad Gita, such an enlightened soul is admitted into the spiritual realm of the Lord and engages in the transcendental loving service of the Lord. So the destination after death is dependent on what kind of activities we perform while we are in this body, in the present life. If we hear about Krishna and we become enlightened about his transcendental nature, then we are eligible to the personal abode of the supreme lord after quitting this body at the time of death and there we can engage in loving devotional service to Krishna. So that's the perfection of life that is the highest perfection and that is possible simply by hearing Krishna katha from the right source. And that is possible simply by hearing Krishna katha from the right source.

---
### Bhagavad Gita, Srimad Bhagavatam, Krishna's Nature
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/cUhOd7jRUlY?t=698
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "The more we hear about the transcendental activities of the Lord as they are stated in the Bhagavad Gita and Srimad Bhagavatam, the more we can know about his transcendental nature."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of hearing about Krishna's transcendental activities from authentic scriptures like Bhagavad Gita and Srimad Bhagavatam, as recommended by Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, to make progress on the path back to Godhead.

So Srila Prabhupada says therefore the more we hear about the transcendental activities of the Lord as they are stated in the Bhagavad Gita and Srimad Bhagavatam, see where do you find such descriptions of the transcendental activities of the Lord? You can find it accurately described in the authentic original scriptures. So in the Bhagavad Gita and Srimad Bhagavatam, two most important scriptures, Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu teaches us, there are so many varieties of Vedic literature, innumerable, but among all of them, two particular literatures are singled out by Chaitanya Mahaprabhu. He says you just focus and concentrate and study or hear or discuss subject matter presented in two books, Bhagavad Gita and Srimad Bhagavatam. So we should hear about the transcendental activities of Krishna from the Bhagavad Gita and from the Srimad Bhagavatam. What happens for somebody who hears that? The more we hear, the more we can know about his transcendental nature. To understand the transcendental nature of Krishna is a little difficult if we consider the fact that we are used to hearing about the material activities of people in this world, but to hear about Krishna, Krishna's transcendental activities means we are making definite progress on the path back to Godhead. So this is a fact that simply by dedicating oneself to hearing about Krishna from the proper from a true representative of Shukudev Goswami, or a true representative of Krishna himself, then we definitely make progress. And by making such progress, we actually go back to Godhead.

---
### Chanting, Bhagavatam, Eternal Life
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/cUhOd7jRUlY?t=862
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "We simply become eligible to go to Krishna's personal abode if our consciousness is properly situated at the time of death."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes by reiterating Chaitanya Mahaprabhu's recommendation to chant Hare Krishna and hear Bhagavatam daily, emphasizing that this practice guarantees eternal life and eligibility to go to Krishna's personal abode if our consciousness is properly situated at the time of death.

So, the recommendation by Chaitanya Mahaprabhu is particularly available, that he says, you chant Hare Krishna, and you hear Bhagavatam every day. The Bhagavatam itself recommends, Nityam Bhagavata Sevaya, always render service to Bhagavatas, two kinds of Bhagavatas, the book Bhagavatam, and the devotee Bhagavatam, the pure devotees particularly. So, we should actually hear Bhagavatam regularly, or read Bhagavatam regularly, and that will guarantee us eternal life after quitting this body, because we simply become eligible to go to Krishna's personal abode if our consciousness is properly situated at the time of death, and such preparation of consciousness is possible by simply chanting Hare Krishna, hearing Bhagavatam daily, etc. I'll stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

**Summary:** The speaker introduces the session's focus on Srimad Bhagavatam, specifically verse 12 of the 18th chapter of the 1st canto, explaining its meaning and context as words spoken by the sages of Naimisharanya glorifying Suta Goswami for distributing the nectar of the lotus feet of Govinda.

Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya Narayanam Namaskritya Naram Chai Vanarottamam. Deveem Saraswateem Vyasam Tato Jayam Udi Rayet. In this session, we will be discussing from the 12th verse of the 18th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. Karmani Asmin Anashvase Dhooma Dhoomratmanam Bhavan Apayayati Govinda Pada Padmasavam Madhu. The meaning of this verse is, We have just begun the performance of this fruited, seductive activity, a sacrificial fire, without certainty of its result due to the many imperfections in our action. Our bodies have become black from the smoke, but we are factually pleased by the nectar of the lotus feet of the personative Godhead Govinda, which you are distributing. So these are words spoken by the sages of Naimisharanya to the people of Godhead. They are glorifying Suta Goswami. And they are glorifying Suta Goswami. You are distributing nectar of the lotus feet of Govinda, the Supreme Lord. And that is very pleasing to us.

### devotional service, association with devotees, value of time
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/lUpn93Jf4rg?t=0
**Verse References:** SB 1.18.13
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "The value of a moment's association with the devotee of the Lord cannot even be compared to the attainment of heavenly planets, or liberation from matter."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces and emphasizes the importance of Srimad Bhagavatam 1.18.13, highlighting the incomparable value of associating with a pure devotee, even for a brief moment, over material achievements or liberation.

Om Agyana Timiraandhasya Gyananjanishalakaya Chaksharunmilitam Yena Tasmai Shri Gurave Namaha. Nama Om Vishnupadaya Krishna Preshtaya Bhutale Shri Mate Bhaktivedanta Svamin Nithinamine. Namaste Saraswate Deve Gauravani Pracharine Nirvishesha Shunyavadi Pashchatyade Shatarine. Jaya Shri Krishna Chaitanya Prabhu Nithyananda Shri Advaita Gadadhara Shri Vasadi Gauravakta Vrinda. Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare. Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya Narayanam Namaskritya Naram Chai Vanarottamam. Deveem Saraswateem Vyasam Tato Jayam Udi Rayet. In this session we discussed from the 13th verse of the 18th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam: Tulayama Lavenapi Nasvargam Napunarbhavam Bhagavatsangi Sangasya Martyanamkim Utashishaha. This is a very famous verse in the Bhagavatam. It's quoted many times later on in the Bhagavatam itself and even quoted by Srila Prabhupada on many different instances. So the meaning of this verse is the value of a moment's association with the devotee of the Lord cannot even be compared to the attainment of heavenly planets, or liberation from matter. And what to speak of worldly benedictions in the form of material prosperity, which are for those who are meant for death. This very important verse.

---
### devotional service, association with devotees, material vs. spiritual
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/lUpn93Jf4rg?t=104
**Verse References:** SB 1.18.13
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "What is the value of associating with a pure devotee even for a very, very short time?"
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the verse, explaining that even a fraction of a second spent in proper association with a pure devotee is immensely valuable, far exceeding the benefits of fruitive activities aimed at attaining heavenly planets, material riches, or impersonal liberation.

This is helping us understand that we don't understand the future. This is a very important verse. What is the value of associating with a pure devotee even for a very, very short time? Here the Sanskrit word is lava. Lavena means lava is a measurement of a duration of time which is a fraction of a second. It's actually a moment, a very, very, very, very small bit of time. Even that, if somebody is able to properly associate with a sadhu, a pure devotee, for even a short time, that is very, very, very, very, very, very valuable. And how valuable? This verse is being spoken by the sages from the mission, and they are telling you cannot compare the immense benefit you get by a moment's association with a pure devotee. You cannot compare that with the benefit of performing so many fruitive activities to elevate oneself to heavenly planets, or so many more fruitive activities to actually get different kinds of material riches, material benefits, or even some kind of fruitive activity for getting liberation from material bondage, impersonal liberation. None of these actually are worthwhile for a person who's about to die, who is subject to death in this material world. None of these are really beneficial.

---
### material happiness, transcendental pleasure, devotional service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/lUpn93Jf4rg?t=232
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "One cannot compare the association of a pure devotee to anything material."
**Summary:** The speaker quotes Srila Prabhupada's purport, emphasizing that material happiness derived from activities like reaching heavenly planets or achieving impersonal liberation cannot be compared to the transcendental pleasure gained from associating with a pure devotee.

Why? Because Prabhupada explains in the purport. The purport of Prabhupada says when there are some similar points, it is possible to compare one thing to another. You'll see the difference between the two. One cannot compare the association of a pure devotee to anything material. Men who are addicted to material happiness aspire to reach the heavenly planets like the moon, Venus, and Indra Loka, and those who are advanced in material philosophical speculations aspire after liberation from all material bondage. Broadly speaking, those who are non-devotees, their attempts at any other form of divine ultimate in the impersonal living man, everybody is his own way of life, taking labor, all kinds of pious activities which help one to go to the heavenly plans to take birth in heavenly pleasures. And the second group of activities are those by which one can get liberation from all material bondage. Here liberation specifically refers to impersonal liberation, merging into Brahman. So both of these are actually, both these kinds of results are considered material benefits, material result, and this cannot be compared to association with a pure devotee because the result of either of these is material happiness.

---
### detachment, liberation, devotional service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/lUpn93Jf4rg?t=358
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The pure devotees of the Lord do not aspire after the happiness obtained in heavenly kingdom nor do they aspire after the liberation from material bondage."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that pure devotees do not seek material pleasures or impersonal liberation, as they find such pleasures to be illusory and temporary, like a 'phantasma goria'.

And the result of association with a pure devotee is transcendental pleasure. So Srila Prabhupada says when one becomes frustrated with all kinds of material advancement, one desires the opposite type of liberation, which is called a punar bhava or no rebirth. One category of people are trying to enjoy, enjoy, enjoy in this material world, but when they realize actually we cannot enjoy in this material world, really real pleasure is not there, we have happiness not, then they try opposite. What is that? They try to avoid taking birth again. And how do they avoid taking birth again? By merging into the existence of the Supreme Lord, called the merging into Brahma Jyoti. But the pure devotees of the Lord do not aspire after the happiness obtained in heavenly kingdom nor do they aspire after the liberation from material bondage. Pure devotees don't want this. Why? For the pure devotees of the Lord, the material pleasures obtainable in the heavenly parents are like phantasma goria. What is phantasma goria? It is something like some imaginary pleasure, some utopian ideal, like too much of promise. Oh, it's very nice, but actually experience that you find what? There is no real, not so much of happiness that I was thinking I will get. It is the same mundane pleasure available anywhere else in any other material situation near the material facility, the same type of flickering momentary happiness.

---
### liberation, eternal service, devotional service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/lUpn93Jf4rg?t=480
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Pure devotional service...is eternal."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts the fleeting nature of material pleasures and positions with the eternal nature of devotional service, explaining that pure devotees are already liberated and engaged in transcendental loving service to Krishna, making material aspirations irrelevant.

So heavenly pleasures are also the same quality. And the devotees, they are already by devotional service, pure devotional service, they're already liberated from all material conceptions of pleasure and distress. They are liberated even while living in the material world. The pure devotees, they're already liberated, so why will they seek liberation? Why do they want liberation? They're already liberated. That means the pure devotees of the Lord are engaged in a transcendental existence, namely in the loving service of the Supreme Lord, both in the material world and in the spiritual world. This is a secret that many people do not know. A pure devotee's activities appear like ordinary activities of a materialistic person, but actually, a pure devotee's actions are all completely spiritual. And a pure devotee is only engaged in loving devotional service to Krishna, even while he is in the material world. So Prabhupada compares this pure devotee's activity with an example. As a garment servant is always the same, either in the office or at home, or at any place, so a devotee has nothing to do with that. He has nothing to do with anything material, for he is exclusively engaged in the transcendental service of the Lord. Since he has nothing to do with anything material, what pleasure can he derive from material benedictions like kingship or other overlordships, which are finished quickly with the end of the body? Why will a pure devotee aspire for becoming a king, becoming an emperor, becoming a resident of heavenly planets, becoming even the king of heaven? Because all these positions are finished with the body. When death comes, finished, over. The positions are all temporal.

---
### eternal service, devotional service, material vs. spiritual
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/lUpn93Jf4rg?t=630
**Verse References:** BG 2.47
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "All material pleasures have a beginning and end. Spiritual happiness, no beginning, no end."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the eternal nature of devotional service, contrasting it with the temporary nature of material pleasures, and highlights Suta Goswami as an example of a pure devotee whose association is invaluable.

Devotional service is eternal. This is a very, very important point. Pure devotional service, of course devotional service here means pure devotional service. It is eternal. That means there is no end. There is no stoppage. It has no end because it is spiritual. Anything spiritual has no end. Everything material has got an end. This Krishna describes in Bhagavad Gita: Adi antavanta kaunteya na teshu ramate buddhaha. All material pleasures have a beginning and end. Spiritual happiness, no beginning, no end. No beginning especially for those who are always engaged in transcendental loving service with the Lord. They are always happy, and their happiness continues without any break. They are always jolly. They are always happy. Therefore, since the assets of a pure devotee are completely different from material assets, there is no comparison between the two. Suta Goswami was a pure devotee of the Lord. Suta Goswami is sitting and speaking Srimad Bhagavatam, the sage of Naimisharanaya. He was a pure devotee of the Lord. And therefore his association with the rishis in Naimisharanaya, the sage of Naimisharanaya is unique. So many. And the sages of Naimisharanaya are very highly qualified brahmanas. They know so much about different kinds of fruitive activities, different kinds of sacrifices, different kinds of study of the spiritual literature, Vedic literature.

---
### detachment, material entanglement, devotional service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/lUpn93Jf4rg?t=747
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "In the material world, association with gross materialists is veritably condemned."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts the futility of material activities with the value of associating with a pure devotee, condemning association with materialists and highlighting the entanglement caused by seeking sense gratification.

But none of them are useful for eternal life. They are useless. For eternal life, they are useless. Whereas, Suta Goswami, he is not going to narrate or describe any of those type of descriptions, how to do some material fruitive activity, how to attain impersonal liberation, none of those things. He simply is reciting the Bhagavatam. In the material world, association with gross materialists is veritably condemned. So the materialist is called Yoshitsangi, or one who is very much attached to material entanglement. Materialists are like that. They are always after material pleasures, and they become entangled. What is this material entanglement? Women and other paraphernalia, they are always looking for sense gratification. So they want sense objects. Here women means the object of enjoyment in terms of the opposite gender. For a man it is a woman, for a woman it is a man. Sex pleasure. And connected with sex pleasure, there are so many other pleasures. Pleasure of eating, pleasure of sleeping, pleasure of enjoying different kinds of sense objects, all those kind of things. Such attachment is conditioned because it drives away the benedictions of life and prosperity.

### Krishna katha, relish, transcendental pleasure
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/o2MEnDucTBA?t=48
**Verse References:** SB 1.18.14
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna's topics are so relishable that you go on hearing and you never become saturated."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the 14th verse of the 18th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam, explaining that topics about Krishna are eternally relishable and never cause satiation.

In this session we will be discussing from the 14th verse of the 18th chapter, the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. Konama Tripyet Rasavit Kathayam Mahattamayikanta Parayanasya Nantam Gunanam Agunasya Jagmahu Yogeshwaraye Bhavapadma Mukhyaha. The meaning of this verse is, the personality of God, the personality of God and Lord Krishna, Govinda, is the exclusive shelter for all great living beings and his transcendental attributes cannot even be measured by such masters of mystic powers as Lord Shiva and Lord Brahma. Can anyone who is expert in relishing nectar or rasa ever be fully satiated by hearing topics about him? This is spoken by the sages of Naimisha Araniyata Sutakoswami. Shri Krishna's disciples always tell us that Krishna's topics are so relishable that you go on hearing and you never become saturated. That means you never feel enough. I can't take anymore, I can't relish anymore. No! It never happens.
