**Summary:** The speaker explains that the duties of a king are clearly defined in the scriptures (Dharma Shastras) and that a ruler who strictly follows these duties should not be punished. He then mentions the qualities of a Kshatriya as described in the Bhagavad-Gita.

Kshatriya Dharma. In the Dharma Shastras, there is clear delineation of Brahmana Dharma, Kshatriya Dharma, Vaishya Dharma, Shudra Dharma, Brahmachari Dharma, Grahastha Dharma, Vanaprastha Dharma, Sanyas Dharma, Shri Dharma, Raja Dharma. Like this, all different duties of different classes of people, under different circumstances, in different situations. Apat Dharma, when there are emergencies, what are the duties of different people in an emergency? Everything is delineated very well. Everything is delineated very well. Everything is delineated very well. Everything is delineated very well. Everything is delineated very well. Everything is delineated very well. Very clearly. And if he is a ruler who is a strict follower of those Dharma Shastras, such a ruler is never to be punished, cursed by anyone. In the Shastras, the duties of all caste and order of society are prescribed. All the qualities of a Kshatriya mentioned in the Bhagavad-Gita, in the 18th chapter, the 43rd verse, it is described, what are the qualifications by which a Kshatriya functions. And that is mentioned in the Bhagavad-Gita. And Srila Prabhupada says in this purport, that Pariksit had all those qualifications which are mentioned in the Bhagavad-Gita. What are those qualifications mentioned in the Bhagavad-Gita? Shauryam is mentioned in the Bhagavad-Gita. So, how many are mentioned? Shauryam, Tejo, Dhritir, Daksham, Yudhecha, Api, Apalayanam, Danam, Ishwarabhava. Seven qualifications. Seven qualifications are mentioned. And what are those seven qualifications? The translation in English, first one is heroism, power, determination, resourcefulness, courage in battle, generosity, leadership. These are the qualities.

### Invocation, Context, Srimad Bhagavatam

**Summary:** The speaker begins with invocatory prayers and introduces the context of the lecture, focusing on Suta Goswami's narration to the sages of Naimisharanya about the events leading to Pariksit Maharaj meeting Shukadeva Goswami and the subsequent recitation of the Srimad Bhagavatam.

Om Agyana Timiraandhasya Gyananjanishalakaya Chakshurun Meelitam Yena Tasmai Shri Gurave Namaha. Nama Om Vishnu Padaya Krishna Preshtaya Bhutale Shri Mate Bhakti Vedanta Swamin Nithinamine. Namaste Saraswate Deve Gauravani Pracharine Nirvishesha Shunyavadi Pashchatya Deshatarine. Jaya Shri Krishna Chaitanya Prabhu Nithyananda Shri Advaita Gadadhara Shiva Sadhi Gaurabhakta Vrinda. Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare. Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya. Narayanam Namaskritya Naram Chai Vanarottamam Deveem Saraswateem Vyasam Tato Jayam Udi Rayet. In this session we will be discussing from the 47th verse of the 18th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. He is describing the whole incident leading to Pariksit Maharaj renouncing his kingdom and meeting Shukadeva Goswami after which the Bhagavatam was spoken by Shukadeva Goswami to Pariksit Maharaj.

---
### Karma, Responsibility, Social Order
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/zwSlTwtZu9w?t=116
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "When an offense is done unto the devotee of the Lord, it is very difficult to overcome the reaction."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that everyone is responsible for their actions according to the law of karma, and Rishi Shamika foresaw the sin his son committed by cursing Maharaj Pariksit, a pious ruler and devotee. Offenses against devotees are difficult to overcome, and Brahmanas should protect subordinates, not curse them, and superiors should not disproportionately punish subordinates for small mistakes.

So Srila Prabhupada explains elaborately this in the purport. Everyone is responsible for his own action, either pious or sinful. This is the law of karma in this material world. Rishi Shamika could foresee that his son had committed a great sin by cursing Maharaj Pariksit who deserved to be protected by the Brahmanas for he, that is Pariksit Maharaj, was a pious ruler and completely free from all sins because of his being a first-class devotee of the Lord. So Pariksit Maharaj as a pure devotee deserves to be protected by the Brahmanas. Instead he is being cursed. It is totally unjust. So Srila Prabhupada further says, when an offense is done unto the devotee of the Lord, it is very difficult to overcome the reaction. Vaishnava Aparath is very, very, very dangerous. Generally Vaishnava Aparath cannot be overcome by the person who has committed that offense or Aparath. So further Srila Prabhupada says, the Brahmanas being at the head of the social orders are meant to give protection to their subordinates and not to curse them. A superior, when dealing with somebody who is a subordinate, has to be considered, if they do some mistake, they cannot be disproportionately punished. In general they should not be punished when they make a mistake, especially when they are subordinate and they have not done a big mistake. For their small mistakes they should not be punished. There are occasions when a Brahmana may furiously curse a subordinate Kshatriya or Vaishya.

---
### Pride, Divine Law, Krishna's Superintendence
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/zwSlTwtZu9w?t=272
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Out of fear of the Supreme Lord who is the controller, director of the entire material nature, the sun is rising every day."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that in Pariksit's case, there were no grounds for the curse, and the boy acted out of vanity, making him liable to divine punishment. Above natural laws is the Supreme Lord's personal superintendence, and phenomena like the sun rising are due to fear of the Lord, contrary to the explanations of modern scientists.

But in the case of Maharaj Pariksit, there were no grounds as already explained. This was explained elaborately in the previous verse. The foolish boy had done it out of sheer vanity, vanity means pride, in being a Brahmana's son, and thus he became liable to be punished by the law of God. Above the law of nature is the Supreme Lord's personal superintendence. This is very important to note. It appears like the nature's laws are working in an impersonal way because we don't see generally the hand of any person. Just like the seasons are changing, the sun is rising every day, and what is the explanation of the scientists? So called scientists. They have no explanation how it is happening. Scriptures are very clear. Bhishasmat vatapavate bhisho deti suryaha. Out of fear of the Supreme Lord who is the controller, director of the entire material nature, the sun is rising every day. The wind is blowing properly to ensure supply of the air for all the living beings on the earth. This is unknown to modern scientists. This is unknown to most people in this, in today's world.

---
### Krishna's Control, Devotee Offenses, Sin vs. Offense
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/zwSlTwtZu9w?t=394
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna is very, very attached to his pure devotees. He is called Bhakta Vatsala."
**Summary:** Krishna is the supreme director with a vast administration of devatas, all working under His superintendence. The Lord never forgives those who condemn His devotees, as Krishna is very attached to them. Cursing Pariksit was not only a sin but a great offense, distinct from violating natural laws. Offenses against Vaishnavas are on a different level, and only the Supreme Lord can deal with them.

Krishna is the supreme director, controller, master. Under him there is a whole big administration of the devatas, headed by Brahma. Everyone is working under the superintendence of Krishna. This is unknown. So here Prabhupada is pointing out if some of the devatas, subordinate controllers make a mistake, then they are liable to be punished by God Krishna. So Prabhupada further says, the Lord never forgives a person who condemns his pure devotee. Krishna is very, very attached to his pure devotees. He is called Bhakta Vatsala. Therefore by cursing a king, the foolish Sringi had committed not only a sin but also the greatest offense. There is a difference between offense and sin. Sin is directly violating the laws of nature in our material activities. Offense is any, any offense done to a pure devotee, to a Vaishnava. Offending a Vaishnava is on a different level. Committing a sin whereby one violates the material laws of nature is different. Therefore the Rishi could foresee that only the Supreme Personality of Godhead could save his boy from his sinful act. Offense is dealt with only by the Supreme Lord, directly. The other subordinate controllers in material nature have no role to play. They cannot do anything about the offense.

---
### Divine Intervention, Laws of Nature, Krishna's Arrangement
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/zwSlTwtZu9w?t=536
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Even the laws of nature which work perfectly, they can be changed only by Krishna. Nobody else can change it."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that only Krishna can undo what seems impossible, even changing the laws of nature. Rishi Shamika prayed to the Lord for pardon, appealing to the boy's foolishness. A question arises: if it was the Lord's desire for Pariksit to be in that situation for his deliverance, why was the Brahmana's son made responsible for the offensive act?

Further Srila Prabhupada says he therefore directly prayed for pardon from the Supreme Lord who alone can undo a thing which is impossible to change. Even the laws of nature which work perfectly, they can be changed only by Krishna. Nobody else can change it. No Devata, no Yogi, no Tapasvi, no Jnani, no Rishi can change any of the laws of nature. They are working perfectly. But Krishna can change. The appeal was made in the name of a foolish boy who had developed no intelligence at all. Rishi Shamika is appealing to the Supreme Lord that my boy who is a foolish boy, he made a mistake. It was a big mistake but he is a foolish boy, kindly pardoned him. Now Srila Prabhupada further explains very nicely a question may be raised herein that since it was the desire of the Lord that Pariksit Maharaj be put into that awkward position so that he might be delivered from material existence, then why was a Brahmana's son made responsible for this offensive act?

---
### Divine Plan, Responsibility, Forgiveness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/zwSlTwtZu9w?t=632
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna arranged a child to make a mistake of cursing Pariksit so that the child can be excused because children if they make a mistake, they are not to be punished like an adult who makes a mistake."
**Summary:** The speaker clarifies that Pariksit was not at fault for becoming angry, as Krishna arranged the situation for the Bhagavatam to be spoken. The Brahmana's son was chosen to commit the offense so he could be easily excused due to his childhood, and the father's prayer was accepted. Another question arises: why is the entire Brahmana community held responsible for allowing Kali to enter the world through this boy's misuse of Brahmanical power?

See earlier it is explained that Pariksit could not have become angry against this sage Shamika Rishi. Normally according to his proper conduct as a pure devotee. It was arranged by Krishna. So actually it is not Pariksit's mistake at all to have become angry against Shamika Rishi and provoke that Brahmana boy, the son of the Rishi. No. Pariksit is not at fault. Krishna arranged it so that Bhagavatam can be spoken. That was Krishna's higher purpose. But then why is the Brahmana's son made responsible? The Brahmana's son has made the mistake. Why is he in the picture? Is the question that may be raised. So Srila Prabhupada says the answer is that the offensive act was performed by child only so that he could be excused very easily. So Krishna arranged a child to make a mistake of cursing Pariksit so that the child can be excused because children if they make a mistake, they are not to be punished like an adult who makes a mistake. And thus the prayer of the father was accepted. Krishna accepted. Yes. Your boy is a foolish boy. He is an immature boy. He deserves to be pardoned for his mistake. Big mistake. Then further Prabhupada raises another question. If the question is raised why the Brahmana community as a whole was made responsible for allowing Kali into the world affairs. See one Brahmachari boy misusing his Brahmanical power. The whole Brahmana community is held responsible that Kali has entered through this boy and is now going to pollute the rest of the Brahmanas in Kali Yuga, become polluted. So why the whole Brahmana community as a whole is getting polluted by this whole act.

---
### Varaha Purana, Demons in Brahmana Families, Opportunity for Purification
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/zwSlTwtZu9w?t=784
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The all-merciful Lord gave them a chance to have their births in families of pious Brahmanas so that they could progress towards salvation."
**Summary:** The speaker explains, citing the Varaha Purana, that demons who were not killed by Krishna in Dwapara Yuga were allowed to be born in Brahmana families in Kali Yuga. These Brahmanas, acting irreligiously, were given a chance for purification, but they often misuse the opportunity due to vanity. Taking birth in a Brahmana family offers a good opportunity for progress, but it can be lost if not utilized.

So the answer Srila Prabhupada says for this is given in the Varaha Purana. What is the answer in the Varaha Purana? The demons who acted inimically toward personality of Godhead but were not killed by the Lord, by Lord Krishna when Krishna incarnated in Dwapara Yuga, end of Dwapara Yuga. Such demons were allowed to take birth in the families of Brahmanas to take advantage of the age of Kali. So who are these Brahmanas in Kali Yuga who don't follow the Brahmanical principles but act irreligiously under the influence of Kali. These Brahmanas were formerly demons who deserve to be killed and purified by the Supreme Lord but they did not, they were not killed. So such Brahmanas are taking birth in Brahmana families in the age of Kali in which they are polluted. So Srila Prabhupada says further the all-merciful Lord gave them a chance to have their births in families of pious Brahmanas so that they could progress towards salvation. The demons missed the chance of being killed by the Supreme Lord and thereby become purified. So now they are given another chance for purification. Take birth in Brahmana families and thereby make some progress towards ultimate liberation. But the demons instead of utilizing the good opportunity misuse the Brahmanical culture due to being puffed up by vanity in becoming Brahmanas. Now Kali Yuga there is a good opportunity taking birth in a Brahmana family in any age there is good opportunity to make progress. But if they don't utilize then they are the losers. That's what is explained here. I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

---

## SB 1.18.48 | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | 30-04-2023 | Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### invocation, prayer

In this session we will be discussing from the 48th verse of the 18th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. Tiraskrita vipralabdhaha shaptahah shivta hata api nashetat pratikurvanti tattbhaktah prabhavo api hi. The meaning of this verse is, the devotees of the Lord are so forbearing that even though they are defamed, cheated, cursed, disturbed, neglected or even killed they are never inclined to avenge themselves. Titikshava, first come quality he says of a pure devotee, he is very very tolerant, so tolerant that when they are defamed, they are cheated, they are cursed, they are disturbed, neglected or even killed they will never die, never react, they will never take action against those who misbehave is so tolerant.

He knew that Pariksit Maharaj will never take any counter action against the boy because he is a pure devotee. He tolerates any amount of inconvenience for the personal self a pure devotee simply tolerates. As such the devotees are never inclined to counteract personal deformation, curses, negligence, and aversion. As far as such things are concerned, in personal affairs the devotees do not care for them.

I Don't know I'm getting what I deserve I am getting a small token a small token of what I deserve to get So that is Krishna's mercy upon me. ### offenses, devotee action, discrimination
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/z9-Or6fYEdA?t=576
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "If somebody offends the pure devotee himself the pure devotee does not take any action against the offender. He tolerates whatever the offender might have done. But if the offender is offending the Supreme Lord or other pure devotees Then this pure devotee will not Tolerate will not sit quiet. He will take proper action against the offender."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that while pure devotees tolerate personal offenses, they take strong action against those who offend the Lord or other devotees, highlighting the twofold consideration in a devotee's actions.

But in the case of An offense committed by somebody against the Lord or Lord's other devotees Then the pure devotee takes strong action against the offender So the way a pure devotee deals with somebody who's committing an offense It's the consideration is twofold if somebody offends The pure devotee himself the pure devotee does not take any action against the offender He tolerates whatever the offender might have done But if the offender is offending the Supreme Lord or other pure devotees Then this pure devotee will not Tolerate will not sit quiet. He will take proper action against the offender It was a personal affair and therefore Shamika Rishi knew that the king would not take counteraction That Rishi Shamika knew because he knew Pariksit Maharaj is a pure devotee Thus there was no alternative than to place an appeal to the Lord for the immature boy Why did the Shamika Rishi Appeal to the Supreme Lord and not to Pariksit Maharaj Because Pariksit Maharaj he knew will never take any counteraction will never take an offense So don't have to go and ask him pardon is he will never take any counteraction, but what about the Lord Lord is not going to tolerate so therefore Shamika Rishi had to beg forgiveness from the Lord and not from Pariksit Maharaj.

### devotee power, service, example
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/z9-Or6fYEdA?t=696
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "A pure devotee is the most powerful personality in the entire creation. He is more powerful than all the demigods, he is more powerful than the most pure brahmana, he is more powerful than all the jnanis, yogis, tapasvis, all kinds of people."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the immense power of a pure devotee, even exceeding that of brahmanas and demigods, but stresses that this power is always used in service to the Lord and his devotees, using the example of Hanuman burning Lanka.

So here for the Srila Prabhupada says it is not only that The brahmanas are powerful enough to award curses of blessings upon the subordinates the devotee of the Lord Even though he may not be a Brahmana is more powerful than a Brahmana So here the Pure devotee happens to be a Kshatriya But generally Kshatriyas are subordinate to brahmanas So even though Kshatriyas are definitely subordinate to brahmanas But in the case of the Kshatriya being a pure devotee Such a pure devotee is more powerful than the brahmana qualified brahmana A pure devotee is the most powerful personality in the entire creation. He is more powerful than all the demigods, he is more powerful than the most pure brahmana, he is more powerful than all the jnanis, yogis, tapasvis, all kinds of people. He is more powerful than everybody. But a powerful devotee never misuses the power for personal benefit. This is the characteristic of a pure devotee. He knows he has power. As a pure devotee he knows. And that's how a pure devotee, because he is powerful, he can take action against offenders who are offenders against Krishna Or offenders against other pure devotees. That's how a pure devotee can take action. He is capable, he is powerful to take action and he does so. What did Hanuman do? Hanuman burnt Lanka. Why? Because Ravana was blaspheming Lord Rama. At first when Hanuman went to get the whereabouts of Sita Devi. Hanuman was trying to see what is the strength of Ravana. So Hanuman did something by which Hanuman was taken in front of Ravana. So when he was taken in front of Ravana, Ravana first insulted Hanuman. Hanuman tolerated silently. But when Ravana started insulting Rama, Hanuman became angry. And in that anger he burnt half of Lanka. So a devotee is very very powerful pure devotee. But he will use that power only against offenders who are offenders of the Supreme Lord Or offenders against other pure devotees. He will never use his power to counteract offenses committed by somebody against himself. Whatever power the devotee may have is always utilized in service towards the Lord and his devotees. Other devotees. That's all.

## SB 1.18.49-50 | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | 01-05-2023 | Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### offense, forgiveness, divine will
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/UvVVMAJ0Wgc?t=49
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Maharaj Parikshit's garlanding the sage with a dead snake was not at all a very serious offense, but Shringi's cursing the king was a serious offense."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the offenses committed by Maharaj Parikshit and Shringi, and how the latter's curse, though a serious offense, was given by a foolish boy who deserved to be pardoned by the Supreme Lord.

In this session, we will be discussing two verses, the 49th and the 50th verses of the 18th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. These are the last two verses of this chapter. This chapter is entitled, Maharaj Parikshit Cursed by a Brahmana Boy. The 49th verse, Iti Putra Krita Ghena Sonu Tapto Mahamunihi Swayam Viprakrto Ragna Naiva Agham Tad Achintayat. The translation is, the Sage Shamika thus regretted the sin committed by his own son. He did not take the insult paid by the king very seriously. Now, this is summarizing the incident itself entirely, the whole chapter which is describing this incident, leading to Parikshit Maharaj renouncing his kingdom and thereby meeting Shukadeva Goswami and Bhagavatam being spoken and revealed for the benefit of the whole world. So in the Purport, Shila Prabhupada says, the whole incident is now cleared up. How is it cleared up? Maharaj Parikshit's garlanding the sage with a dead snake was not at all a very serious offense, but Shringi's cursing the king was a serious offense. So it's very clear. Now, Parikshit's action, which was the, which led to the whole incident of cursing by the Shamika Rishi's son, it is not at all a serious offense, and the cursing by Shringi was a very, very serious offense. Now, how is it adjusted? Very nicely it's adjusted. The serious offense is committed by a foolish child only. Therefore, he deserved to be pardoned by the Supreme Lord because the curse was given by a foolish boy.

---
### divine plan, instrument, boon in disguise
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/UvVVMAJ0Wgc?t=177
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The curse was in no way an impediment for him to achieve the highest perfection, rather it became a kind of boon in disguise."
**Summary:** This segment explains that Maharaj Parikshit did not mind the curse and took full advantage of the situation, achieving the highest perfection of life through Shukadeva Goswami's grace. The curse became a boon in disguise, and the whole series of events was a desire of the Lord, with Maharaj Parikshit, Rishi Shamika, and Shringi acting as instruments.

Because the curse was given by a foolish boy, and the small boy has made a mistake unknowingly, immaturely, foolishly he has done a mistake, so he deserved to be pardoned by the Supreme Lord. Although it was not, it was not possible to get free from the sinful reaction. The reaction is, the sinful activity is very, very, very serious, so ordinarily a person cannot get away by committing such a very, very serious sinful activity, a grave sinful activity. Nobody can get away, but this case, specific case, the foolish boy deserves to be pardoned. Now, Maharaj Pariksit also did not mind the curse offered to him by a foolish Brahmana. This is Maharaj Pariksit's personal reaction when he heard that he is going to die within seven days, having been cursed by the son of Shamika Rishi. So he did not mind at all. On the contrary, he took full advantage of the awkward situation, this clumsy situation in which Pariksit Maharaj had been placed. He took full advantage. What did he do? By a great will of the Lord, Maharaj Pariksit achieved the highest perfection of life through the grace of Srila Shukadeva Goswami. The events that led to Pariksit Maharaj achieving the highest perfection of life. The curse was in no way an impediment for him to achieve the highest perfection, rather it became a kind of boon in disguise. They say boon in disguise, so it came as something very, very beneficial for Pariksit, that he took advantage by renouncing his kingdom and trying to prepare for meeting with death within one week. And in the course of that, he happened to meet Shukadeva Goswami and Shukadeva Goswami spoke the entire Bhagavatam to Pariksit Maharaj. Pariksit Maharaj got enlightened and thereby he achieved the ultimate liberation and also others who heard also achieved the same destination. And in future, anybody by hearing Bhagavatam can get the highest perfection of life simply by hearing like Pariksit Maharaj did, simply hearing Bhagavatam. Actually it was a desire of the Lord. The whole series of events was a desire of the Lord, and Maharaj Pariksit, Rishi Shamika and Rishi Shamika's son Shringi were all instrumental in fulfilling the desire of the Lord. The Lord wants to do something in the material world. Sometimes he directly does it, sometimes he indirectly does it through somebody, through some instruments.

---
### instrument, divine will, Krishna consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/UvVVMAJ0Wgc?t=389
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "When everything is in relationship with Krishna, nothing is bad, nothing is wrong, nothing is punishable."
**Summary:** This segment uses the analogy of Krishna using the Pandavas as instruments in the Kurukshetra war and Kala using accidents to illustrate how the Lord uses Maharaj Parikshit, Rishi Shamika, and Shringi as instruments for his purpose of speaking the Srimad Bhagavatam. When everything is in relationship with Krishna, nothing is bad or punishable.

Like it is actually Krishna only who killed all those Kauravas on the battlefield of Kurukshetra, but Krishna did it through the Pandavas headed by Arjuna. So Krishna tells Arjuna that I have already killed all these Kauravas, you can only be an instrument. As Kala, Krishna is always acting in this material world. Kala destroys everything in course of time, so destruction by Kala nobody can avoid, but sometimes Kala also uses some instrument to kill somebody. It is like people say natural death and death by some vehicle accident. So when it is death by vehicle accident, people think oh he died because of vehicle accident. No, it is Kala only who is using the vehicle accident as an instrument to actually destroy. Even if somebody dies a natural death, it is Kala only who is actually bringing about the destruction. So similarly, the Lord is using Maharaj Pariksit, Rishi Shamika and Shamika's son Shringi as instruments for his purpose, that the Srimad Bhagavatam be spoken. None of them were put into difficulty because everything was done in relation with the Supreme Person. So when everything is in relationship with Krishna, nothing is bad, nothing is wrong, nothing is punishable.

---
### transcendentalism, detachment, material vs. spiritual
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/UvVVMAJ0Wgc?t=510
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Conditioned souls are always busy trying to avoid material distress and trying to maximize material happiness, but the transcendentalists, the spiritualists, those who are seriously engaged always in spiritual activities, they never care whether by some reason they get material happiness or material distress."
**Summary:** This segment begins the discussion of the 50th verse, explaining that transcendentalists are not distressed or happy in the material world because they are spiritually engaged. It contrasts the behavior of conditioned souls, who seek material pleasure and avoid distress, with that of transcendentalists, who are indifferent to both.

The 50th verse, the last verse of this chapter, prayashaha sadavo loke paraidhvanveshu yojitah na vyathanti na hrishyanti yata atma agunashrayah. The meaning of this verse is generally the transcendentalists, even though engaged by others in the dualities of the material world, are not distressed, nor do they take pleasure in worldly things, for they are transcendentally engaged. Prabhupada explains this very nicely that those who are transcendentalists, they are described as here, sadavaha, transcendentalists, saintly persons. They are never distressed or they are never happy in this material world. Why? Because they are always spiritually engaged. So they don't even consider what is the material situation. As materialists or as people who are under the crutches of maya, we are always concerned whether we are getting material pleasure or material happiness and we try to avoid material distress. We are busy always. Conditioned souls are always busy trying to avoid material distress and trying to maximize material happiness, but the transcendentalists, the spiritualists, those who are seriously engaged always in spiritual activities, they never care whether by some reason they get material happiness or material distress. They don't care for either.

---
### transcendentalism, jnana, yoga, bhakti
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/UvVVMAJ0Wgc?t=649
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The transcendentalists are of three kinds: the empiric philosophers, the mystics, and the devotees of the Lord; the jnanis, yogis, and bhaktas."
**Summary:** This segment explains that transcendentalists are of three kinds: empiric philosophers (jnanis), mystics (yogis), and devotees (bhaktas). Each aims for a different aspect of the absolute truth: merging into the absolute, perceiving the Supersoul, and engaging in loving service to the Personality of Godhead, respectively.

So Srila Prabhupada explains in the purport, the transcendentalists are of three kinds: the empiric philosophers, the mystics, and the devotees of the Lord; the jnanis, yogis, and bhaktas. There is a samskara for that. Empiric philosophers aim at the perfection of merging into the being of the absolute. What is the ultimate goal of the empiric philosophers, the jnanis, those who cultivate spiritual knowledge with the aim of merging into the absolute truth, sayujya mukti, they want sayujya mukti. Mystics aim at perceiving the all pervading super soul. Mystics are the ashtanga yogis. Their goal is to realize the Paramatma who is all pervading in this material world. He is, Paramatma is present in everyone's heart, of every living being's heart, and Paramatma is also present entered into every atom. So they want to perceive that Paramatma. This is their perfection, the ashtanga yogis. And devotees of the Lord are engaged in the transcendental loving service of the personality of God. Devotees, pure devotees, bhaktas, they only want one thing. They want to be always engaged in the transcendental loving service of Krishna. Now Srila Prabhupada further explains since Brahman, Paramatma and Bhagavan are different phases of the same transcendence. This is explained in the Bhagavatam, vadantita tattva vidas tattvam yad jnanam advayam brahmeti paramatmeti bhavaneti sabhyate. All categories of transcendentalists, their aim is the absolute truth only.

---
### Brahman, Paramatma, Bhagavan, detachment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/UvVVMAJ0Wgc?t=773
**Verse References:** SB 1.2.11
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Parikshit Maharaj did not mind the cursing of that boy. Shamika Rishi did not mind Parikshit Maharaj insulting him. Both are transcendentalists."
**Summary:** This segment elaborates on the three features of the absolute truth: Brahman, Paramatma, and Bhagavan, and how different transcendentalists aim for each. It emphasizes that all three are non-dual and beyond the modes of material nature. It concludes by stating that Parikshit Maharaj and Shamika Rishi were unattached to the incident because they were transcendentalists, with the child being an instrument of the Lord's will.

But there are three features of the absolute truth and accordingly the three categories of transcendentalists are aiming for one of the features. The jnanis aim for Brahman, the mystic yogis or the ashtanga yogis aim for Paramatma, and the devotees or bhaktas, bhakti yogis aim at Bhagavan. But Brahman, Paramatma and Bhagavan are described in the Bhagavatam as advayagyana, non-dual substance of the absolute truth. They are all the same. Therefore, Prabhupada says these three categories of transcendentalists are beyond the three modes of material nature. All of them are transcendental to the three modes. Agunashrayaha, Atma, Agunashraya, those are transcendentalists. They are always beyond the modes. Material distress and happiness are products of the three modes. Always interactions of the three gunas produces either distress or happiness, material distress or material happiness. That's the nature of this material existence. Therefore the causes of such material distress and happiness have nothing to do with the transcendentalists. Transcendentalists are engaged in spiritual activities because of which they don't care whether a material situation gives them happiness or distress. They don't care for that because they don't want, they neither want happiness nor want distress. What do they want? They want the supreme. They only want the supreme. The king was a devotee and the rishi was a mystic. That's why all this is explained. Parikshit Maharaj was a pure devotee of the supreme lord. He was a, he was a bhakta. And Shamika Rishi was a mystic yogi. So he was, Shamika Rishi was also a transcendentalist seriously engaged in Ashtanga Yoga practice. That's why he was sitting in trance trying to fix his attention on the Paramatma within the heart. So therefore both of them were unattached to the accidental incident created by the supreme lord. Parikshit Maharaj did not mind the cursing of that boy. Shamika Rishi did not mind Parikshit Maharaj insulting him. Both are transcendentalists. The playful child was an instrument in fulfilling the lord's will. Other than these two, the child was simply an instrument in the lord's plan. So I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

### Kali Yuga, Krishna's departure, irreligion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/WLy1R6cyyMs?t=39
**Verse References:** SB 1.18.6
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "The very day and moment the personality of Godhead Lord Sri Krishna left this earth, the personality of Kali who promotes all kinds of irreligious activities came into this world."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the 6th verse of the 18th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam, explaining that the verse describes how the age of Kali began immediately after Krishna's departure from the Earth, bringing with it irreligious activities.

Naram Chai Vanarottamam Deveem Saraswateem Vyasam Tato Jayam Udi Rayet. In this session we will be discussing from the 6th verse of the 18th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam: Yasmin Ahani Yarkhyeva Bhagavan Utsasarjagam Tadai Vehanu Vrittosav Adharma Prabhavak Kadihi. The meaning of this 6th verse is: The very day and moment the personality of Godhead Lord Sri Krishna left this earth, the personality of Kali who promotes all kinds of irreligious activities came into this world. So this verse follows the previous verse which described that.

---
### Parikshit Maharaj, Kali's influence, counteracting irreligion

**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the context of the verse, explaining how Kali entered the earth planet before Parikshit Maharaj began his rule, how Parikshit Maharaj confronted Kali, and emphasizes the importance of understanding how to counteract Kali's irreligious influence.

Kali entered this earth planet long before Parikshit Maharaj started ruling. That is, Krishna's disappearance at the end of Dwapar Yuga. Krishna's disappearance, then the Pandavas learned about that and then they retired, and they handed over charge of ruling the entire earth planet to Parikshit Maharaj. Then Parikshit Maharaj began ruling, and while he was storing his kingdom, he came across this personality of Kali. Kali was trying to enter his kingdom, but Parikshit Maharaj stopped him from engaging in his nefarious activities. So this particular verse is very important to understand what is the way to counteract the irreligious influence of this age of Kali.

### Krishna consciousness, absolute truth, identity
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/WLy1R6cyyMs?t=178
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna is fully present in the sound vibration of his name. This is very very important to understand."
**Summary:** The speaker begins to explain Srila Prabhupada's purport, emphasizing the identity between Krishna and His name, form, qualities, and activities, which is a central point of Krishna consciousness philosophy.

So Srila Prabhupada explains in the Purport in some detail, the personality of Godhead and his holy name qualities etc are all identical. This is very very important to understand. This is central point of our philosophy of Krishna consciousness. Krishna is absolutely divine and is the creator of heaven and earth. Krishna is absolute. Absolute means there is no difference at all between the person Krishna and his name, his form, his qualities, his activities, his abode, his paraphernalia, his pure devotees. All are identical. What does it mean they are all identical? The name is as good as Krishna himself. Krishna is fully present in the sound vibration of his name. This is very very important to understand.

### Krishna's presence, transcendental sound, absolute reality

**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker continues to elaborate on Krishna's presence in His name, form, and descriptions, emphasizing that Krishna is fully present with all His potencies in the sound vibration of His name and in the deity form.

When I say Krishna, that sound Krishna, two syllables. Krishna, two syllables. Rama. Hare. Two syllables. In those two syllables Krishna is fully present with all his, Krishna means Krishna with all his potencies. The complete whole, he is the absolute truth, so Krishna is fully present in his name, he is fully present in the form, the form of the deity in the temple. Krishna is fully present. Krishna is fully present in his descriptions given in the Bhagavatam. Shukadeva Swami is describing Krishna in those descriptions, in those words. Krishna is fully present. This is different from the material form.

### material vs. spiritual, name and form, water analogy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/WLy1R6cyyMs?t=339
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Saying water is not going to quench my thirst because the name water or the sound of the name water or the word water is different from the substance water."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts the absolute nature of Krishna with the relative nature of material objects, using the example of a person's name and the substance of water to illustrate that material names and forms are different from the actual entity, unlike Krishna.

Material name of a material personality for example take an ordinary person, some Deepak Sharma some gentleman now Deepak Sharma the person and his name Deepak Sharma the two are completely different Deepak Sharma personality is not present in the sound Deepak Sharma no. Or let's take another example the substance water and the name water. I refer to the substance water as water by the name. If I want water I may ask you please give me a glass of water. If I simply say water water water water water water water water, that saying water is not going to quench my thirst because the name water or the sound of the name water or the word water is different from the substance water. I have to actually drink water only then my thirst will be quenched.

Whereas Krishna is not like that you say Krishna you directly contact the supreme person Krishna in full. Krishna is fully present. Now we are not able to perceive the presence of Krishna in his name or in his deity form we think this name is just another sound like so many other sounds, material sounds no it is not or we think the deity in the temple is just a statue made of wood or stone or metal we think it is just a representation. Somebody believes that in the statue of Krishna there is Krishna they are thinking that is Krishna no it's not what we think. You are not able to perceive the presence of Krishna in his name or in his body it's not what we believe it is what actually is the fact Krishna is present in the form of the deity because he's all-powerful he agrees to enter into the deity form when Krishna is invited by the proper spiritual process of deity installation that's a big subject I won't talk about that in the next lecture I won't get into the details of that but in the deity Krishna is a present in his transcendental form in the sound vibration of Hare Krishna Krishna is present in his transcendental form with all his transcendental potencies Krishna is fully present.

### Kali's influence, protection, devotional service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/WLy1R6cyyMs?t=547
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Even now, if we chant Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare, Kali cannot touch us."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that Kali could not enter the earth while Krishna was present and that even now, constant chanting of the holy names, worship of the deity, and engaging in devotional service can protect one from Kali's influence.

So therefore Srila Prabhupada says the personality of Kali was not able to enter the jurisdiction of the earth due to the presence of the personality of Godhead as long as Krishna incarnated and he was present on the earth Kali could not enter and similarly if there is an arrangement for the constant chanting of the holy names qualities etc of the supreme personality of Godhead there is no chance at all for a personality of Kali to enter. Even now, if we chant Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare, Kali cannot touch us. Kali cannot enter our life. Kali cannot enter the jurisdiction of the place where such chanting goes on, where Srimad Bhagavatam is recited, where Krishna is worshipped in his Deity form, where topics of Krishna are discussed by devotees, where Krishna is remembered, where Krishna is glorified, where Krishna is offered food and Prasadam is distributed. Kali cannot influence. Kali cannot touch. Kali cannot enter. Kali cannot spread his influence. That is the technique of driving away the personality of Kali from the world. Now Kali is, Kali Yuga has begun, has started. Kali is present in this world, but Kali cannot influence, even if it is not Kali, even if we use this technique of driving away Kali's influence by constant chanting Hare Krishna, or constantly engaging in different types of devotional service to Krishna, by constantly remembering Krishna, by constantly thinking of Krishna, by constantly hearing about Krishna.

---
### broadcasting Krishna's name, technology, spiritual transformation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/WLy1R6cyyMs?t=691
**Verse References:** BG 9.14
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "If the state arranges to distribute transcendental sound by resounding the holy name, fame and activities of the Lord, as they are authorized in the Bhagavad Gita or Srimad Bhagavatam, then a favorable condition will be created."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses how modern technology, such as radio and television, can be used to broadcast the holy name of the Lord and create a favorable condition for reestablishing religious principles and driving away corruption.

So many ways, so many ways one can remain immune to the influence of Kali. In modernized human society there are great advancements of material science, and they have invented the radio to distribute sound in the air. Very good example Prabhupada is giving. Now it is not just radio, it is also the television, but when Prabhupada was writing this purport, at that time the radio was there available everywhere. Television was not available everywhere in all countries. So Prabhupada is writing radio is spreading that sound everywhere. So earlier they used to have what is called as a radio set. They initially had a big set, then they had a pocket transistor or a pocket device where you just switch on with a battery operated device and you can hear by tuning into some radio station, some music or some commentary or some news etc. Exactly like now you can switch on a TV which is connected through a dish antenna to the satellite which is broadcasting some TV program from some channel. All over the world it broadcasts and you catch that broadcast by dish antenna and you watch that program on a TV. So instead of vibrating some nuisance sound for sense enjoyment, if the state arranges to distribute transcendental sound by resounding the holy name, fame and activities of the Lord, as they are authorized in the Bhagavad Gita or Srimad Bhagavatam, then a favorable condition will be created. The principles of religion in the world will be reestablished and thus the executive heads who are so anxious to drive away corruption from the world will be successful. In the present age there is so much of corruption, there is so much of evil influence due to so much rampant sinful activities all over the world, everywhere. But we can drive away this evil influence by broadcasting the holy name of the Supreme Lord, the transcendental sound through radio, through television, through satellite TV broadcasting, etc. Nothing is bad if properly used for the service of the Lord. There is radio, TV, all these things, they are not bad by themselves if they are used properly, if they are used for broadcasting the holy name of the Supreme Lord, chanting of the holy name, then that will be very very effective in driving away the evil influence of Kali.

### Kali Yuga, Parikshit Maharaj, envy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/vZrz6MWhLgc?t=43
**Verse References:** SB 1.18.7
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Maharaj Parikshit was a realist. He knew perfectly well that in this age of Kali auspicious things produce good effects immediately whereas inauspicious acts must be actually performed to render effects. So he was never envious of the personality of Kali."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the 7th verse of the 18th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam, focusing on Maharaj Parikshit's understanding of Kali Yuga and his lack of envy towards the personality of Kali.

Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya Narayanam Namaskritya Naram Chai Vanarottamam Devim Saraswati Vyasam Tato Jayam Udhirayet. In this session, we will be discussing from the 7th verse of the 18th chapter of the 1st canto of Shri Madbhagavatam. Nanu Dveshti Kalim Samrat Saranga Ivasarabhuk Kushalani Ashu Siddhanti Netarani Krithaniyat. The meaning of this verse is Maharaj Parikshit was a realist like the Bhutakashtra. Maharaj Parikshit was a realist. He knew perfectly well that in this age of Kali auspicious things produce good effects immediately whereas inauspicious acts must be actually performed to render effects. So he was never envious of the personality of Kali.

### Kali Yuga, sinful acts, auspicious acts
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/vZrz6MWhLgc?t=113
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "A person is not a victim of a sinful act until the act is actually performed. In other ages, simply by thinking of performing a sinful act, one used to become a victim of the act."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the special facilities granted in Kali Yuga, explaining that one only suffers the consequences of sinful acts when they are actually performed, unlike previous ages where even thinking of sin could bring negative reactions.

This is a very important verse, which gives some practical guidance for us to understand what kind of evil influence does Kali have in this age on all of us. So, Srila Prabhupada says in the purport, the age of Kali is called the fallen age. This is the age where the influence of the Kala, this particular Yuga, is the most evil influence compared to Sathya, Treta or Dwapara Yugas in the past. It's called the fallen age. In this fallen age, because the living beings are in an awkward position, the Supreme Lord has given them the power to do so. The Supreme Lord has given some special facilities to them. They are handicapped because of being in Kali Yuga. So the Supreme Lord has given some special facility. What is that? By the will of the Lord, a living entity does not become a victim of a sinful act until the act is actually performed. Please hear this carefully. A person is not a victim of a sinful act until the act is actually performed. Please hear this carefully. A person does not become a victim of a sinful activity until he actually performs that sinful activity. What does this mean? The next sentence will help you understand this better. In other ages, simply by thinking of performing a sinful act, one used to become a victim of the act. What does this mean? Supposing, in a previous Yuga, somebody thought of smoking a cigarette. Immediately, they become a victim of that sinful activity. They get sinful reaction simply for thinking of smoking. Now, Kali Yuga is such that if somebody thinks of smoking, they don't get any sinful reactions for thinking in a sinful way. They get the sinful reaction simply for thinking of smoking. The sinful reaction, only if they actually smoke, they actually perform the sinful activity.

---

He then returns to the example of Maharaj Parikshit, who was not envious of Kali but also did not allow him to spread irreligion.

Now, in this same Kali Yuga, on the contrary, Prabhupada says, a living being in this age is awarded with the results of pious acts simply by thinking of them. Opposed to sinful activity supposing I think of doing some worship of the Supreme Lord I think of doing it then simply by thinking I get some spiritual benefit of actually having performed simply by thinking you get the benefit so this is the special facility the Supreme Lord has given us in this age of Kali because we are handicapped due to the influence of the age evil influence of Kali so therefore it is described about Maharaj Pariksit Maharaj Pariksit being the most learned and experienced King by the grace of the Lord was not unnecessarily envious of the personality of Kali see personality of Kali is adharma bandhu he spreads his evil influence he wants to bring in irreligion in a place where there is no irreligion so Kali is not a very welcome personality but still.

**Summary:** The speaker explains that Maharaj Parikshit did not allow Kali to spread his evil influence among innocent people, protecting his citizens from sinful activities while also allotting Kali specific places to reside.

A truly dikkat in the nakata of human nature so Kali is a very polite and repentant and always engaging in good deeds so Maharaj Pariksit was not unnecessarily envious of the personality of Kali why was he not envious because he did not intend to give him any chance to perform any sinful activity Pariksit did not kill Kali Purusha at the same time he did not allow Kali Purusha to remain in this kingdom and do his nefarious act of spreading his evil influence among innocent people. Poor Pariksit did not allow that. So it is further said by Srila Prabhupada, Pariksit Maharaj protected his subjects from falling prey to the sinful acts of the age of Kali and at the same time he gave full facility to the age of Kali by allotting him some particular places. He gave Kali Purusha some particular places but he also protected the sin citizens from not performing the sinful activities. ### Kali, sinful activities, avoidance
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/vZrz6MWhLgc?t=470
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "If Kali is not given a chance to reside in this earth planet where will Kali be able to reside wherever sinful activities like meat-eating intoxication gambling and illicit sex are going on four places and of course there is a fifth place also where gold is kept accumulated for sins gratification five places Kali can come and reside and spread the evil influence of spreading irreligious activities."
**Summary:** Using the analogy of tuberculosis bacteria, the speaker explains that Kali resides where sinful activities occur, specifically mentioning meat-eating, intoxication, gambling, illicit sex, and hoarding wealth for sinful gratification. Avoiding these activities prevents Kali from spreading his influence.

It is just like supposing there is a tuberculosis bacteria if somebody catches that infection of the tuberculosis bacteria they get tuberculosis infection then the tuberculosis bacteria sitting in the body of one person who is infected will spread to others while living and multiplying in the body of a healthy person so if Kali is not given a chance to reside in this earth planet where will Kali be able to reside wherever sinful activities like meat-eating intoxication gambling and illicit sex are going on four places and of course there is a fifth place also where gold is kept accumulated for sins gratification five places Kali can come and reside and spread the evil influence of spreading irreligious activities so to prevent Kali from spreading irreligious influence evil influence one should not give Kali a place to reside that means there should be nobody who indulges in these five things indulges in these five things consuming intoxication killing animals for meat-eating and gambling indulging in illicit sex and keeping wealth for sins gratification so these five things if we avoid then Kali will have no place to reside and spread this evil influence.

### Kali Yuga, chanting, liberation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/vZrz6MWhLgc?t=605
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Even though all nefarious activities of the personality of Kali are present there is a great advantage in the age of Kali one can attain salvation simply by chanting the holy name of the Lord."
**Summary:** The speaker highlights the advantage of Kali Yuga: liberation can be attained simply by chanting the holy name of the Lord, particularly the Hare Krishna mantra. He mentions Maharaj Parikshit's efforts to propagate chanting and Srila Prabhupada's Krishna consciousness movement.

So it is further told by Srila Prabhupada in the purport at the end of the Srimad Bhagavatam it is said that even though all nefarious activities of the personality of Kali are present there is a great advantage in the age of Kali one can attain salvation simply by chanting the holy name of the Lord Bhagavatam particularly says this Bhagavatam is a special scripture for this Kali Yuga and Bhagavatam says simply by chanting Hare Krishna one can attain ultimate liberation from material bondage thus Maharaja Pariksit made an organized effort to propagate the chanting of the Lord's holy name and thus he saved the citizens from the clutches of Kali exactly what Srila Prabhupada has done as done or is still doing through his sincere followers Prabhupada's movement, Krishna conscious movement, Hare Krishna movement is a movement of life, wisdom, self- Respect. This con movement is meant for spreading the chanting of the holy name of the Supreme Lord Krishna, the Hare Krishna mantra, so that Kali cannot exert his influence. Kali cannot spread his evil influence. And everyone can be saved simply by this propaganda of chanting Hare Krishna. It is only for this reason that sages sometimes wish all good for the age of Kali. On the one side it is said Kali Yuga is a dangerous age, because the influence of Kali people become very sinful. But another statement is also there, Kali Yuga is a very very special age where simply by chanting Hare Krishna, one can drive away the evil influence of Kali, one is protected completely by chanting Hare Krishna, and one is also able to achieve ultimate liberation simply by chanting. The example of a Jamila given in the sixth canto of Bhagavatam explains in detail this particular special facility in Kali Yuga.

### Krishna Katha, devotional service, Vaishnava compassion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/vZrz6MWhLgc?t=754
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "A Vaishnava is compassionate upon all the living beings but a Vaishnava never allows a sinful living entity to influence other innocent people in a negative way one sinful person should not influence other innocent people to also become sinful."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of discourse on Lord Krishna's activities and recitation of Srimad Bhagavatam for overcoming the disadvantages of Kali Yuga. He concludes by highlighting the Vaishnava principle of compassion for all beings, while also stressing the need to protect innocent people from the negative influence of sinful individuals.

In the Vedas also it is said that by discourse on Lord Krishna s activities, one can get rid of all the disadvantages of the age of Kali. In the beginning of the Srimad Bhagavatam, it is also said that by the recitation of Srimad Bhagavatam, the Supreme Lord becomes at once arrested within one s heart. The Supreme Lord is present in every ones heart as Paramatma, but if we engage in hearing Krishna Katha, the Supreme Lord becomes active in our heart and then he protects us from the evil influence of Kali these are some of the great advantages of the age of Kali and Maharaj Parikshit took all the advantages and did not think any ill of the age of Kali true to his Vaishnavite cult a Vaishnava is compassionate upon all the living beings but a Vaishnava never allows a sinful living entity to influence other innocent people in a negative way one sinful person should not influence other innocent people to also become sinful.

### protection, intelligence, kali
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/PiXOU9Yikq4?t=39
**Verse References:** SB 1.18.8
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The king was powerful like a tiger and took care of the foolish careless persons."
**Summary:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami introduces the verses to be discussed and begins with verse 8, explaining that Maharaj Parikshit protected the people of his kingdom from the influence of Kali.

In this session we will be discussing from three verses, verses 8, 9 and 10 of the 18th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. So first verse number 8: Kimnu Baleshu Surena Kalina Dheera Bhiruna Apramatta Pramateshu Yovruko Nrushu Vartate. The meaning of this eighth verse: Maharaj Parikshit considered that less intelligent men might find the personality of Kali to be very powerful, but those who are self-controlled would have nothing to fear. The king was powerful like a tiger and took care of the foolish careless persons. So very important description of the protection given by Parikshit Maharaj to the people of his kingdom.

### devotion, intelligence, spiritual intelligence
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/PiXOU9Yikq4?t=115
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Only an intelligent person takes to devotional service."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the purport, explaining that devotees of the Lord are intelligent and that this intelligence is spiritual, not merely material, and is used to understand God and engage in spiritual activities.

The Purport to the Prabhupada says, Those who are not devotees of the Lord are careless and unintelligent. Why is this said? Because unless one is thoroughly intelligent, one cannot be a devotee of the Lord. Ye Krishna bhaje sei bada chatura, the statement of Chaitanya Charitamrita. One who is a devotee of Krishna is actually very intelligent. So only an intelligent person takes to devotional service. This intelligence is not ordinary material intelligence. It is actually spiritual intelligence. Spiritual intelligence is little different. What is spiritual intelligence? How is it different from material intelligence? Material intelligence is to be able to use one's intelligence for eating, sleeping, mating, defending, defending for sense satisfaction for some bodily meeting bodily demands. That intelligence even the animals have, the birds have, all creatures have that intelligence. That intelligence is not being talked of here, the intelligence to actually try to understand God, try to understand what is spirit, engage in spiritual activities for eternal benefit, ultimate welfare. That is spiritual intelligence and that is different, that is not necessarily connected with the material intelligence.

### kali, devotional service, protection
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/PiXOU9Yikq4?t=219
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "It will not be possible to bring about a saner condition in society unless we are prepared to accept the modes of action adopted by Maharaj Pariksit that is propagation of the devotional service of the Lord to the common man."
**Summary:** The speaker continues to explain how Kali influences non-devotees, enticing them into sinful activities, and emphasizes the importance of propagating devotional service to protect people from Kali's influence.

So those who are not devotees of the Lord fall prey to the actions of Kali. Kali can influence somebody who is not a devotee. What will be Kali's influence? Like they will be enticed into engaging in sinful activities like meat-eating, intoxication, gambling, illicit sex, or earn a lot of money for sense gratification. These are all the evil influence of Kali on unintelligent people. And who are these unintelligent people? They are basically not devotees of Krishna. It will not be possible to bring about a saner condition in society unless we are prepared to accept the modes of action adopted by Maharaj Pariksit that is propagation of the devotional service of the Lord to the common man. So in this society if we want to avoid Kali's influence, if we want people to be protected, then we have to propagate this devotional service among the common people. That is absolutely essential if at all we want to drive away Kali or keep people protected against evil influence of Kali.

### hearing, bhagavatam, eternal life
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/PiXOU9Yikq4?t=680
**Verse References:** SB 1.2.6
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers

**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of systematically hearing about Krishna's transcendental activities, qualities, and names from the Bhagavatam, explaining that it leads to eternal life.

Okay? So, we should hear topics connected with the transcendental activities of the personality of Godhead. Because he's always with his devotees. In a purport, Srila Prabhupada says, the systematic hearing of the transcendental activities, qualities and names of Lord Sri Krishna pushes one towards eternal life. This is described elsewhere in the Bhagavatam. Here in the first chapter, in the first canto, in the second canto, this is described. You should make a program to systematically hear the Bhagavatam. Bhagavatam is a very big scripture. 18,000 verses. But hear little by little by little every day. And what will happen if you hear every day? It is said here that you go on hearing, hearing, hearing Bhagavath Shravan. Hearing Srimad Bhagavatam every day. Prabhupada instituted the system of having a Bhagavatam discourse every day in the temple. And if you cannot come to the temple, you can watch the Bhagavatam discourse on the channel, on the broadcast channel. You can hear the Bhagavatam discourse. So, systematic hearing. Every day you hear. Little, little, little, little. And if you cannot hear at the time the class is conducted, because morning you may be busy, class happens in the morning, then you can hear later in the day or in the evening, or in the evening, or in the evening, or in the evening, or in the evening, or in the evening, or in the evening. And you can hear the Bhagavatam discourse. You are hearing the same discourse. It is stored. So, by simply hearing, hearing, hearing, hearing, hearing, you are progressing towards eternal life.

Systematic hearing means knowing Him gradually in truth and in fact. See. You know factually about Krishna, not as an imaginary person, not some sentiment, but in actuality become realized about Krishna's personality. It is very, very special. And knowing Krishna in truth and fact means attaining eternal life, as stated in Bhagavad Gita. Bhagavad Gita says that anyone who knows Krishna in truth and fact, Krishna says, janma karma chame divyam evam yo veti tatvatah tyatva deham punar janma neti mam eti sorjuna. Simply by knowing in truth and fact, in truth and fact about Krishna, means attaining eternal life. Tyatva deham punar janma neti mam eti. One goes back to the spiritual abode of the Supreme Lord, and attains eternal life in association with Him. Punar janma neti. So, that's why Prabhupada is saying here, make a habit of hearing Bhagavatam every day, and your life will be successful. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.
