## SB 1.19.1-3 | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | 02-05-2023 | Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### repentance, devotee, sin
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/fpqJj6EI3oI?t=32
**Verse References:** SB 1.19.1
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Accidental sins committed by a devotee are sincerely regretted, and by the grace of the Lord, all sins unwittingly committed by a devotee are burnt in the fire of repentance."
**Summary:** This segment introduces the chapter and discusses Maharaj Parikshit's repentance for his accidental offense, emphasizing that a devotee's sincere regret burns away sinful reactions.

Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare, Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya, Narayanam Namaskritya, Naram Chai Vanarottamam, Devim Sarasvatim Vyasam Tato Jayam Udhirayet. In this session we will be discussing from three verses, verse numbers 1, 2 and 3 of the 19th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. We are beginning a new chapter. This 19th chapter of the first canto is entitled The Appearance of Shukadeva Goswami. This is text number 1. The meaning of this verse is: Shri Suta Goswami said, 'While returning home, the king Maharaj Pariksha felt that the act he had committed against the faultlessness of the king was not right. The powerful brahmana was heinous and uncivilized. Consequently, he was distressed. The pious king regretted his accidental improper treatment of the powerful brahmana who was faultless. Such repentance is natural for a good man like the king, and such repentance delivers a devoted heart. The devotees are naturally faultless. Accidental sins committed by a devotee are sincerely regretted, and by the grace of the Lord, all sins unwittingly committed by a devotee are burnt in the fire of repentance.' So essentially, Srila Prabhupada is telling that Pariksit, being a pure devotee, is naturally faultless, but accidentally he committed a mistake, and he was very, very repentant about it. So in the fire of repentance, such sinful reactions are burnt away.

---
### repentance, consequences, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/fpqJj6EI3oI?t=199
**Verse References:** SB 1.19.2
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "King Pariksit thought, 'Due to my neglecting the injunctions of the Supreme Lord, I must certainly expect some difficulty to overcome me in the near future. I now desire without reservation that the calamity come now, for in this way I may be freed of this sinful action and not commit such an offense again.'"
**Summary:** This segment focuses on the second verse, where King Parikshit anticipates difficulties due to neglecting the Lord's injunctions and desires the calamity to come quickly to be freed from sin and learn a lesson.

The second verse, dhruvam tatome krita devahelanat duratyayam vyasanam nathidirghat tadastukamam yaga niskrtayame yathana kuryam unarevam addhav. The meaning of the second verse is, King Pariksit thought, 'Due to my neglecting the injunctions of the Supreme Lord, I must certainly expect some difficulty to overcome me in the near future. I now desire without reservation that the calamity come now, for in this way I may be freed of this sinful action and not commit such an offense again.' In this verse, Pariksit is thinking to himself, while he is returning home, that Pariksit has certainly neglected to follow the injunctions of the Supreme Lord. Now what are the injunctions of the Supreme Lord? That is explained in the purport, which we will just read. And because of that, he definitely is expecting to face some problem, some difficulty. Now he also is desiring that let that calamity happen now. Why? Because he will be then freed from that sinful action, and it will also be a lesson for him that he will never again commit such an offense.

---
### brahmanas, cows, laws of nature
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/fpqJj6EI3oI?t=320
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The Supreme Lord enjoins that Brahmanas and cows must be given all protection."
**Summary:** This segment explains the purport of the second verse, emphasizing the importance of protecting Brahmanas and cows, and how Parikshit knew he would be punished for insulting a brahmana, as the laws of nature are the laws of God.

So this is sincere repentance by Pariksit, which is mentioned in the previous verse. In the purport, Srila Prabhupada says, 'The Supreme Lord enjoins that Brahmanas and cows must be given all protection. The Lord is himself very much inclined to do good to Brahmanas and cows. go brāhmaṇa hitāya ca.' There is one prayer to Krishna. namo brahmaṇya devāya go brāhmaṇa hitāya ca jagadhitāya kṛṣṇaya govindāya namo namah. So in this prayer to Krishna, it is clearly declared that Krishna is very much inclined, go brāhmaṇa hitāya ca, for the welfare of go brāhmaṇa, cows and the brāhmaṇas. Maharaj Pariksit knew all this and thus he concluded that his insulting a powerful brāhmaṇa was certainly to be punished by the laws of the Lord. Laws of nature are the laws of God, and they operate in this material world very impartially, very strictly. Nobody can escape them. Somebody may escape the laws of the government, but nobody can escape the laws of God or laws of nature. And it is said further, he was expecting something very difficult in the very near future. He therefore desired the imminent calamity to fall on him and not on his family members. Why is this? Because it is said here, a man's personal misconduct affects all his family members. Therefore Maharaj Pariksit desired the calamity to fall on him alone, not on his family members.

---
### responsibility, devotee, family
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/fpqJj6EI3oI?t=470
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The family members of a devotee also share the effects of a devotee's service to the Lord."
**Summary:** This segment continues the purport, highlighting the responsibility of a devotee, the impact of their actions on their family, and how a devotee's service benefits their family members, using Prahlad Maharaj as an example.

By suffering personally, he would be restrained from future sins, and at the same time, the sin which he had committed would be counteracted so that his descendants would not suffer. That is the way a responsible devotee thinks, very, very disciplined in thinking, speaking and acting. Through mind, through words, and through the body, we act three ways. So all of them are very regulated, restrained in the case of a devotee, a responsible devotee. The family members of a devotee also share the effects of a devotee's service to the Lord. Example is given by Srila Prabhupada, Maharaj Prahlad saved his demon father by his personal devotional service. A devotee's son in the family is the greatest boon or blessing of the Lord.

---
### repentance, civilization, inauspiciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/fpqJj6EI3oI?t=527
**Verse References:** SB 1.19.3
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "I am uncivilized and sinful due to my neglect of Brahmanical culture, God consciousness, and cow protection. Therefore I wish that my kingdom, strength and riches burn up immediately by the fire of the Brahman's wrath so that in the future I may not be guided by such inauspicious attitudes."
**Summary:** This segment introduces the third verse, where Parikshit expresses his desire for his kingdom and riches to be destroyed due to his neglect of Brahmanical culture, God consciousness, and cow protection, hoping to avoid future inauspicious attitudes.

The third verse, adyaiva rājyaṁ balamṛddha-kōśam prakopita brahma kulānalome dahatu abhadrasya unarna me abhūt pāpīya sīdhir dvijadeva gobhyahā. The meaning of this third verse is, 'I am uncivilized and sinful due to my neglect of Brahmanical culture, God consciousness, and cow protection. Therefore I wish that my kingdom, strength and riches burn up immediately by the fire of the Brahman's wrath so that in the future I may not be guided by such inauspicious attitudes.' So this is another description in the Bhagavatam, in this chapter, that Pariksit was very, very, very repentant about the mistake he had done, and he wants the reaction to come immediately now.

---
### civilization, culture, economic development
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/fpqJj6EI3oI?t=613
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Progressive human civilization is based on Brahmanical culture, God consciousness, and protection of cows."
**Summary:** This segment explains Prabhupada's purport on the third verse, emphasizing that progressive human civilization is based on Brahmanical culture, God consciousness, and cow protection, and that economic development is useless without these principles.

In the Parportskīla, Prabhupāda explains, progressive human civilization is based on Brahmanical culture, God consciousness, and protection of cows. dvijadeva gobhyahā. This is the Sanskrit that is used in this verse. vijā refers to the Brahmanical culture. deva refers to God consciousness. gobhyahā refers to protection of cows. All economic development of the state by trade, commerce, agriculture and industries must be fully utilized in relation to the above principles, the principles of Brahmanical culture, God consciousness, and cow protection. Otherwise, all so-called economic development becomes a source of degradation. If economic development does not actually result in taking care of Brahmanical culture, God consciousness, and cow protection, then such economic development is useless. Human society will not make real progress without such principles being adhered to. Cow protection means feeding the Brahmanical culture, which leads towards God consciousness, and thus, the perfection of human civilization is achieved.

---
### Kali Yuga, irreligion, wealth
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/fpqJj6EI3oI?t=725
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Where wealth and strength are not engaged in the advancement of Brahmanical culture, God consciousness, and God protection, the state and home are surely doomed by providence."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the influence of Kali Yuga, which aims at killing the higher principles of life, and how even a strong king like Parikshit was affected. It concludes with the lesson that wealth and strength must be engaged in advancing Brahmanical culture, God consciousness, and cow protection to avoid being doomed.

The age of Kali aims at killing the higher principles of life. Kali Yuga is an age in which the influence of Kali promotes irreligion. Kali is called adharma bandhu. Kali influence spreads irreligion. The influence of Kali is the same throughout the state. Therefore, the age of Kali aims at killing the higher principles of life. Although Maharaj Pariksit strongly resisted the domination of the personality of Kali within the world, the influence of the age of Kali came at an opportune moment, and even a strong king like Maharaj Pariksit was induced to disregard the Brahmanical culture due to a slight provocation of hunger and thirst. How did Maharaj Pariksit commit this mistake? He was extremely thirsty and hungry. Now, such hunger and thirst is not difficult to tolerate for a great soul like Maharaj Pariksit, but somehow, it so happened that he did commit this mistake while he was thirsty and tired and looking for drinking water. Maharaj Pariksit lamented the accidental incident and he desired that all his kingdom strength and accumulation of wealth would be burned up for not being engaged in Brahmanical culture, etc. He wanted punishment, and that punishment also, should be such that he learns a lesson, he does not commit a similar mistake in the future, and also, the sin is counteracted so that the future generations of his family are not affected. So, considering all this, he wanted that immediately, he get the reaction. So, Srila Prabhupada says, the lesson for us is, where wealth and strength are not engaged in the advancement of Brahmanical culture, God consciousness, and God protection, the state and home are surely doomed by providence. Providence here means the laws of nature, the laws of God.

---

**Summary:** This segment concludes the lecture by stating that to achieve peace and prosperity, every state and home must advance Brahmanical culture, God consciousness, and cow protection for a perfect civilization.

If we want peace and prosperity in the world, we should take lessons from these words. Every state and every home must endeavor to advance the cause of Brahmanical culture for self-purification, God consciousness for self-realization, and God protection for getting sufficient milk and the best food to continue a perfect civilization. ### humility, saintliness, detachment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/jIWwjKyejpo?t=58
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The appearance of all those great saintly persons itself is indication that Pariksit Maharaj is the greatest saintly person who has ever been worshipped as a saintly person."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the context of the verse, explaining Pariksit Maharaj's humility and recognition of the greatness of the assembled sages, emphasizing the independence of saintly persons from worldly power.

The meaning of this verse is: The fortunate king said, 'Indeed we are the most great, full of all the kings, who are trained to get favors from the great souls. Generally you the sages consider royalty as refuse to be rejected and left in a distant place.' So these are the first words spoken by Pariksit Maharaj after receiving the great saintly persons, all categories of great sages, by bowing down and standing up with folded hands and telling them of his decision to fast until death. Then he is telling that the appearance of all those great saintly persons itself is indication that Pariksit Maharaj is the greatest saintly person who has ever been worshipped as a saintly person. He is very very fortunate to be able to get such wonderful association at the last stage of his quitting the body in the association of great saintly persons. Because such great souls, they have no respect for great kings who are powerful and have great personalities, who have great courage in fighting in battle and who wield power and rule over citizens. They are independent. Saintly persons are independent of all such Kshatriyas, Vaishyas, and other greats of people. So why does Pariksit say that sages consider royalty as refuse to be rejected and left in a distant place?

---
### renunciation, association, detachment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/jIWwjKyejpo?t=216
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Lord Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu said that to be in intimate touch with dollars and cents men or the kingly order is worse than suicide for one who desires to go back to Godhead."
**Summary:** The speaker quotes Srila Prabhupada's explanation, drawing an analogy between discarding refuse and the need for spiritual aspirants to distance themselves from worldly attachments and materialistic people, citing Chaitanya Mahaprabhu's warning against associating with those overly attached to wealth and power.

So Srila Prabhupada explains in the purport. Prabhupada says, 'According to religious principles, stool, urine, wash water, etc. must be left at a long distance.' Formerly, the toilets, etc. were built or placed very far away from the living quarters because all such refuse garbage has to be discarded at a long distance, far away from where we live in a clean atmosphere. Attached bathrooms is cited herein in relation to the kingly order for those who are progressively marching back to Godhead. So, if you are serious about going back to Godhead, you should not associate too much with worldly-minded people. And Lord Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu said that to be in intimate touch with dollars and cents men or the kingly order is worse than suicide for one who desires to go back to Godhead.

Nishkinchanasyabhagavadbhajanonmukhasyaparamparamjigamishorbhavasagarasya nishkinchanasyabhagavadbhajanomukhasyaparamparamjigamishorbhavasagarasya nishkinchanasyabhagavadbhajanomukhasyaparamparamjigamishorbhavasagarasya. If only he is an artist, he will do all these acts of flattery, będzie Reid revere. But if he doesn't do any of this, he will fundamentally say, that this is what he is doing. Anathapusriyuma spirituality ricord-shri ở 37aji põ . Course 14th March, 2017. one shore of the ocean to the other shore. Ocean means a huge body of water. You cannot expect that you will cross over the ocean by swimming, by your own endeavour, independent endeavour. No. So material existence is not easy to cross over. It is compared to an ocean. So many obstacles and difficulties are there in crossing over this material existence. So such a person, a serious person, a serious transcendentalist, for him, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu says, sandarshanam vishainam atha yoshitam ca, merely glancing, sandarshanam vishainam sense enjoyers, or objects of sense gratification, vishainam atha yoshitam ca, ha hantahant vishabhakshanato pya sadhu, is worse than willingly drinking poison. So it is very, very important. So, it is very, very important. So, it is very, very dangerous for a saintly person, who has renounced all material facilities and comforts, to even look at sense gratifiers or objects of sense enjoyment. So, this is Chaitanya Mahaprabhu's instruction. So, this is Chaitanya Mahaprabhu's instruction. not generally associate with men who are too enamored by the external beauty of God's creation.

---
### illusion, spiritual reality, detachment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/jIWwjKyejpo?t=523
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "This material world is a perverted reflection of the spiritual world."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that material beauty is an illusion meant to entangle beings in the cycle of birth and death, while transcendentalists renounce sense objects and avoid sense enjoyers, understanding the material world as a mere reflection of the spiritual reality.

Whatever material beauty that is actually the creation of the illusory energy meant for those who want to enjoy materially and thereby become entangled in material existence and continue the repetition of birth and death, whereas transcendentalists give up all illusory sense objects, objects of sense gratification and also don't associate with sense enjoyers. By advanced knowledge in spiritual realization, the transcendentalist knows that there is nothing but a shadowy reflection of the reality, the kingdom of God. This is very clearly explained in the 15th chapter. This material world is a perverted reflection of the spiritual world. Reflection means the object which is reflected and seen as a reflection. That object is the real reality. Reflection is not the reality, it is a shadow of the reality. So, if you are interested in reality. If you have a material world, which is the kingdom of God, then this material world, however nice it may appear or however nice it may be promising, it actually is nothing other than a reflection or a shadow. It means there is no substance in trying to enjoy materially. So, a transcendentalist who has got advanced knowledge in spiritual realization knows this fact, has realized this fact and therefore such transcendentalists are not very much captivated by royal opulence or anything like that.

---
### realization, knowledge, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/jIWwjKyejpo?t=697
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "First of all one has to hear from the proper authority, spiritual authority. And then one has to internalize that, what has been heard and digest that information, that knowledge."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes that transcendentalists are not attracted to material comforts and royal opulence, because they have realized the true nature of reality. This realization comes through hearing from a spiritual authority, internalizing the knowledge, and practicing it in life. The speaker then clarifies that Pariksit Maharaj was not an ordinary person, despite being cursed.

They are not attracted by the palace in which a king may stay, the comfortable facilities a very rich man may have for his bodily comforts, for his enjoyment, for his happiness, material happiness. A transcendentalist is never attracted by any of those things. This is not possible, only if there is realization of the knowledge. First of all one has to hear from the proper authority, spiritual authority. And then one has to internalize that, what has been heard and digest that information, that knowledge. realized knowledge, jnana has to become vijnana, then vijnana means one is able to practice, one is able to follow, one is able to act on the platform of that knowledge. Now here Srila Prabhupada says about Pariksit Maharaj, but in the case of Pariksit Maharaj the situation was different. Apparently the king was condemned to death by an inexperienced brahmana boy. So it appears like when the king was cursed, oh he had insulted the sage, therefore the sage Shamika Rishi, his Shamika Rishi's son Sringi, a powerful brahmana boy condemned him. King Pariksit to die within seven days by snake bite, so somebody may think that the king is a very ordinary person in spiritual terms, he is very ordinary person, he is foolishly acted by insulting a great saintly person, a great sage, but no. Prabhupada clarifies, don't mistake Pariksit to be an ordinary worldly person. But factually Pariksit was called by the Lord to return to him, to return to God.

---
### devotion, association, transcendentalism
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/jIWwjKyejpo?t=862
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Devotional service is the topmost spiritual practice and great devotees are recognized by advanced transcendentalists as the topmost transcendentalists in the material world."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that Pariksit's curse was Krishna's arrangement for him to return to Godhead. The great sages assembled because they recognized Pariksit as a pure devotee going back to Godhead and wanted to associate with him. They also respected him as a descendant of the Pandavas, who were known for their devotional service.

So it is Krishna's arrangement for Pariksit to return back to godhead immediately. Yes. Yes. So, Prabhupada says further, other transcendentalists, the great sages and mystics who assembled together because of Maharaj Parikshit's fasting unto death, were quite anxious to see him for he was going back to Godhead. Why did these great sages come there? They knew that Parikshit is not an ordinary king, he is a pure devotee and he is ready to go back to Godhead. Let us associate with him. Maharaj Parikshit could also understand that the great sages who assembled there were all kind to his forefathers, the Pandavas, because of their devotional service to the Lord. See, devotional service is the topmost spiritual practice and great devotees are recognized by advanced transcendentalists as the topmost transcendentalists in the material world. So, Pandavas were respected like that and Parikshit Maharaj was a descendant of the Pandavas. Therefore, he was very happy to actually receive the great sages and have their association at the last moment of his life. I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

## SB 1.19.14-15 | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | 20-05-2023 | Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### humility, surrender, mercy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/XYR_B2uQVU8?t=54
**Verse References:** SB 1.19.14, SB 10.14
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "A devotee is fully surrendered to Krishna, and his life is in Krishna's hands."
**Summary:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami explains Pariksit Maharaj's humble acceptance of the brahmana's curse as the Lord's mercy, illustrating the attitude of a surrendered devotee who sees both good and bad as Krishna's grace, referencing Lord Brahma's prayers in the Srimad Bhagavatam.

In this session, we will be discussing the fourteenth and fifteenth verses of the nineteenth chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. The meaning of the fourteenth verse is: The Supreme Personality of Godhead, the controller of both the transcendental and mundane worlds, has graciously overtaken me in the form of a brahmana's curse. Due to my being too much attached to family life, the Lord, in order to save me, has appeared before me in such a way that only out of fear I will detach myself from the world. So what is Pariksit telling? That he considers himself to be an attached householder. He is taking a humble position as a pure devotee. Pure devotees are always very, very humble. Even though they are the most advanced transcendentalists, they don't consider themselves as very advanced. So they are careful not to become proud, not to be arrogant, not to offend others. Pariksit is taking, especially in front of the saintly persons who have all assembled there, he is taking a very humble position, that I am too much attached to family life. I was living in a comfortable palace. I was surrounded by so much wealth, power, comforts. So in order to make me detached from this world, the Supreme Lord has appeared before me in the form of a brahmana's curse to die within seven days. So this is the nature of a devotee. A devotee is fully surrendered, a pure devotee especially, is fully surrendered to Krishna, and his life is in Krishna's hands. So he considers anything that happens, good or bad in his life, materially good or bad, to be the mercy of the Supreme Lord. This is explained in the Bhagavatam, 10th canto, 14th chapter, in the prayers of Lord Brahma to Krishna: Tattenu Kampam Susamiksha Mano Bhunjana Evatmakrita Vipakam. My dear Lord, it is only your mercy that I am facing some material difficulties or some material miseries because actually I deserve to suffer much, much, much, much more due to my own past misdeeds. But you are so kind to your devotee that you have reduced that suffering that I have to undergo to the minimum as a token to encourage me in my devotional service. And that is only because of your causeless mercy upon me. So this is the attitude of a devotee when the devotee faces some difficulty or some calamity or some danger or some misery. He never blames the Lord, but rather he considers that difficulty to be the Lord's mercy upon him. And as a result of that, the devotee becomes detached from everything material, becomes completely detached, 100% detached.

---
### detachment, divine intervention, surrender
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/XYR_B2uQVU8?t=342
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Such direct action is taken by the Lord in the case of a special devotee."
**Summary:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami elaborates on Srila Prabhupada's purport, explaining that despite being born in a family of great devotees and trained in transcendental attachment, Pariksit Maharaj still required the Lord's direct intervention to detach him from family life, drawing a parallel to Srila Prabhupada's own experience of facing business losses that led him to fully dedicate himself to spiritual life.

In the Purport, Srila Prabhupada explains, Maharaj Pariksit, although born in a family of great devotees, the Pandavas, and although securely trained in transcendental attachment for the association of the Lord, still found the allurement of mundane family life so strong that he had to be detached by a plan of the Lord. Now, Pariksit is no ordinary king. He has appeared in the family of great devotees, pure devotees, the Pandavas. And the Pandavas have trained him in spiritual practice because of which he has developed transcendental attachment for Krishna. In spite of that, he considers family attachment as very, very strong. It is so strong that he had to be detached by a plan of the Lord. Now, Prabhupada explains, such direct action is taken by the Lord in the case of a special devotee. Lord does not intervene in the lives of everyone. Only in the lives of very, very, very great devotees the Lord intervenes. Srila Prabhupada explains, in his own life, he was doing business to maintain his family, but he was an ideal grihasta. He was very strictly practicing Krishna consciousness and he was following the instructions of his spiritual master, Srila Bhaktisiddhanta Saraswati Thakur. But, when Prabhupada faced a lot of difficulties in running his business, he underwent heavy losses, so much so that he had to wind up his business, close down his business and leave home and go away to Vrindavan. So, he considered that it was Krishna's special blessing upon him that he faced those great losses, that he was forced by Krishna to give up his family, give up the business, engagement, and fully dedicate himself exclusively for spiritual life. Similarly, Pariksit considers that this Brahmana's curse is an arrangement of the Supreme Lord. It's a direct action taken by the Lord because of Lord's mercy on Pariksit.

---
### association, divine presence, surrender
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/XYR_B2uQVU8?t=525
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The Lord resides with his devotees and therefore, the presence of the great saintly persons indicated the presence of the Lord."
**Summary:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami explains that Pariksit Maharaj understood the Lord's mercy through the presence of assembled sages, who, as topmost transcendentalists carrying the Lord in their hearts, signified the Lord's presence. He then begins to explain the meaning of the 15th verse, highlighting Pariksit's complete surrender to the brahmanas and Mother Ganges, desiring only to hear the deeds of Lord Vishnu.

Maharaj Pariksit could understand this by the presence of the topmost transcendentalists in the universe. However, assembled sages are no ordinary persons. The topmost transcendentalists in the universe, they have come to the earth planet. So, the Lord resides with his devotees and therefore, the presence of the great saintly persons indicated the presence of the Lord. Why? Why is it like that? Because these great saintly persons carry the Lord within their heart. They carry the Lord within their heart. So, their presence is the same as the presence of the Supreme Lord himself. The king, therefore, welcomed the presence of the great rishis as a mark of favor of the Supreme Lord. So, Krishna is favoring Pariksit by arranging the topmost transcendentalists to that holy place so that Pariksit could get their association during the last stage of his life in this material world in order to perfectly prepare himself to go back to Godhead. The 15th verse: tamopayatam pratiyantu vipra gangachadevi dhrtachittamise dhijopasrishtah kuhakas takshakova dashatu alam gayata vishnugathah. The meaning of this 15th verse is: O brahmanas, just accept me as a completely surrendered soul and let Mother Ganges, the representative of the Lord, also accept me in that way. For, I have already taken the lotus feet of the Lord into my heart. Let the snake bird or whatever magical thing the brahmana created bite me at once. I only desire that you all continue singing the deeds of Lord Vishnu.

---
### surrender, fearlessness, association
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/XYR_B2uQVU8?t=670
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "This freedom from fear of death is a very very important point."
**Summary:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami emphasizes Pariksit Maharaj's perfect surrender to Krishna, the spiritual master, the Vaishnavas, and Mother Ganga, highlighting his fearlessness in the face of death due to his firm Krishna consciousness and desire to hear the glories of the Lord. He concludes by explaining that the association of devotees creates a spiritual atmosphere that guarantees one's return to Godhead, freeing them from the bewilderment and fear that accompany death for a conditioned soul.

So this is the right attitude for a perfect devotee who is properly prepared to go back to Godhead. That Parikshit is sitting there in the holy place on the bank of the river Ganga in the association of great saintly persons that he is praying to them, accept me as a completely surrendered soul. Surrender does not mean only surrender to Krishna. Surrender means surrender to God. Surrender to Krishna. Surrender to the spiritual master. Surrender to the Vaishnavas. Surrender to the saintly persons. So this is surrender. Perfect surrender. And he is also praying, let Mother Ganges, the representative of the Lord, also accept me in that way. That means he is surrendered even to Mother Ganga. Mother Ganga is especially carrying the dust of the lotus feet of the Supreme Lord. So she purifies whoever comes in contact with her. So Parikshit wants to be completely purified in preparation for going back to Godhead. So therefore he is praying to Mother Ganga also. Please accept me as a surrendered soul and purify me. And also he is telling he has taken the lotus feet of the Lord into his heart. He is not afraid of death due to the snake bite which is the Brahmana's curse because he is firmly situated in Krishna consciousness. Of course he is requesting the saintly persons to sing the deeds of Lord Vishnu, Vishnugatha. So he will be immersed in hearing the glories of the Supreme Lord even at the last moment of his life in this material world. In the purport Srila Prabhupada says as soon as one is given up completely unto the lotus feet of the Supreme Lord, he is not at all afraid of death. The atmosphere created by the presence of great devotees of the Lord on the bank of the Ganges and Maharaj Pariksit's complete acceptance of the Lord's lotus feet was sufficient guarantee to the King for going back to Godhead. See when devotees are present they create a completely spiritual atmosphere and in such a spiritual atmosphere one can be perfectly situated in Krishna consciousness an aspiring transcendentalist or even a devotee who is prepared to go back to Godhead cannot get a better atmosphere than this. The association of topmost transcendentalists in the universe and Pariksit Maharaj has also accepted the Lord's lotus feet in his heart. So these are guaranteed for the King to go back to Godhead. And in this way, Srila Prabhupada says, he became absolutely free from all fear of death. This freedom from fear of death is a very very important point. It is only because of the fear of death and when actually death comes we are so afraid that we are completely bewildered in what we should do when we have to quit the body. We try to take steps to take some material shelter or some material remedy or some material attachment. All these things bewilder a conditioned soul when quitting the body at the time of death. It is a very fearful moment. But for a pure devotee it is not at all fearful. It is completely free from fear of death. I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

---

## SB 1.19.16-17 | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | 21-05-2023 | Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### humility, devotion, association
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/3r-KfJCCS0s?t=35
**Verse References:** SB 1.19.16
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "I pray that if I should again take my birth in the material world, I will have complete attachment to the unlimited Lord Krishna, association with his devotees, and friendly relations with all living beings."
**Summary:** Pariksit Maharaj expresses his desire for complete attachment to Krishna, association with devotees, and friendly relations with all beings, highlighting the importance of these three aspects for a devotee.

Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare. Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya Narayanam Namaskritya Naram Chai Vanarottamam. Devim Sarasvatim Vyasam Tato Jayam Udhirayet. In this session, we will be discussing the 16th and 17th verses of the 19th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavata. The 16th verse is as follows: Punashya Bhuvyad Bhagavatyanante Ratiprasangascha Tad Ashrayeshu Mahatsuyamyam Upayami Srishtim Maitriyastu Sarvatra Namo Dvijayabhyaha. The meaning of this verse is, again offering obeisances unto all you brahmanas, I pray that if I should again take my birth in the material world, I will have complete attachment to the unlimited Lord Krishna, association with his devotees, and friendly relations with all living beings. So Pariksit is speaking to the assembled sages, great saintly personalities from all the universe have assembled there to witness Pariksit quitting his body because Pariksit is a pure devotee. Therefore they come to witness that event, but Pariksit is taking a humble position and he offers his obeisances to all the brahmanas assembled there, and he prays that if at all he takes birth again in the material world, he desires three things. What are the three things? One is that he will have complete attachment to the unlimited Lord Krishna. The second thing he desires is association with Krishna's devotees. And the third thing he desires is friendly relation with all living beings. So Srila Prabhupada explains on the purport these three points.

---
### devotee, association, Krishna consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/3r-KfJCCS0s?t=192
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "A devotee of the Lord is no one's enemy, although there may be many enemies of a devotee."
**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada explains that a devotee is the only perfect living being, characterized by being no one's enemy, avoiding association with non-devotees, desiring association with devotees, and having complete attachment to Krishna.

Srila Prabhupada says that a devotee of the Lord is the only devotee of the Lord. The devotee of the Lord is the only devotee of the Lord. The devotee is the only perfect living being explained herein by Maharaj Pariksit. Of all the different categories of people in this world, material world, the devotee is the only perfect living being. Why? Because of certain qualifications. Srila Prabhupada says, a devotee of the Lord is no one's enemy, although there may be many enemies of a devotee. What does this mean? Yudhishthira had no enemy. He had no enmity with anybody, including Duryodhana. But Duryodhana considered Yudhishthira Maharaj as his enemy. So this is the position of a devotee. Further, Srila Prabhupada says, a devotee of the Lord does not like to associate with non-devotees, although he has no enmity with them. So Chaitanya Mahaprabhu said, the characteristic of a Vaishnava is asatsangatyag-e-Vaishnava-achar. So one category of a devotee. Asat means those who are materially attached. One category of asat is non-devotee, Krishna-abhakta. And another category of asat is strisangi, those who are simply attached to objects of sense gratification, strisangi. So a devotee does not like to associate with non-devotees. He always wants association of other devotees. So Srila Prabhupada says further, he desires, that is, a devotee desires association with the devotees of the Lord. This is perfectly natural because birds of the same feather mix together. Naturally, a devotee is inclined to do devotional service, hear about and glorify the Supreme Lord always. So he would like to be in the association of other like-minded persons who are devotees like him. And the most important function of a devotee is to have complete attachment for Lord Shri Krishna, the father of all living beings.

---
### Krishna, fatherhood of God, devotee qualities
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/3r-KfJCCS0s?t=380
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "It's insanity to forget Krishna, to be independent of Krishna, to disobey Krishna's instructions. It's insane."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the devotee's perspective of seeing all living beings as related to Krishna, the Supreme Father, and the devotee's role in guiding others towards Krishna consciousness, emphasizing that forgetting Krishna is a form of insanity. He also highlights that a devotee values the association of other devotees regardless of their material status.

A devotee perfectly understands Krishna is the supreme father of everyone. As a good son of the father behaves in a friendly way with all his other brothers, so also the devotee of the Lord, being a good son of the Supreme Father Lord Krishna, sees all other living beings in relation with the Supreme Father. Why is it that a devotee has no enmity with anyone? Because he sees everyone, irrespective of their present condition. Everyone is part and parcel of Krishna. Everyone is a good son of the Supreme Lord Krishna. But presently, some of them are forgetful of Krishna. He tries to bring back the upstart sons of the father to a saner stage. It's insanity to forget Krishna, to be independent of Krishna, to disobey Krishna's instructions. It's insane. So a devotee preaches in such a way that somebody may become sane. And also his preaching is meant to get them to accept the supreme fatherhood of God. Maharaj Pariksit was certainly going back to Godhead. But even if he were not to go back, he prayed for a pattern of life, which is the most perfect way in the material world. How to live in this world perfectly, if at all somebody wants to live like a devotee. So Srila Prabhupada says, a pure devotee does not desire the company of a personality as great as Brahma, but he prefers the association of a petty living being, provided that petty living being is a devotee of the Lord. So a devotee desires association of other devotees. Now the other devotees' material position doesn't matter. Doesn't matter. That's not important. Just important is the fact that they are devotees.

---
### renunciation, auspiciousness, preparation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/3r-KfJCCS0s?t=536
**Verse References:** SB 1.19.17
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Facing the north is still more favorable for attaining spiritual success."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the meaning of the seventeenth verse, describing Pariksit Maharaj's renunciation and preparation for death, including handing over his kingdom to his son and sitting in a specific posture on the bank of the Ganges. He highlights the auspiciousness of these actions in facilitating spiritual success.

The seventeenth verse. Itismaraja adhyavasayayuktaha Prachinamuleshu Deshu dhiraha Udanmukho dakshina koola aaste Samudra patnya swasuta niyasta bhara ha. The meaning of this seventeenth verse is, in perfect self-control, Maharaj Pariksit sat down on a seat of straw, with straw roots facing the east, placed on the southern bank of the Ganges, and he himself faced the north. Just previously, he had given charge of his kingdom over to his son. This means Pariksit completely renounced all connections with his wealth, with his family, with his possessions, everything. But, responsibly, he did hand over charge of ruling the world to his worthy son. So in the Patpotshila, Prabhupada says, the river Ganges is celebrated as the wife of the sea. This is a description in the scriptures. The river always goes and joins the sea. The river flows from where it starts, and goes on flowing, flowing, flowing, till it joins the sea. So, in that sense, the river Ganga is celebrated as the wife of the sea. Here, Samudra Patnya, that is referring to the Ganges, the wife of the sea. The seat of Kusha's straw is considered to be sanctified if the straw is taken out of the earth complete with root, and the root is pointed toward the east, it is considered to be auspicious. That means Pariksit is preparing himself to go back to Godhead with all auspicious conditions, favoring his going back to Godhead. Facing the north is still more favorable for attaining spiritual success. Though, as a pure devotee, he is not dependent on all these arrangements, but taking a humble position, he adopts all the necessities required for going back to Godhead. Maharaj Pariksit handed over the charge of administration to his son before leaving home.

---
### Varnashrama, administration, spiritual success, back to Godhead
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/3r-KfJCCS0s?t=734
**Verse References:** BG 8.5
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "That is the ultimate success of the human form of life, that one may simply aim to go back to Godhead after quitting the present body."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the Vedic Varnashrama system, the roles of Brahmanas and Kshatriyas, and how Pariksit Maharaj trained his son before renouncing the kingdom. He concludes by emphasizing that the ultimate goal of human life is to go back to Godhead, as taught by Krishna in the Bhagavad Gita.

You see, the Vedic culture, the Varnashrama system, the administration is carried out by the Kshatriyas, qualified Kshatriyas. The Brahmanas are intelligent persons who are learned in the scriptures, but they don't occupy the post of the administrator or the king. They give guidance to everyone else in the society, including the king, the Kshatriyas, the Vaishyas, the Shudras, everyone, for their spiritual success in life, and also guides them in maintaining themselves by appropriately carrying out some occupational activities. So, Brahmana is the perfect guide for everyone in the society. The Kshatriyas are meant to do administration. So, Pariksit Maharaj is ruling perfectly. When he was given charge by Yudhishthira, his grandfather, when the Pandavas retired timely, they handed over charge to Pariksit. They had trained up Pariksit before they could hand over charge. Similarly, Pariksit had trained his son Janamejaya, and Janamejaya took charge after Pariksit retired, renouncing the kingdom. So, Srila Prabhupada concludes by telling, Pariksit was thus fully equipped for all favorable conditions in order to go back to Godhead. That is the ultimate success of the human form of life, that one may simply aim to go back to Godhead after quitting the present body. And that's what Krishna teaches in the Bhagavad Gita. punar janma naiti mam eti sorjuna tyaktva deham punar janma naiti mam eti sorjuna. So, that is the perfection of the human form of life, as Krishna says in Bhagavad Gita. I'll stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

---

## SB 1.19.18-19 | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | 22-05-2023 | Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### introduction, Srimad Bhagavatam, Pariksit Maharaj
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/I6Dira1IULE?t=29
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "That's the entire first canto, practically speaking."
**Summary:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami introduces the session, outlining the verses to be discussed from the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam, focusing on Pariksit Maharaj's meeting with Shukadeva Goswami and the setting of Suta Goswami speaking to the sages of Naimisharanya.

Shri Advaita Gatadhara, Shri Vasadi Gauravakta Vrinda, Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Harē Harē, Harē Rāma, Harē Rāma, Rāma Rāma, Harē Harē, Om Namo Bhagavate Vāsudevāya, Nārāyaṇaṃ Namaskritya Naramchayi Vaṇarottamam, Devīṃ Saraswatīṃ Vyāsaṃ Tatojayaṃ Udīrayet. In this session, we will be discussing from two verses, the eighteenth and the nineteenth verses of the nineteenth chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. This chapter describes Pariksit Maharaj meeting Shukadeva Goswami and subsequently, from the second canto onwards, the conversation between Pariksit and Shukadeva Goswami begins. That's the actual beginning of the Bhagavatam. But before the conversation between Pariksit and Shukadeva Goswami, the first canto, there are the descriptions of Suta Goswami conversing with, speaking to the sages of Naimisharanya. That's the entire first canto, practically speaking.

---
### Pariksit Maharaj, demigods, fasting until death
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/I6Dira1IULE?t=112
**Verse References:** SB 1.19.18
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "All the demigods of the higher planets praised the king's actions and in pleasure continually scattered flowers over the earth and beat celestial drums."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the meaning of the eighteenth verse, describing Pariksit Maharaj's decision to fast until death and the reaction of the demigods who praised his actions and honored him.

So, the eighteenth verse, evam cha tasmin naradeva deve, prayo pavishte dvideva sanghaha, prashasya bhumau vyakiran prasunaihi, muda muhur dundubhayascha niduhu. The meaning of this eighteenth verse is, thus the king, Maharaj Pariksit, set to fast until death. All the demigods of the higher planets praised the king's actions and in pleasure continually scattered flowers over the earth and beat celestial drums. So, this is a description of Pariksit Maharaj getting the consent of the assembled great saintly persons.

### interplanetary communication, life on other planets, Srila Prabhupada
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/I6Dira1IULE?t=175
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Even up to the time of Maharaj Pariksit, there were interplanetary communications."
**Summary:** The speaker quotes Srila Prabhupada's purport, emphasizing the existence of interplanetary communication and life on other planets, contrasting this with the modern view that only Earth is inhabited.

To fast until death and prepare himself to quit his body in order to go back to Godhead. So, the devatas took note of this because he is such a great personality, famous throughout the universe because of his being a pure devotee. So, in the purport, Srila Prabhupada says, even up to the time of Maharaj Pariksit, there were interplanetary communications. And the news of Maharaj Pariksit's fasting unto death to attain salvation reached the higher planets in the sky where the intelligent demigods live. See, Prabhupada is telling us in the upper planets there are more intelligent beings than the humans on the earth. Modern day people tend to think that all other planets are vacant and there is life only on this earth planet. But that is completely wrong. There are living beings on every planet of this creation. And there are living beings even in the spiritual planets.

---
### life in different environments, adaptation, universal life
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/I6Dira1IULE?t=258
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "But actually every type of atmosphere supports life, in its own way."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the concept of life existing in various environments, using the analogy of desert life and different types of bodies adapted to different atmospheres, including fire, water, and land.

All are inhabited. What to speak of other planets even in this desert area of this earth planet. There may seem to be only vast expanse of some sand. But that is the uninformed ignorant foolish people who think like that there is no life. But there are so many living creatures who are adapted to live there. It is like even in a desert there are some vegetation like there are date trees. There are cactus which grow. And buried in the sand there are so many crawling insects. Some varieties of worms. So naked eye cannot see them. So therefore somebody may wrongly conclude that there is no life. But actually every type of atmosphere supports life, in its own way. Just like Srila Prabhupada informs us. Even in the sun planet there are living beings with bodies made of fire. In the water there are so many aquatics. They have bodies suitable for living in water. We are terrestrial beings. We are meant to live on the land. We cannot live in the sea, like the aquatics. The aquatics cannot live on the land. You bring a fish out of water and place him on the land. He will not survive for long. So similarly if this atmosphere on the land is suitable for us, the other creatures have suitable living conditions in other types of atmosphere.

---
### material bodies, demigods, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/I6Dira1IULE?t=391
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "There is no one in the heavenly planets who is an atheist or non-believer. This is the truth or the fact."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the different types of material bodies adapted to various elements and the nature of demigods, emphasizing their intelligence, piety, and devotion to the Supreme Lord.

Like there are so many germs that are living in the air. They live in the air. There are some living beings living in the sky that we are not aware of. But from the scriptures we get information. So just like this body is made of the earthly element that we have, there could be a body made of fire. There could be a body made of water. There could be a body made of air. There could be a body made of sky. So these are different types of material bodies for different creatures, for adapting or acclimatizing themselves to live in different places, different types of atmosphere. So the demigods live in higher planets. They are adapted to living in those conditions. So they are many times more intelligent than humans. They have much better facilities for living there, and they have much much longer duration of life. And they are pious, many times more pious than the pious human beings on the earth. So Srila Prabhupada describes the devatas. The demigods are more luxurious than human beings, but all of them are obedient to the orders of the Supreme Lord. Vishnu Bhaktas Vruto Daiva. This is the definition in the scriptures. All of them are devotees of the Supreme Lord. Therefore they are obedient to the order of the Supreme Lord. There is no one in the heavenly planets who is an atheist or non-believer. This is the truth or the fact.

---
### devotee, demigods, Krishna worship
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/I6Dira1IULE?t=519
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "On the other hand devatas will be very pleased with such a devotee."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that devotees of the Lord are praised by the demigods, who are pleased to see someone go back to Godhead, and clarifies that the demigods are not angered when one exclusively worships Krishna.

Thus any devotee of the Lord on the surface of the earth is always praised by them, the residents of the demigods in the heavenly planets. And in the case of Maharaj Pariksit, they were greatly delighted and thus gave tokens of honor by scattering flowers over the earth and by beating celestial drums. A demigod takes pleasure in seeing someone go back to Godhead. He is always pleased with a devotee of the Lord, so much so that by his Adi Daivik powers he may help the devotees in all respects. So sometimes people foolishly think that when the scriptures, we point out in the scriptures it is said you should exclusively worship Krishna and not the devatas. They think oh if we don't worship the devatas, devatas may become angry against us. But Srila Prabhupada is telling here devatas are always pleased with a devotee of the Supreme Lord Krishna, because they themselves are devotees of Krishna. So don't think that if a person who is in ignorance worshipping some devata stops devata worship and instead worships Krishna as Krishna is instructed in the Bhagavad Gita, don't think that the devatas will become angry. No. On the other hand devatas will be very pleased with such a devotee. And by their actions the Lord is pleased with them. There is an invisible chain of complete cooperation between the Lord.

---
### sages, Pariksit Maharaj, approval
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/I6Dira1IULE?t=631
**Verse References:** SB 1.19.19
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "All the great sages who were assembled there also praised the decision of Maharaj Pariksha, and they expressed their approval by saying very good."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the 19th verse, explaining that the sages present approved of Maharaj Pariksit's decision to fast until death, seeing it as a way to return to Godhead.

The demigods and the devotee of the Lord on earth. The 19th verse, Maharsha Yovai Samupagataye Prashasya Sadhu Iti Anumoda Manaha, Uchuhu Prajanugraha Sheela Saara, Yad Uttama Shloka Guna Bhi Roopam. The meaning of this 19th verse is as follows. All the great sages who were assembled there also praised the decision of Maharaj Pariksha, and they expressed their approval by saying very good. This is called Sadhu Vada. Naturally the sages are inclined to do good to common men, for they have all the qualitative powers of the Supreme Lord. Therefore they were very much pleased to see Maharaj Pariksha a devotee of the Lord, and they spoke as follows. So this is a description of what is the reaction of the great saintly persons who were assembled in that spot where Pariksha was sitting and fasting unto death in the holy place on the bank of the river Ganga. What is their reaction? They said very good. They approved Pariksha's decision to fast until death and prepare himself to go back to Godhead.

### spiritual beauty, devotion, Sadhu
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/I6Dira1IULE?t=722
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The natural beauty of a living being is enhanced by rising up to the platform of devotion and service. This is called spiritual beauty."
**Summary:** The speaker quotes Srila Prabhupada on spiritual beauty and explains that the sages approved of Pariksit's devotion. He defines a Sadhu as one who lives for the welfare of others and is surrendered to Krishna.

In the purport Srila Prabhupada says, the natural beauty of a living being is enhanced by rising up to the platform of devotion and service. This is called spiritual beauty. Spiritual beauty is different. Material beauty is different. Maharaj Pariksha was absorbed in attachment for Lord Krishna. Seeing this the great sages assembled were very pleased, and they expressed their approval by saying very good. Sadhu Vada. Sadhu. Sadhu. Because Pariksha requested them that I am asking your opinion whether it is appropriate for me to prepare myself to go back to Godhead by fasting until death after having renounced my kingdom. So they are telling very good because that is the natural way of preparation for quitting the body for a devotee of the Lord. Such sages are naturally inclined to do good to the common man. Sadhu means, the very word Sadhu means one who lives for the welfare of others. A Sadhu is not concerned about his own welfare because he is completely surrendered to the Supreme Lord Krishna.

---
### Sadhu, devotional service, auspiciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/I6Dira1IULE?t=822
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The devotional service of the Lord is so auspicious that all demigods and sages up to the Lord himself become pleased with the devotee."
**Summary:** The speaker continues defining a Sadhu, emphasizing their active role in benefiting others and the auspiciousness of devotional service, which pleases the demigods, sages, and the Lord himself. He concludes by noting that Pariksit's curse was a blessing in disguise.

So Krishna takes care of the Sadhu completely. He doesn't have to care for himself. On the other hand a Sadhu as a devotee. Sadhu means a devotee. So as a good devotee of the Supreme Lord, a Sadhu tries to do good to others always. So if he doesn't have to do anything for himself doesn't mean he is inactive. He is very very active in doing good for others. And when a Sadhu sees a personality like Maharaj Pariksit advance in devotional service such a Sadhu's pleasure knows no bounds, and such saintly persons offer all blessings in their power. The devotional service of the Lord is so auspicious that all demigods and sages up to the Lord himself become pleased with the devotee, and therefore the devotee finds everything auspicious. For a devotee is preparing to go back to God and nothing is inauspicious. All inauspicious matters are removed from the path of a progressive devotee. Meeting all the great sages at the time of death was certainly auspicious for Maharaj Pariksit, and thus he was blessed by the so-called curse of a Brahmana's boy. It's ironical that Pariksit was cursed but that curse turned out to be a boon in disguise. That's because Pariksit is a pure devotee. I'll stop here. Thank you very much.

## SB 1.19.20 | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | 23-05-2023 | Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### detachment, materialism, liberation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/tztk2AgxKQ4?t=87
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Foolish politicians who hold political administrative force think that the temporary post they occupy are the highest material gain of life and therefore they stick to those posts even up to the last moment of life without knowing that achievement of liberation as one of the associates of the Lord in his eternal abode is the highest gain of life."
**Summary:** The speaker explains Srila Prabhupada's purport on the verse, contrasting the attachment of materialistic politicians to temporary posts with the higher goal of achieving liberation and association with the Lord.

The meaning of this verse: the sages said, "O chief of all the saintly kings of the Pandu dynasty who are strictly in the line of Lord Sri Krishna, it is not at all astonishing that you give up your throne which is decorated with the helmets of many kings to achieve eternal association with the personality of Godhead." This is the praise of these assembled saintly persons with regard to Parikshit's action of renouncing his kingdom and coming to this holy place to prepare himself to go back to Godhead by fasting unto death. So an elaborate purport is given by Srila Prabhupada explaining this appreciation of the saintly persons of Parikshit's action. Srila Prabhupada says in the purport, "Foolish politicians who hold political administrative force think that the temporary post they occupy are the highest material gain of life and therefore they stick to those posts even up to the last moment of life without knowing that achievement of liberation as one of the associates of the Lord in his eternal abode is the highest gain of life." Here Prabhupada is comparing modern-day politicians who are ignorant about spiritual reality. They think that to hold some very high position in this material world, even though it is a temporary position, they think that the post they occupy is the highest gain of their life, and they stick on to that position till they are forced to leave that by being forced to die, quit the body, and they are ignorant of the fact that they are not the body, they are spirit soul. Therefore, after quitting the body at the time of death, they have to enter another body that they are not aware. But if they know about spiritual reality, then they understand there is something much, much, much, much greater to be obtained in this human form of life by practicing Krishna consciousness and achieving liberation to become one of the associates of the Supreme Lord in his personal abode in the spiritual world that they do not know.

---
### human life, material suffering, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/tztk2AgxKQ4?t=279
**Verse References:** BG 9.34
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The human life is meant for achieving this end, this is the goal of human life."
**Summary:** The speaker quotes Prabhupada on the purpose of human life and then discusses Prahlad Maharaj's fear of the materialistic way of life, explaining how it is like being crushed by a grinding stone due to the constant harassment of the three gunas and the miseries they inflict.

So Srila Prabhupada says in the purport further, "The human life is meant for achieving this end, this is the goal of human life." The Lord has assured us in the Bhagavad Gita many times that going back to Godhead, his eternal abode, is the highest achievement. Yad gatva nani vartante tad dhama paramam mama. Madh karmakrana madh paramo madh bhaktasangavarjitah nirvaira sarvabhuteshu yassa mameti pandava. Yaktvadeham punarjanmaneti mameti sorjuna. So many places in the Bhagavad Gita Krishna is talking about this. Further Srila Prabhupada says Prahlad Maharaj, while praying to Lord Narasimha, said, "Oh my Lord, I am very much afraid of the materialistic way of life." Prahlad Maharaj is a pure devotee, actually he is fearless, but still he is teaching us by telling that he is very much afraid of the materialistic way of life. And he further says, "I am not the least afraid of your present ghastly ferocious feature as Nrsimha Deva." Well, Lord Nrsimha appeared to protect Prahlad Maharaj against his demoniac father. Nrsimha was very, very, very angry. Against whom was he angry? He was angry against Hiranyakashyapu. He was never angry at Prahlad, and Prahlad knew this. Therefore, Prahlad is telling Lord Nrsimha, "I am not afraid of your ferocious form, because that ferocious form is meant for protecting the devotee. He is meant to chastise the non-devotee, atheist, demon, that the devotee is confident." And Prahlad Maharaj is telling why is he afraid of materialistic way of life. He is telling this materialistic way of life is something like a grinding stone and we are being crushed by it. See, foolish people don't realize that taking birth in the material world means accepting a material body. And this material body is subject to so many miseries under the influence of these three gunas which are constantly harassing us in the form of different kinds of miseries being inflicted against our being—vishnu. Adi bhautika klesha, adhyatmika klesha, adi daivika klesha, adi bhautika kess wastewater, abhyatmika klesha, vishulya, and any kunti and any sort of dem Dievikal klesha. They don't want us to face the agenda beyond our awareness and control. In the form of different kinds of miseries being inflicted against our wish. Adi bhautika klesha Adi spiritually 大以上的外外. And balanced by that divine nature and grit. Constantly being harassed by this.

---
### material world, suffering, liberation, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/tztk2AgxKQ4?t=486
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "If at all you want freedom from the suffering of this world then you should simply aim to become one of the eternal associates of the supreme lord in his personal abode as his loving devotional servitor."
**Summary:** The speaker continues explaining Prahlad Maharaj's prayer, using the analogy of someone drowning in the ocean to describe the suffering in the material world. He emphasizes that liberation comes from becoming an eternal associate of the Lord.

So Prahlad Maharaj is giving an analogy, it's something like being crushed in a grinding stone continuously. And another example Prahlad is giving here when praying to Narasimha Deva, he is telling, "We have fallen into this horrible whirlpool of the tossing waves of life. My lord, I pray at your lotus feet to call me back to your eternal abode as one of your servitors." Another example Prahlad is giving, suppose somebody who's meant to live on the land falls into the middle of the ocean, then ocean means continuously there are waves and these waves will toss the person up and down, up and down, so it will be a struggle to keep himself afloat because continuously waves will be coming and trying to overpower the person who has to protect himself against being drowned by those waves. So in a similar way, through this Prahlad is telling this material world we are drowning in the waves of the attacked by Maya in the form of continuous miseries being inflicted through the gunas. Instead Prahlad Maharaj is praying to the lord, "Please call me back to your eternal abode as one of your servants." Prahlad further says this is the summit liberation of the materialistic way of life. If at all you want freedom from the suffering of this world then you should simply aim to become one of the eternal associates of the supreme lord in his personal abode as his loving devotional servitor. That is what Prahlad Maharaj is teaching in this prayer.

---
### material experience, suffering, detachment, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/tztk2AgxKQ4?t=627
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "In whichever species of life I have taken birth compelled by the force of my own activities, I have very painfully experienced two things: separation from my beloved and meeting with what is not wanted."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that Parikshit is preparing to return to the spiritual world. He then elaborates on Prahlad Maharaj's bitter experiences in the material world, highlighting the pain of separation from Krishna and the experience of unwanted miseries, and how material remedies are worse than the disease itself.

And what is Pariksit praying for? He is also preparing to go back to the spiritual world to become one of the associates of the supreme lord. Further Prahlad is telling, "I have very bitter experience of the materialistic way of life." He has already said it is like a grinding stone in which we are getting crushed or like falling into a rock or like falling into a rock or like falling into a middle of the ocean being tossed by the waves continuously. Now further he is telling the bitter experience he has had in this material world. What is that? "In whichever species of life I have taken birth compelled by the force of my own activities, I have very painfully experienced two things." This is common for everyone, not just for Prahlad. Please hear carefully. Two things that everyone is experiencing which they don't realize. What are the two things? Separation from my beloved. Who is our beloved? Our beloved is the supreme lord Krishna. In the material world we are separated from him. And the second thing is we all experience meeting with what is not wanted. Unwanted miseries are forced upon us in this material existence. And further Prahlad says, "To counteract these unwanted miseries, the remedies which I undertook were more dangerous than the disease itself." We are suffering from a disease called bhava roga, bhava roga, and material means to counteract this bhava roga are worse than suffering the disease of bhava roga itself. So I drift from one point to another birth after birth. The misery doesn't stop simply by giving up one body, thinking that my suffering will be coming to an end when I quit the body. Just like when somebody is suffering too much, they wish that they die rather than live, especially somebody who has got a terminal illness, chronic disease, they cannot tolerate the suffering and they wish and pray that I may die, but death is not the solution because they are going to take birth again. And taking birth again without preparing to go back to God, it means one more material body, and again that material body also under the clutches of maya they are going to suffer similarly. Therefore Prahlad is concluding the prayer by telling, "I pray to you therefore to give me shelter at your lotus feet. I don't want to be under the clutches of maya." That's what Prahlad is telling.

---
### detachment, parampara, Krishna consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/tztk2AgxKQ4?t=833
**Verse References:** BG 4.2
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The Pandava kings who are more than many saints of the world knew the bitter results of materialistic way of life."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes by explaining how the Pandava kings, despite their power, were not captivated by material allurements and were always ready to return to the Lord. He emphasizes their detachment and adherence to the parampara, following Krishna's instructions.

Now Srila Prabhupada continues by telling, "The Pandava kings who are more than many saints of the world knew the bitter results of materialistic way of life." They were never captivated by the glare of the imperial throne they occupied. They were very, very powerful. They were emperors of the world, entire world, very powerful, but they were not captivated by some allurement of maya. Therefore they always sought the opportunity being called by the lord to associate with him eternally. They were just waiting, as long as the lord wants us to rule this world, we will rule, but when he calls us back, we are ready to go for eternal life there. So this is temporary, material existence is temporary. So Maharaj Parikshit said that, so Maharaj Parikshit was the worthy grandson of Maharaj Yudhishtira. So just like Maharaj Yudhishtira gave up the imperial throne to his grandson, who is his grandson? Parikshit. Similarly, Parikshit gave up the imperial throne to his son Janame Jaya. That is the way of all the kings in the dynasty. They are all strictly in the line of Lord Krishna. That is what is described in this verse by the sages. All the kings in these saintly dynasty of saintly kings, Rajarshi Parampara. Krishna says, evam parampara praptam imam rajarsha yo viduh, the saintly kings, they strictly follow the instructions of Krishna. They don't permanently hold on to any post in the material world. So they live unattached to all the objects of materialistic way of life. They never are attached, so giving up is very easy for them, and giving up for what? For spiritual life. I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.
