### devotee, departure, Srimad Bhagavatam
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/NPycqizH4DA?t=56
**Verse References:** SB 1.19.21
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "We shall all wait here until the foremost devotee of the Lord Maharaj Pariksit returns to the supreme planet which is completely free from all mundane contamination and all kinds of lamentation."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the verses to be discussed, SB 1.19.21-22, explaining that the sages assembled to witness Pariksit Maharaj's departure and to hear the Srimad Bhagavatam.

In this session, we are discussing from the 21st and 22nd verses of the 19th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. The 21st verse is as follows: Sarvevayam Tavad Ihasmahetha Kalevaram Yavad Asavvihaya Lokamparam Virajaskam Vishokam Yasyati Ayam Bhagavatam Pradhanaham. The meaning of this verse is, we shall all wait here until the foremost devotee of the Lord Maharaj Pariksit returns to the supreme planet which is completely free from all mundane contamination and all kinds of lamentation. This verse is spoken by the assembled sages. They have assembled in that holy spot where Pariksit decided to sit down to fast until death, preparing for quitting his body to go back home, back to the environment, on the bank of the river Ganga, a holy place. Quiser sages who were received very respectfully by Pariksit. It was keep saying that, Maharaj, they have come from different parts of the universe and they are all very very great saintly persons and they could foresee that the Srimad Bhagavatam is going to be spoken and therefore they have come here to listen to the Bhagavatam.

---
### spiritual sky, material creation, Vaikuntha
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/NPycqizH4DA?t=170
**Verse References:** BG 9.4, BG 15.6
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Beyond the limitation of the material creation, which is compared to the cloud in the sky, there is the paravyoma or the spiritual sky full of planets called Vaikunthas."
**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada's purport is discussed, explaining the difference between the limited material creation and the unlimited spiritual sky (Paravyoma) filled with Vaikuntha planets, referencing the Bhagavad Gita and Srimad Bhagavatam.

And the purport Srila Prabhupada explains, beyond the limitation of the material creation which is compared to the cloud in the sky, there is the paravyoma or the spiritual sky full of planets called Vaikunthas. This explained in the Bhagavad Gita and the Srimad Bhagavatam. In the Bhagavad Gita it is said by Krishna, changes in terms of creation, maintenance and destruction. Unlike material nature, there is another spiritual nature. That spiritual nature is beyond this material creation, beyond the material sky. So, Srila Prabhupada is quoting that Bhagavad Gita and telling beyond the limitation of the material creation. Material creation is limited even though in the material creation there are millions of universes and each universe has millions of planets, each planet has millions of living entities but still it is comparable to a cloud in the sky. It is like a sky is very vast but the cloud is just few hundred meters or at the most a few kilometers spread over the sky, vast sky, expanse of the sky. Similarly, there is the Paravyoma, the spiritual sky, Paravyoma. Vyoma means the sky and Paravyoma is the spiritual sky. Now, the spiritual sky is not known to everyone. Of course, the materialists have no conception of spiritual nature at all because they only accept what is perceivable by their gross senses.

---
### spiritualists, impersonalists, devotees, Vaikuntha Lokas
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/NPycqizH4DA?t=324
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The information or the reality of the truth about spiritual planets in the spiritual sky is known only to the devotees, only to bhakti yogis, the category of transcendentalist called personalists or bhakti yogis or devotees."
**Summary:** The speaker differentiates between materialists, impersonalists, and devotees, explaining that only devotees (bhakti yogis) truly understand the spiritual sky and the Vaikuntha planets, which have different names like Purushottama Loka and Achyuta Loka.

And some other material is who may accept something about the mind. They may imagine something beyond what is grossly manifest. But the transcendentalists from the authoritative scriptures are aware of the spiritual nature. But even among the spiritualists, there is a spiritual nature. Transcendentalists who are aware of the spiritual nature the impersonalists only know about the spiritual sky which is called paravyoma or brahma jyoti or brahman manifestation beyond the material creation but they do not know that the spiritual sky is full of planets called vaikunthas. So the information or the reality of the truth about spiritual planets in the spiritual sky is known only to the devotees only to bhakti yogis the category of transcendentalist called personalists or bhakti yogis or devotees. Such vaikuntha planets are also differently known as proper gives the names of different vaikuntha planets just like the material planets we have names like brahma loka mahar loka jana loka tapo loka satya bhu loka bhuvar loka atala sutala vitala talatala like that so vaikuntha planets also have different names such as purushottama loka achyuta loka trivikrama loka hrishi kesha loka kesava loka aniruddha loka mada loka pradyumna loka sankarshana loka mada loka sridhar loka vaikuntha contains the ultimate nirvana of all things good and evil the true and absolute truth of the whole world is that.

### Vaikuntha Lokas, Supreme Lord, Devatas
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/NPycqizH4DA?t=490
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Unlike in the material world the different planets the predominating deities are different Devatas, but in the spiritual world in the spiritual sky the spiritual planets are predominated by one person."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that the names of Vaikuntha planets are derived from the predominating form of the Supreme Lord, contrasting this with the material world where different planets are governed by different Devatas.

Shridhara Loka, Vasudeva Loka, Ayodhya Loka, Dwarka Loka and many other millions of spiritual Lokas. So the name of the Vaikuntha planet if it is say the name is Purushottama Loka because it is predominated by the Supreme Lord whose name is Purushottama. If the predominating deity of the Vaikuntha planet is Trivikrama that planet is called Trivikrama Loka. If the predominating deity is Vasudeva it is called Vasudeva Loka like that. Now Dwarka is Krishna's own abode. Ayodhya is Lord Ramachandra's own abode. Like this there are many millions of spiritual Lokas wherein the personality of Godhead predominates. Unlike in the material world the different planets the predominating deities are different Devatas. So different Devatas are different. Different living beings. But in the spiritual world in the spiritual sky the spiritual planets are predominated by one person.

### Supreme Personality of Godhead, liberated souls, conditioned souls, spiritual bodies, material contamination
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/NPycqizH4DA?t=580
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Here anybody who is taking birth in this material world with a material body is a conditioned soul. The liberated souls never accept a material body."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the nature of the Supreme Personality of Godhead and the liberated souls in the spiritual planets, emphasizing the absence of material contamination and the presence of spiritual bodies, contrasting this with the conditioned souls in the material world.

One and only one Supreme Personality of Godhead. Now the Supreme Personality of Godhead himself even though he is one and only one person he expands himself in millions of forms each form having a different name. So according to the name of the particular form of the Supreme Personality of Godhead the Loka also derives its name. Achyuta is one expansion of the Supreme Lord. So where Achyuta predominates that planet is called Achyuta Loka like that. Now the special thing about these planets is the spiritual planets is all the living entities there in the spiritual planets are liberated souls. They are all liberated souls. And in the material world in the material planets in the material universes all are conditioned souls. You see the difference. Here anybody who is taking birth in this material world with a material body is a conditioned soul. The liberated souls never accept a material body. They live in the spiritual planets Vaikuntha Loka's. They live with spiritual bodies as good as that of the Lord. The Lord has a spiritual body. He never comes in touch with matter. He never has any material contact or material body never. So just like the Lord the living entities also there are liberated souls with spiritual bodies as good as that of the Lord. There is no material contamination. We are just come. Everything there is spiritual. And therefore there is nothing objectively lamentable. Vishokam. They are full of transcendental bliss and are without birth, death, old age and disease. See the material world is characterized by these four defects. Janmamrtyu, Jaravyadhi, Dukha, Doshanu, Darshanam. That is a description of the material world. The spiritual world in the spiritual planets there is no birth, no death, no disease, no old age.

Samagata Sarvata Eva Sarve Vedayatha Murti Dharastri Prishthe Nehat Namutra Nakaschanarth Rite Paranugraham Atma Sheelam. The meaning of this verse is as follows. The king said, Parikshit Maharaj is responding to the sages. O great sages, you have all very kindly assembled here, having come from all parts of the universe. You are all as good as supreme knowledge personified, who resides in the planet above the three worlds. That means who resides inside. Satyaloka. Consequently, you are naturally inclined to do good to others. But for this, you have no interest either in this life or in the next.

There are three planetary systems namely the lower worlds, the intermediate worlds and the upper worlds, upper worlds. Bhuloka, Bhuvarloka, Svarloka, this is the intermediate worlds. Then Maharloka, Janloka, Tapoloka, Satyaloka they are the upper worlds and lower worlds are Atala, Sutala, Talatala, Mahatala, Rasatala, Patala like that seven planetary systems below the earth. The conclusion arrived at thereof is that devotional service of the Lord Shri Krishna as it is confirmed by the Lord himself in the last phases of the Bhagavad Gita is the last word in relation to everyone's permanent duty in life. Situationally according to our present condition or situation we may have some temporary duties like a student has got a duty to study as long as the student. A householder has a duty towards maintenance of his family members but that is also limited to the period of the life of the householder. When he is a householder if the question is asked what is everyone's permanent duty in life that is answered in the Bhagavatam. The permanent duty of everyone is to render service to Krishna.

## SB 1.19.25 | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | 28-05-2023 | Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### Shukadeva Goswami, Srimad Bhagavatam, Appearance
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/IrxuFbv_D3Y?t=35
**Verse References:** SB 1.19.25
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "This verse describes the appearance of Shukadeva Goswami and that is the title of this chapter."
**Summary:** This segment introduces the verse describing the appearance of Shukadeva Goswami at the place where Parikshit Maharaj is fasting, highlighting the significance of the event and the gathering of saintly persons.

Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare. Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya Narayanam Namaskritya Naram Chai Vanarottamam Deviim Saraswatiim Vyasam Tato Jayam Udi Rayet. In this session we will be discussing from the 25th verse of the 19th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. Tatra Bhavad Bhagavan Vyasa Putro Yadritcha Yagam Atamano Anapekshaha Alakshalingo Nijalabhatushto Vritascha Balair Avadhuta Vesaha. The meaning of this verse is, at that moment there appeared the powerful son of Vyasadeva who travelled over the earth disinterested and satisfied with himself. He did not manifest any symptoms of belonging to any social order or status of life. He was surrounded with women and children and he dressed as if others had neglected him. This verse describes the appearance of Shukadeva Goswami and that is the title of this chapter. The appearance of Shukadeva Goswami. Where is he appearing? He is appearing in the place where Parikshit Maharaj has decided to sit and fast until death. In that holy place, on the bank of the river Ganga, where many saintly persons have come from all over the universe to witness Parikshit quitting his body. And they could also foresee, some of them could foresee that actually Bhagavatam is going to be spoken by Shukadeva Goswami. So now Shukadeva Goswami has arrived there.

---
### Bhagawan, Liberation, Devotional Service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/IrxuFbv_D3Y?t=172
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Devotional service is so satisfying that a person taking satisfaction from always being engaged in devotional service loses all interest in material affairs."
**Summary:** This segment explains the meaning of 'Bhagawan' and its application to Shukadeva Goswami, emphasizing the characteristics of liberated souls and their disinterest in material affairs due to their satisfaction in devotional service.

And the description is a wonderful description of Shukadeva Goswami. He is described, Prabhupada explains that in the purport elaborately. He is described by so many nice adjectives. Bhagawan. Here Bhagawan, Prabhupada says in the purport, in the purport Prabhupada says the word Bhagawan is sometimes used in relation to some of the great devotees of the Lord, like Shukadeva Goswami. Like Shukadeva Goswami. Bhagawan actually means the supreme personality of Godhead, Krishna. Bhagawan, one who possesses six particular opulences to an unlimited degree. That is a precise definition. Ayishwar Yashya Samagrasya Veer Yashya Yashyasa Shreyaha Gyanavairagya Yoshchaiva Shannambhaga Itingana. This is very specific. Specific definition for the word Bhagawan, when it applies to the supreme personality of Godhead, Krishna. But here Bhagawan is used for Shukadeva Goswami. Sometimes Bhagawan is used for Vyasadeva. Sometimes Bhagawan is used for Narada. Now when it is used in relation to some of the great devotees of the Lord, then it is explained by Prabhupada. Why? It doesn't mean that they are God. No. Such liberated souls are disinterested in the affairs of this material world. This is a characteristic. They are all liberated souls. So because they are liberated, they are completely pure. They don't have any taint. They don't have any taint of material contamination. They are never touched by any of the three gunas, three material modes. So they are always spiritually active. They are only engaged in spiritual activities, even in this material world. Now Shri La Prabhupada explains why is it that they are disinterested in the affairs of this material world? Because they are self-satisfied by great achievements of devotional service. Devotional service is so satisfying that a person taking satisfaction from always being engaged in devotional service loses all interest in material affairs. Therefore it is described, disinterested in material affairs. Anapek shah disinterested in material affairs.

---
### Shukadeva Goswami, Liberation, Spiritual Master
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/IrxuFbv_D3Y?t=367
**Verse References:** SB 1.2.6
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Shukadeva Goswami is a liberated soul by birth so he doesn't need to study the Vedas, he doesn't need to practice any process of self-realization because it is already self-realized."
**Summary:** This segment discusses Shukadeva Goswami's unique position as a liberated soul from birth, explaining why he didn't require formal spiritual training or Vedic study, and how he realized the Brahman feature before hearing the Bhagavatam.

As explained before, Shukadev Goswami never accepted any formal spiritual master, nor did he undergo any formal reformatory performances. This was explained right in the beginning of the Bhagavatam first canto second chapter divinity and divine service when Suta Goswami begins to speak address the sages of Naimisharanya the beginning of the Bhagavatam opening of the Bhagavatam the sages ask Suta Goswami six questions and Suta Goswami answers them so before he begins to answer he offers his prayers to his spiritual master whose Suta Goswami spiritual master Shukadeva Goswami so in that in those prayers he says that Shukadeva Goswami is a liberated soul by birth he is liberated soul by birth so he doesn't need to study the Vedas, he doesn't need to practice any process of self-realization because it is already self-realized though before he studied the Bhagavatam under his father Vyasa Deva he was only realized he had only realized the impersonal aspect of the absolute truth Brahman realized his soul there are three of the absolute truth. Brahman, Paramatma and Bhagavan. So Shukadeva Goswami had realized only the Brahman feature. But after he heard the Bhagavatam, he became fully self-realized in the Bhagavan feature also. Brahman, Paramatma, Bhagavan. So Bhagavan is the highest realization, complete realization of the absolute truth. And Bhagavan realization includes Brahman and Paramatma realization. So after hearing the Bhagavatam, Shukadeva Goswami became Bhagavan realized, became a great devotee of the Supreme Personality. Got it? So he never accepted any formal spiritual master like one goes to Gurukul or one takes initiation and begins to study the scriptures under the guidance of the bona fide spiritual master. No, he did not have to do that. Nor did he undergo any formal reformatory performance. Reformatory performance is samskara, purification, cultural purification. Just like Upanayana, the sacred thread ceremony. That signifies that one is eligible to study the Vedas under the spiritual master. One receives the Gayatri mantra from the bona fide spiritual master and one begins study of the Vedas. But Shukadeva Goswami did not have to undergo any of these samskaras including Upanayana because he was already a liberated soul. He was realized. He didn't have to study the Vedas.

---
### Vyasa Deva, Srimad Bhagavatam, Narada
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/IrxuFbv_D3Y?t=594
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "This dissatisfaction is because none of the literatures you have compiled, in none of them you have exclusively glorified the Supreme Personality of Godhead Krishna."
**Summary:** This segment explains how Vyasa Deva became Shukadeva Goswami's natural spiritual master by teaching him Srimad Bhagavatam, and recounts the story of Narada instructing Vyasa Deva to compile the Bhagavatam to exclusively glorify Krishna.

Shukade Goswami's father Vyasa Deva was his natural spiritual master. Why natural spiritual master? Because Shukade Goswami heard Srimad Bhagavatam from Vyasa Deva, his own father. Vyasa Deva was compiling the Bhagavatam after being instructed to do so by Narada. This has already been described in an earlier chapter in this first canto, fifth chapter. Vyasa Deva, after compiling all the Vedic literature, after compiling the Mahabharata, after compiling the other Puranas, Dharmasastras, everything he did, extensive work for the welfare of the human beings, in this Kali Yuga especially, he was dissatisfied, feelings incomplete. So his spiritual master Narada noticed this dissatisfaction and pointed out, this dissatisfaction is because none of the literatures you have compiled, in none of them you have exclusively glorified the Supreme Personality of Godhead Krishna. So Narada instructed Vyasa Deva to compile this Srimad Bhagavatam, which is the only literature, which is containing the exclusivity, exclusive glorification of Krishna and nothing else. So Vyasa Deva compiled the Bhagavatam exactly according to the instructions of Narada, and when he was revising the Bhagavatam, Shukadeva Goswami heard the Bhagavatam from Vyasa Deva. Shukadeva Goswami got attracted to the description of Krishna in the Bhagavatam, so he began inquisitive, who is this personality Krishna? Special personality. Not another person like so many other historical persons, no. He is the absolute truth. He is the personality of Godhead Bhagavan, who is the absolute truth. So that he realized by hearing the Bhagavatam. He was father Vyasa Deva, natural spiritual master. Father is described as the first spiritual master. In the case of Brahmanas especially, father is the first spiritual master. And Vyasa Deva was self-realized himself. He was a great devotee, empowered incarnation. So Vyasa Deva taught the Bhagavatam to Shukadeva Goswami.

---
### Avadhuta, Detachment, Social Customs
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/IrxuFbv_D3Y?t=779
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Those who are spiritually very very very very advanced, not just advanced, they are completely self-realized, but they are disinterested in social customs, they are called Avadhuta."
**Summary:** This segment describes Shukadeva Goswami's complete self-satisfaction after hearing the Bhagavatam, his disregard for formal processes, and his appearance as an Avadhuta, indifferent to social customs and often misunderstood by common people.

Now after hearing the Bhagavatam from Vyasa Deva, Shukadeva Goswami became completely self-satisfied. Nijalabha Tushto, completely satisfied within himself. Thus, he did not depend upon any formal process. He did not depend upon any formal process. The formal process are necessary for those who are expected to reach the stage of complete liberation. But Shukadeva Goswami is already in that status by the grace of his father. So he didn't require it. As a young boy, it is described here, he was dressed as if neglected by others. That means, he did not care to dress himself properly. In fact, he was not even dressed. He was walking around naked. That we will describe a little later in the next verse. So he went about naked, uninterested in social customs, indifferent to everything. That, people following the civilized society, he was disinterested. He was neglected by the general populace. If somebody walks naked, not properly tending the body, not properly dressing themselves, common people will not be able to identify that here is a great personality. No. They will think he is some madman. So they will generally neglect. They will not pay any attention. But, he was surrounded by inquisitive boys and women, less intelligent persons, as if he were a madman. As if he were mad. Why will somebody walk naked? Unless they are mad. So, we should understand. Those who are spiritually very very very very advanced, not just advanced, they are completely self-realized, but they are disinterested in social customs, they are called Avadhuta. Nithyananda was Avadhuta. And Nithyananda Prabhu, the associate of Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, member of the Panchatattva, incarnation of Lord Balarama, he was an Avadhuta, like Shukadeva Goswami was an Avadhuta. Rishabhadeva, he became Avadhuta, later stage of his life, the incarnation of the Supreme Lord. So, Avadhuta means disinterested in all social customs.

---
### Liberated Soul, Supreme Lord, Guidance
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/IrxuFbv_D3Y?t=963
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Even though he may appear to be like simply roaming without any particular aim in life, but actually he is directed by the Lord."
**Summary:** This segment concludes by emphasizing that even though an Avadhuta like Shukadeva Goswami may appear to be roaming aimlessly, he is actually guided by the Supreme Lord within his heart, leading him to fulfill a divine purpose.

So, it is described by Srila Prabhupada here. He thus appears on the scene while traveling on the earth of his own accord. He is freely roaming. But, we should also know, he is directed by the Supreme Lord within his heart. There is a characteristic of a liberated soul. Even though he may appear to be like simply roaming without any particular aim in life, but actually he is directed by the Lord. He is constantly in touch with the Supreme Lord within the heart. So, Supreme Lord directing him. Go and speak Bhagavatam to Pariksit Maharaj. So, he has appeared there. I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

So further description of what is appearance. 26th verse, tvam, sorry, tvam vyashtavarsham sukumarapada karorubahu amsa kapolagatram charu ayataksho charu ayataksha unnasa tulyakarna sabhru ananam kambu sujatakantham. I have split the words because each one is describing a particular feature of the external appearance of Sukhadeva Goswami. So the meaning of these verses, this son of Vyasadeva tam, the previous reference was there, Bhagavan Vyasaputro, the son of Vyasadeva, that of Sukhadeva Goswami, was only 16 years old. Here, Sanskrit, dvi ashta, 2 times 8 is 16. So, he was only 16 years old. His legs, hands, thighs, arms, shoulders, forehead and other parts of his body were all delicately formed. Sukumara, very delicately formed. His eyes were beautifully wide. Beautifully wide eyes he had. His nose and ears were highly raised. Raised nose and raised ears. He had a very attractive face. Subru ananam. Very beautiful eyebrows and very beautiful face, very attractive face. And his neck was well formed and beautiful like a conch shell. Kambu sujata kantham. Kambu means conch shell. Sujata kantham, very beautiful beautifully formed neck. Kantham means neck. So, these beautiful features are special.

### physiognomy, recognition, respect
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/FUyT2SK9T84?t=262
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "A person is honored for his achievements and not for advanced age."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that these features are special and can be recognized by those who understand the science of physiognomy. He quotes Srila Prabhupada, stating that a respectable personality is described from legs to head, and emphasizes that a person is honored for their achievements, not just their age.

If somebody observes him very carefully, not thinking he is a madman, that we described how the members of that assembly, the saintly persons in Pariksit's assembly, some of them, they could recognize a person by studying the name of the person, the name of the person, the name of the person, the name of the person, the name of the person. By studying his external features, that is called the science of physiognomy. It's a special science. The bodily features when observed externally can tell a lot about that personality, the nature of that personality within. Because the person is not the body. But still, great persons by their external bodily features, they are recognized by those who know these specific symptoms by which to recognize such personalities. So, Sri La Pravapa says in the purport, a respectable personality is described beginning with the legs. And this honored system is observed here with Shukadeva Goswami. Always from the bottom to the top. Even the Supreme Lord, we should have darshan of the deity of the Supreme Lord beginning with the feet. And then gradually move up. He was only sixteen years of age. Compared to the other persons in the assembly, probably he was the youngest in that assembly of great saintly persons. So, Shri La Pravapa remarks here, a person is honored for his achievements and not for advanced age. Merely being elderly in terms of physical age is not the basis of honoring somebody. That alone is not the basis.

So, this will be further described in the next two verses. So, the 27th verse is the same as the last. nigūḍha yatrūṃ prututunga vakṣasam āvartanābhim valivalgu udaramcha digambaram vaktravikīrņā kesham pralambabāhum suamara uttama ābham amara uttama The meaning of this verse is, his collarbone was fleshy, his chest broad and thick, his navel deep and his abdomen beautifully striped. His arms were long and curly hair was strewn over his beautiful face. The face was already described in the previous verse, beautiful face. He was naked and the hue of his body reflected that of Lord Krishna. Lord Krishna is very dark complexion. Similarly, Shukadev Goswami was also dark complexion like Krishna.

### physiognomy, uncommon symptoms, Aajanabahu
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/FUyT2SK9T84?t=502
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Distinct features are there by which he can be distinguished as an extraordinary personality, even among great persons."
**Summary:** The speaker quotes Srila Prabhupada's explanation of Shukadeva Goswami's bodily features, highlighting that they indicate him to be different from common men and are typical of greatness according to physiognomical calculations. He gives the example of Aajanabahu (long arms reaching below the knees) as a symptom of a great personality, referencing Lord Ramachandra.

So, Srila Prabhupada explains these bodily features of Shukadev Goswami. In the purport, his bodily features indicate him to be different from common men. Distinct features are there by which he can be distinguished as an extraordinary personality, even among great persons. So, Srila Prabhupada says, all the signs described in connection with the bodily features of Shukadev Goswami are uncommon symptoms. Typical of greatness. Great personalities according to physiognomical calculations. See, there are many branches of Vedic knowledge. One of them is how to recognize a person's greatness by studying his external features. For example, if somebody's arms, when they stretch, when they're standing and they stretch the arms, in front of their body, it goes below their knees. That is called Aajanabahu. Aajanabahu. That's a description of Lord Ramachandra. Lord Ramachandra, when he appeared, he had his hands, when he stretched, it would reach up to his knees or even below his knees. No common personality will have such a description. No common personality will have such arms in a human form, will have his arms reaching up to his knees, it will always be above the knees, you can check, you stretch your arms, it will reach only somewhere in the thigh region, it will not reach up to your knees, unless you are a great personality.

### physiognomy, Vedic knowledge, youth

He then discusses verse 28, noting Shukadeva Goswami's blackish complexion, beauty due to his youth, and how he was pleasing to women despite trying to conceal his glories.

So, one of the symptoms is of a great personality, the arms will reach up to the knees. That's described about Shukadev Goswami here. Praalambabahum. Elongated hands. So, these symptoms are described in a branch of the Vedas called physiognomy physiognomy this probably is not known in the modern education system this physiognomy as a science there are definite symptoms so it is described his bodily hue resembled that of lord krishna who is the supreme among the gods so amara means the demigods and means the supreme among the demigods who's that krishna so his bodily hue was that of krishna the 28th verse the meaning of this 28th verse is he was blackish shamam and very beautiful due to his youth very beautiful due to his youth because of the glamour of his body and his attractive smiles he was pleasing to women though he tried to cover his natural glories

---

The great sages present there were all expert in the art of physiognomy and so they honored him by rising from their seats they were all seated parikshit maharaj received the great saintly persons who arrived from all over the universe they arrived before shukadeva swami came to that assembly so they were all seated and parikshit maharaj bowed down to them and and acknowledged their presence glorified them but now they were standing up to receive shukadeva swami who could not be recognized by common people who taught him some mad man they neglected him and he was surrounded by some urchins and some foolish people but he is a madman but not these sages who knew the art of physiognomy they recognized him him as a very very very great personality simply by seeing his bodily features so that is the description here that Lakshanagya the sages very very great personalities from the upper planets of the universe they have arrived there so they could recognize even though gudavarchasam his glories were covered externally common people cannot make out he is a great person and that too when he is walking naked you will think he is some great personality so these sages could recognize so they all stood up out of respect for Shukadeva swamy so I will stop here thank you very much

## SB 1.19.29-31 | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | 30-05-2023 | Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### respect, reception, humility
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/flED_YKikRk?t=31
**Verse References:** SB 1.19.29
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Maharaj Pariksit was very glad to receive a great devotee of the Lord, and he bowed down before Shukadeva Goswami with all the limbs of his body."
**Summary:** This segment describes Maharaj Pariksit's respectful reception of Shukadeva Goswami, including his bowing down and the reaction of the surrounding people.

Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare, Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya, Narayanam Namaskritya, Naram Jai Vanarottamam, Devim Sarasvatim Vyasam Tato Jayam Udhirayet. So, in this session, we'll be discussing three more verses, the 29th, the 30th, and the 31st verses of the 19th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. These set of verses are describing the appearance of Shukadeva Goswami, and so far we have heard the sages in that assembly, they stood up out of respect for Shukadeva Goswami, recognizing him to be a very great personality. What was Pariksit's reaction? That we are going to hear now. 29th verse. Sravishnurato atithaya agataya tasmaisa pariyam shirasajahara tato nivrttahi abudhastriyo arbhaka mahasanesopavishesha pujitaha. Sorry. mahasanesopavishesha pujitaha. The meaning of this verse is, Maharaj Pariksit, who is also known as Vishnurata, Vishnurata means one who is protected always by Lord Vishnu, he bowed his head to receive the chief guest, Shukadeva Goswami. At that time, all the ignorant women and boys ceased following Srila Shukadeva. He was surrounded by some urchins and some foolish women, so all of them became alerted that here is a great personality, he is not a madman, so then they stopped following him, they just disappeared from there. Receiving respect from all, Shukadeva Goswami took his exalted seat. It's in the purport Srila Prabhupada explains very nicely.

---
### respect, spiritual master, etiquette
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/flED_YKikRk?t=169
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Shukadeva Goswami knew how to exchange greetings, depending on the different person's position or achievements."
**Summary:** This segment elaborates on the proper etiquette and reasons behind the different ways Shukadeva Goswami and others offered respects, highlighting the roles of spiritual masters and advanced devotees.

On Shukadeva Goswami's arrival at the meeting, everyone except Srila Vyasadeva, Narada and a few others stood up. See, it was described, great sages, because of physiognomy, they could recognize Shukadeva Goswami, even though he is not properly dressed by his external bodily features, so they stood up out of respect. But not Vyasadeva Narada. Why? Because Vyasadeva is Shukadeva Goswami's own spiritual master. Narada is the spiritual master of the spiritual master. Narada is Vyasadeva's spiritual master. So Vyasadeva and Narada did not stand up, and few others who were great personalities, they did not stand up. But everybody else stood up out of respect for Shukadeva Goswami. Now, what about Maharaj Pariksit? It is described here. Maharaj Pariksit was very glad to receive a great devotee of the Lord, and he bowed down before Shukadeva Goswami with all the limbs of his body. Dandavat, dandavat pranama, or sastanga pranama, different ways this is said. So here it is described that he bowed down with all his limbs of his body. Saparyam shirasam ajaharao. With all the limbs of his body, he bowed down completely to Shukadeva Goswami. Shukadeva Goswami also exchanged the greetings and reception by embrace, shaking of hands, nodding and bowing down, especially before his father and Narada Muni. Shukadeva Goswami knew how to exchange greetings, depending on the different person's position or achievements. So with some equals, he was shaking hands. With juniors, he was embracing them. And acknowledging some people's greetings by nodding his head. And especially to his father Vyasadeva and Narada Muni, he bowed down himself, because he is a junior. Thus, Shukadeva Goswami was offered the presidential seat at the meeting, because he was the greatest, realized, advanced, spiritually advanced personality of his time, recognized by all other great persons. So he was offered the seat of the president of the assembly, the chief person in that assembly of Parikshit Maharaj. When he was so received by the kings and sages, the street boys and less intelligent women who were following him were struck with wonder and fear. So they retired from their frivolous activities. Everything was full of gravity and calm. You see how the whole atmosphere changed completely after the arrival of Shukadeva Goswami. The 30th verse.

---
### glory, devotee, comparison
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/flED_YKikRk?t=383
**Verse References:** SB 1.19.30
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Shukadeva Goswami's appearance there in the assembly of Parikshit Maharaj is very, very, very special."
**Summary:** This segment describes Shukadeva Goswami's glorious appearance in the assembly, comparing it to the moon surrounded by stars and planets, emphasizing his special position among other great personalities.

The meaning of this 30th verse is. The meaning of this 30th verse is. Shukadeva Goswami was then surrounded. Shukadeva Goswami was then surrounded by saintly sages and demigods, just as the moon is surrounded by stars, planets and other heavenly bodies. His presence was gorgeous and he was respected by all. So Shukadeva Goswami's appearance there in the assembly of Parikshit Maharaj is very, very, very special. Because in that assembly, Parikshit Maharaj himself is a pure devotee, the greatest Rajarshi of his time. And other Rajarshis, Devarishis, Brahmarishis, Arunodayas, they all arrived from different parts of the universe to witness Parikshit Maharaj quitting his body. And other Rajarshis, Devarishis, Brahmarishis, Arunodayas, they all arrived from different parts of the universe to witness Parikshit Maharaj quitting his body. So, in that great assembly, how was Sukhadeva Goswami appearing? Like the moon is surrounded by stars, planets and other heavenly bodies. This is a natural beauty in the sky at night, especially on a full moon night. How does it look? The moon will be shining the brightest among all the other heavenly bodies in the sky as seen from the earth at night. But the other heavenly bodies will be twinkling like stars, planets and other heavenly bodies. Moon will outshine all others, all other heavenly bodies, particularly on a full moon night. So, that is the beauty, natural beauty. So, this presence, the presence of Sukhadeva Goswami surrounded by all varieties of saintly persons, all great personalities is compared to this moon in the night sky, full moon surrounded by stars, planets and other heavenly bodies.

---
### devotee, respect, worship
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/flED_YKikRk?t=550
**Verse References:** SB 1.19.30, SB 11
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The devotee of the Lord is more honored in the world than the Lord himself."
**Summary:** This segment explains that a devotee of the Lord is often more honored than the Lord Himself, quoting Krishna's instruction to Uddhava that His pure devotee should be respected more than Him.

So, in the purport, Srila Prabhupada says, In the great assembly of saintly persons, there was Vyasadeva, the Brahmarshi, Narada, the Devarishi, Parashurama, the great ruler of Kshatriya kings, etc., Rajarishi. Some of them were powerful incarnations of the Lord. Parashurama was an incarnation of the Supreme Lord. Vyasadeva was an incarnation. Narada was an empowered incarnation. Sukhadeva Goswami was not known as Brahmarshi, Rajarishi, Devarishi, nor was he known as an incarnation, like Narada, Vyasa or Parashurama. Yet, he excelled all of them in respects paid. There is something very, very important to note. Sukhadeva Goswami, in spite of not having a formal position like all the many other personalities in the assembly, great personalities, he was offered more respect than all of them. Why? Srila Prabhupada explains, This means that the devotee of the Lord is more honored in the world than the Lord himself. So, Srila Prabhupada many times quotes another verse in the Bhagavatam. Krishna, while instructing Uddhava in the 11th canto of the Bhagavatam, he says to Uddhava, Madbhakta Pooja Abhyadhika. What does it mean? Krishna says, My pure devotee, who is very, very, very dear to me, he should be respected more than me by the others. Madbhakta Pooja Abhyadhika. He should be worshipped with greater respect than me. Krishna is the topmost worshipable personality among all worshipable personalities in this whole universe. But it is Krishna's own choice that he would be more pleased if instead of worshipping Krishna, we worship Krishna's pure devotee. So, therefore, Prabhupada concludes this purport by telling, One should therefore never minimize the importance of a devotee like Shukadeva Goswami.

---
### humility, inquiry, spiritual master
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/flED_YKikRk?t=745
**Verse References:** SB 1.19.31
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "One should humbly approach a spiritual master to understand the transcendental signs."
**Summary:** This segment discusses Shukadeva Goswami's readiness to answer questions and Pariksit Maharaj's humble approach to inquire from him, emphasizing the importance of approaching a bona fide spiritual master with humility.

31st verse. Prasantham asinam akuntha medasam munim rupo bhagavato abhyupetya pranamya murdha avahita avahita tutanjalir natwagira sunruta anvaprichat. So, the meaning of this 31st verse is The sage Shri Shukadeva Goswami sat perfectly pacified, intelligent and ready to answer any question without hesitation. Shukadeva Goswami knew by the indication of the Paramatma within his heart what is he supposed to do in that assembly. Even without Pariksit speaking, before Pariksit could even speak anything, he knew why he was there, what was his purpose in being brought there by the Supreme Lord, and what was he supposed to do in that assembly, everything he knew. So, it is described, he sat perfectly pacified. He was the most intelligent personality among all the persons assembled there. And therefore, he was ready to answer any question without hesitation. He was fully knowledgeable, fully self-realized, greatest authority. So, he was ready to answer any question. Because, already we have heard, Shukadeva Goswami, Pariksit Maharaj asked two questions from the assembled saintly persons. So, Prabhupada says further, The great devotee Maharaj Pariksit approached him, offered his respects by bowing before him, and politely inquired with sweet words and folded hands. Pariksit inquired from him. So, what Pariksit, what inquiry did he make of Shukadeva Goswami will be described in the later verses. But, Prabhupada says in the purport, The gesture adopted by Maharaj Pariksit of questioning a master is befitting in terms of scriptural directions. What do scriptural directions say? One should humbly approach a spiritual master to understand the transcendental signs. One must approach a bona fide spiritual master. So, Pariksit Maharaj is already actually fully aware what he is supposed to do. He has already told assembled saintly persons, kindly sing the glories of Vishnu, so that I can fix my mind on the lotus feet of the Supreme Lord before I quit my body. But, when Shukadeva Goswami came, as a matter of etiquette, he approached Shukadeva Goswami with folded hands and actually began to inquire about the transcendental signs, assuming humble position. So, I will stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

---

## SB 1.19.32 | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | 02-06-2023 | Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### devotee, purification, Krishna consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/-CQRH7GQTdI?t=53
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The spiritual potency of a pure devotee who is completely immersed in Krishna consciousness is so great, simply by remembering him, one's house becomes completely sanctified instantaneously."
**Summary:** Pariksit Maharaj tells Shukadeva Goswami that remembering a pure devotee in Krishna consciousness sanctifies one's house, and seeing, touching, and serving them brings even greater purification.

In this session, we will be discussing the 33rd verse of the 19th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam, येशाम सम्स्मरनात पुम्साम, सद्यशुद्यन्तिवैगृहाह, किम्पुनर्दर्शनस्पर्ष, पादशौच आसनादिपिही. The meaning of this verse is, simply by our remembering you, our houses become instantly sanctified, and what to speak of seeing you, touching you, washing your holy feet, and offering you a seat in our home. This is Pariksit speaking to Shukadeva Goswami, addressing Shukadeva Goswami, that the spiritual potency of a pure devotee who is completely immersed in Krishna consciousness is so great, simply by remembering him, one's house becomes, one's surroundings, one's place becomes completely sanctified instantaneously. And then if one is more fortunate, one is able to not only remember him, but even see him, touch his holy feet, wash his feet, and give him a seat of honor, and serve him by hearing from him, worshipping him, taking his instructions, etc.

---
### holy places, sin, purification
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/-CQRH7GQTdI?t=184
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Sinful persons go to holy places and leave their sins there to accumulate, but the presence of the great saints in such holy places disinfects the accumulated sins."
**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada explains that holy places are important because of the presence of great sages and saints, who disinfect the sins left behind by ordinary people who visit those places to purify themselves.

So, Shri La Prabhupada explains in the purport, the importance of holy places of pilgrimage is due to the presence of great sages and saints. Why is a place called a holy place of pilgrimage? It is primarily because in that place great saintly persons are present. And by their presence, what happens to that holy place? That's explained by Shri La Prabhupada in the next sentence in this purport. Prabhupada says, it is said that sinful persons go to holy places and leave their sins there to accumulate. Anybody goes to a holy place, ordinary people, they will take a bath there, and they will leave behind their sinful reactions. They get purified. They get purified means their sinful reactions are washed away by going there and taking a bath in the holy place. But the presence of the great saints in such holy places disinfects the accumulated sins. Now so many people go to holy places, ordinary people, big numbers, they go, and they leave behind all their sinful reactions. Now those sinful reactions become a burden, a big burden in the holy place. But that burden is cleared away by the presence, mere presence, of the saintly persons like Shukadeva Goswami. And because of the presence of the saintly persons in such holy places, the holy places continue to remain sanctified by the grace of the devotees and saints present there. See this is not known to many people. This is explained in another place in the Bhagavatam. So Prabhupada is quoting from that portion of the Bhagavatam, that a holy place becomes burdened by sinful reactions of those ordinary people who visit the holy place and wash away their sinful reactions. But such accumulated sin is cleansed away by the presence of saintly persons.

---
### saints, householders, purification
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/-CQRH7GQTdI?t=345
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "The only aim of such saints is to sanctify the houses of the householders, to purify their house, to make their house free from sinful reactions, so that it will benefit them to practice spiritual life and make spiritual advancement."
**Summary:** Srila Prabhupada explains that when saints visit the homes of worldly people, they neutralize accumulated sins, and genuine saints have no self-interest but aim to purify the householders' homes to benefit their spiritual lives.

Now further Srila Prabhupada says, if such saints appear in the homes of worldly people, certainly the accumulated sins of such worldly enjoyers becomes neutralized. Home also, people are staying, ordinary people stay in their home. But generally they are inclined to enjoy materially, and thereby accumulate some sinful reactions. Now, if such a home is visited by a saintly person like Shukadeva Goswami, then again the home becomes completely purified. Home becomes completely purified. That's what Parishad Maharaj is telling Shukadeva Goswami. Therefore, Prabhupada says further, the holy saints actually have no self-interest with the householders. That means when a saintly person visits a home of an ordinary person, a worldly person, then the saint has nothing to get for himself from the householder. Don't mistakenly think that when saints come to your house, genuine saints, I'm talking about genuine saints, they come to your house, it's not that they want some bhiksha, they want some benefit from you, they want to take something from you. No. It's never. They have no self-interest. The only aim of such saints is to sanctify the houses of the householders. Only aim, to purify their house, to make their house free from sinful reactions, so that it will benefit them to practice spiritual life and make spiritual advancement.

---
### gratitude, offense, saints
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/-CQRH7GQTdI?t=485
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "A householder who dishonors such holy orders is a great offender."
**Summary:** Householders should be grateful when saints appear at their doors, welcome them, and take spiritual benefit from their presence; dishonoring such holy orders is a great offense, requiring fasting to neutralize it.

Therefore, Srila Prabhupada says further, the householders should feel grateful when such saints and sages appear at their doors. So when a saintly person comes to the door of a householder, the householder should welcome them and take spiritual benefit from their presence. A householder who dishonors such holy orders is a great offender. It's an offense to not honor such a saintly person. It is enjoined, therefore, that a householder who does not bow down before a saint at once must undergo fasting for the day in order to neutralize the great offense.

---
### body, self, ignorance
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/-CQRH7GQTdI?t=550
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "A human being who identifies this material body with the self... is no better than an animal, like an ass or a cow."
**Summary:** Krishna speaks strongly about those who identify the material body as the self and go to holy places merely to wash away sins for material enjoyment, considering them no better than animals.

So, the Bhagavatam says in the 10th canto... These are very strong words, strongly worded words. They are spoken by Krishna in the 10th canto, in an assembly of saintly persons at Kurukshetra, when there was a solar eclipse. There was a gathering of saintly persons on that occasion, and Krishna was speaking to the saintly persons. Krishna had gone there as the king of Dwaraka. Krishna is playing that role in his pastimes, in his Leela, Dwaraka, the king of Dwaraka. So, Krishna is telling, a human being who identifies this body, this material body made up of three elements, with the self, Yasya Atma Buddhi. Who am I? Actually, most people think, I am this body, this body is who I am, is myself. But Shastra says no, scripture says no, Bhagavad Gita says no, this body is not you, you are not the body, you are spirit, soul. So, what if a person, in ignorance, mistaken identity, that I am this body, and what is this body made of? It is described here, Kunape, Tridhatuke. Kunape means a bag consisting of three dhatus, three elements. What are the three elements? According to Ayurveda, this body is made up of Kapha, Vata, and Pitta. Kapha is mucus, Vata is air, and Pitta is bile. So, somebody who thinks this bag is... called the physical body, which consists of three elements, somebody thinks this has the self. And further, Swadhi, Kalatra, Deishu, who considers the byproducts of the body to be his kinsman, that is, my wife, my children, they are all mine. Who considers his land of birth... And he thinks, such a person, a materialistic person, an ignorant person, a foolish person, a person under the influence of the illusory energy, thinks that one should go to a place of pilgrimage simply to take a bath, so that if all my sins are washed away, I don't have to suffer any sinful reaction. I can enjoy without any hindrance. If this attitude a person has, such a person is considered Saha eva Goh Kharaha. He is no better than an animal, like an ass or a cow. Kharaha means ass, and Goh means cow. That means he is not a human being with developed intelligence. He is acting on the platform of the animals, on the bodily platform.

---
### spiritual platform, human life, liberation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/-CQRH7GQTdI?t=813
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Human life gives us an opportunity to rise to the spiritual platform and act on the spiritual platform to gain complete freedom from material bondage and go back to Godhead."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes that human life is meant for rising to the spiritual platform, not remaining on the bodily or material platform, to achieve freedom from material bondage and return to Godhead.

Everywhere in the scriptures this is said. Human beings should not remain on the bodily platform, on the material platform, on the platform of material enjoyment. No. Human life gives us an opportunity to rise to the spiritual platform and act on the spiritual platform to gain complete freedom from material bondage and go back to Godhead.

## SB 1.19.32| HH Stoka Krishna Swami | 30-05-2023 | Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### Shukadeva Goswami, Transcendentalism, Respect
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9_s9iM5RQEk?t=34
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The appearance of Shukadeva Goswami was certainly a very, very divine event that he was sent by Krishna."
**Summary:** This segment introduces Shukadeva Goswami's divine appearance, his purpose in speaking Srimad Bhagavatam to Pariksit Maharaj, and the extraordinary respect he received from assembled transcendentalists due to his advanced spiritual status.

Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya Narayanam Namaskritya Naram Chai Vanarottamam Devi Saraswati Vyasam Tato Jayam Udi Rayet. In this session, we will be discussing from the 32nd verse of the 19th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. Just to refresh your memory, this chapter is entitled The Appearance of Shukadeva Goswami. In this chapter, it will be described how Shukadeva Goswami and Pariksit Maharaj met at a time when Pariksit was actually prepared to quit his body after having renounced his kingdom. He was in a holy place on the bank of the river Ganga, and he was surrounded by so many saintly persons from all over the universe. Exactly when Pariksit was inquiring from the sages assembled there that kindly tell me what is my duty, exactly at that time Shukadeva Goswami walks into that assembly, and the description has already been given in the previous few verses. The appearance of Shukadeva Goswami was certainly a very divine event that he was sent by Krishna, and he was sent to speak Srimad Bhagavatam to Pariksit Maharaj for the first time after it was compiled by Vyasadeva under instruction of Shri Narada. So this 32nd verse now, Pariksit is addressing Shukadeva Goswami after Shukadeva Goswami has been welcomed, he has taken the seat, the seat of honor, and when everyone has settled down acknowledging Shukadeva Goswami's most exalted position is the top most among the transcendentalists of his time even though he is just 16 years old and he doesn't formally belong to any category of transcendentalists as Brahmarshi or Rajarshi or Devershi or Arunodaya. He is not formally initiated, he has not undergone any reformatory process samskara. Yampravra jantam anupetam apetakrutyam in the beginning of the Bhagavatam in the first canto in the same canto second chapter, Suta Goswami offers respects to his spiritual master Shukadeva Goswami and he says my spiritual master, he was a liberated soul by birth, he did not undergo any reformation after his birth because he already liberated. So that Shukadeva Goswami was recognized by the great transcendentalists rishis who had arrived from higher planets. They knew the art of physiognomy. That means by looking at the external features, bodily features, face symptoms, the build up, they could say here is some very advanced transcendentalist. So they all stood up in respect except Vyasa Deva and Narada Muni. Vyasa Deva was the father of Shukadeva Goswami, Shukadeva Goswami's natural spiritual master, and Narada was the spiritual master of the spiritual master Jick Riva swan the spiritual master of Vyasa Deva so they did not stand up and few others, Parashurama the incarnation of the supreme personality of Godhead. So they did not stand but everybody else stood up in respect and Parikshit Maharaj was very glad to receive him now what is Parikshit telling Shukadeva Goswami.

---
### Detachment, Materialism, Spiritual Advancement
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9_s9iM5RQEk?t=582
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "For a devotee who desires to go back to Godhead, two things are strictly prohibited, worldly enjoyers and women."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the principle that serious transcendentalists avoid contact with worldly enjoyers and those interested in sense gratification, using the example of Chaitanya Mahaprabhu refusing to meet King Pratap Rudra to illustrate the dangers of such association for spiritual progress.

So, it is also true that great saintly persons who are completely aloof from worldly enjoyers, they do not bother to meet or contact or approach any of the materialistic persons or people in royal position, big big kings, big big wealthy merchants, people in big positions in material estimation, they never approach. So, Srila Prabhupada next gives an example. Maharaja Pratap Rudra was a follower of Lord Chaitanya. He was the king of Orissa. But when Maharaja Pratap Rudra wanted to see the Lord, see Lord Chaitanya, Lord Chaitanya refused to see him because he was a king. So, Pratap Rudra, even though he was a king, he was a saintly king. So, he was seeking an interview with Chaitanya Mahaprabhu just to get blessings, just to get some instructions, personal instructions, when Chaitanya Mahaprabhu was staying in Puri. So, Pratap Rudra, who would not give any appointment, would not agree to meet Pratap Rudra Maharaj, why? Why would Chaitanya refuse? Prabhupada says, for a devotee who desires to go back to Godhead, two things are strictly prohibited, worldly enjoyers and women. Now, this is based on a verse that is there in the Chaitanya Charitamrita. That verse says, Nishkinchanasya Bhagavad Bhajanon Mukhasya Paramparam Jigamishor Bhavasagarasya Sandarshanam Vishayinam Atha Yoshitamcha Hahantahantavishabhakshanatopyasadhu. The meaning of this verse is, It is more abominable than drinking poison willingly. So, as Prabhupada says in this purport, two things are strictly forbidden for a serious transcendentalist who is practicing spiritual life in order to get out of material existence entirely and go back to Godhead. So, this verse says, this is a verse quoted by, spoken by Chaitanya Mahaprabhu. When Chaitanya Mahaprabhu was requested by one of his associates, Sarvam Bhattacharya, that please give an appointment for Maharaj Pratap Rudra who wants to come and see you, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu said, no, I cannot give an appointment. Why? This is the reason he is quoting. Nishkinchanasya, for one who is completely detached himself from material enjoyment, Bhagavad Bhajanon Mukhasya. And, he is engaged, eager to be engaged in serving the Supreme Personality of Godhead. For him, and also, Paramparam Jigamishor Bhavasagarasya. He wants to cross over the ocean of material existence and go back to Godhead. For such a person, Sandarshanam, just seeing, not even mixing or interacting, not even interacting very closely. No, just seeing. Sandarshanam. Two categories of people. Vishayinam, Yoshitam. Vishayinam means those who are engaged in materialistic activities. Vishayinam. Sense enjoyers in this world. And, Yoshitam means women. Women here refers to a member of the opposite gender who is very much interested in enjoying. For a woman, it is man. Yoshit is man. For a man, a Yoshit is woman who is interested in enjoying. Such a Darshan, just a Sandarshanam, just seeing, is very, very dangerous. How dangerous? More dangerous than drinking poison willingly, than knowingly drinking poison. That means, it is very very dangerous. It is a big big big obstacle for spiritual advancement. Therefore, such a transcendentalist, will never agree to see or meet or interact or mix with such worldly minded people.

---

More dangerous than drinking poison willingly, than knowingly drinking poison. That means, it is very, very dangerous. It is a big, big, big obstacle for spiritual advancement.
