## SB 1.19.35-36 | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | 04-06-2023 | Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### Krishna's relationship with devotees, family relationships, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/gLgxrCFkVVs?t=34
**Verse References:** SB 1.19.35
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So Krishna is the supreme personality of Godhead, but when he appears in this world, he definitely does take his birth, even though he is unborn."
**Summary:** This segment introduces verse 35, explaining Krishna's familial relationship with the Pandavas and their devotion to Him.

Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya Narayanam Namaskritya Naram Chai Vanarottamam Devim Sarasvatim Vyasam Tato Jayam Udi Rayet. In this session we will be discussing from two verses, the thirty-fifth and the thirty-sixth verses of the nineteenth chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. The thirty-fifth verse is as follows: Apime Bhagavan Preetaha Krishna Pandu Sutapriyaha Paitrishvaseya Preetyartham Tadgotrasyathabandhavaha. The meaning of this verse is, Lord Krishna, the personality of Godhead, who is very dear to the sons of King Pandu, has accepted me as one of those relatives just to please his great cousins and brothers. This is what Parikshit has spoken. This is what Parikshit has spoken while addressing Shukadeva Goswami. Parikshit is particularly relating the close connection between his grandparents, Arjuna and the other Pandavas, their connection with Krishna in a family relationship. So Krishna is the supreme personality of Godhead, but when he appears in this world, he definitely does take his birth, even though he is unborn. He takes his birth as the son of Devaki and Vasudeva. Now Vasudeva is a Kshatriya and Vasudeva's sister, Kunti, her sons are the Pandavas. So there is a family relationship between Krishna, the supreme personality of Godhead and the Pandavas, their cousins. Not only that, the Pandavas are great devotees of Krishna, pure devotees of Krishna. So, given these facts, Prabhupada explains the significance of this particular statement by Parikshit Maharaj.

---
### devotion, family, protection
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/gLgxrCFkVVs?t=251
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The Lord gives special protection to the family members and descendants of a devotee, even though such members are themselves non-devotees."
**Summary:** This segment focuses on Prabhupada's explanation of how pure devotees serve their families better and how Krishna protects the families of devotees, even non-devotees.

In the Purport Shila, Prabhupada says, a pure and exclusive devotee of the Lord serves his family interest more dexterously than others who are attached to illusory family affairs. Pure devotees are never under illusion. But commonly, ordinary people, they are completely under illusion, because their relationships are based on, I am this body, and therefore, all bodily connections are mine. My wife, my children, my relatives, my brothers, my cousins, my aunt. You see, totally based on illusory family affairs. Whereas the pure devotees, the Pandavas, they know that Krishna is related to them, but independent of that relationship, they are pure devotees of Krishna. You see. So that makes a big difference. So Prabhupada says, generally people are attached to family matters, and the whole economic impetus of human society is moving under the influence of family affection. Such deluded persons have no information that one can render better service to the family by becoming a devotee of the Lord. People think that if I become a devotee, I have to cut off all my relationships with my family members and completely forget them, as if I have nothing to do with them. That's not correct. That understanding is not correct. Why? Because, Prabhupada says, the Lord gives special protection to the family members and descendants of a devotee, even though such members are themselves non-devotees. Of course, in the case of Pandavas, they were all devotees. Pandu was a devotee. Kunti was a devotee. Kunti was a devotee.

---
### devotion, protection, family
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/gLgxrCFkVVs?t=490
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna personally saved this Pariksit when he was in the womb of his mother, because this Pariksit was the only surviving descendant of the entire Pandava family."
**Summary:** This segment continues the discussion on Krishna's protection of devotees' families, using the examples of Hiranyakashipu and Prahlad, and Parikshit's protection in the womb.

Subhadra was a devotee. Draupadi was a devotee. Everyone was a devotee. But, here is an example. Maharaj Pariksit was a great devotee of the Lord. But his father, Hiranyakashipu, was a great atheist, and declared enemy of the Lord. Even though the Lord is nobody's enemy, but these atheists and non-devotees, they consider Vishnu or Krishna as their enemy. Despite all this, Hiranyakashipu was awarded salvation, salvation means liberation, due to his being the father of Maharaj Prahlad. Krishna is so kind. Hiranyakashipu does not deserve to get liberation. He is an atheist. But, just because he is the father of a great devotee, Prahlad, the Lord gave liberation to this Hiranyakashipu. So, Srila Prabhupada says further, the Lord is so kind that he gives all protection to the family members of his devotee, and thus the devotee has no need to bother about his family members, even if one leaves such family members aside to discharge devotional service. Generally, when somebody dedicates oneself to serving the mission of the Lord, they may have to give up the family responsibility. Then, is there a loss for the family members? That somebody is giving up their responsibility and not discharging family duties, duties towards family members? No. Because Krishna takes care. Maharaj Yudhishthira and his brothers were the sons of Kunti, the paternal aunt of Lord Krishna. And Maharaj Pariksit admits the patronage of Lord Krishna because of his being the only grandson of the great Pandavas. Krishna personally saved this Pariksit when he was in the womb of his mother, because this Pariksit was the only surviving descendant of the entire Pandava family.

---
### spiritual master, Krishna's arrangement, devotional service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/gLgxrCFkVVs?t=559
**Verse References:** SB 1.19.36
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna arranged, Pariksit should hear Bhagavatam, so that Pariksit is benefited, by Shukadeva Goswami speaking the Bhagavatam, and not only Pariksit, through Pariksit, many many millions of people, and all future generations of people, are benefited."
**Summary:** This segment introduces verse 36 and discusses Shukadeva Goswami's appearance before Parikshit, emphasizing Krishna's arrangement for delivering the Srimad Bhagavatam and the role of the spiritual master.

The 36th verse, Anyathate avyakta-gathir darshanam na katham drinam nitharam mriyamananam samsiddhasya vaniyasah. The meaning of this 36th verse is, otherwise, without being inspired by Lord Krishna, how is it that you have voluntarily appeared here, though you are moving incognito, incognito means unrecognized, to the common man, and are not visible to us for on the verge of death. Shukadeva Goswami was just roaming around, as if he has no aim in life, he has nothing to do with society, he is not having any proper social appearance, he was roaming around naked, he appeared like a madman, and he did not bother about what people thought, what people said, the way people reacted, he never bothered about it, he was completely absorbed within himself in Krishna Consciousness. So, such a paramahamsa, who cannot be recognized by most people, and he himself has come, voluntarily, he has walked into that assembly, and Pariksit is able to recognize him, because of, of course, Pariksit can recognize Shukadeva Goswami, because Pariksit himself is a pure devotee, it takes a pure devotee to recognize another pure devotee, but there is another way, that is, great saintly persons, who are recognized spiritual authorities, they certify that here is a pure devotee, because when Shukadeva Goswami walked into that assembly of great saintly persons, they immediately recognized him, and stood up in respect, and welcomed him with folded hands, into that assembly, and he was offered the seat of honor, the presidential seat in that assembly, by all present. So, Pariksit Maharaj is telling that I am so fortunate that you have come at the time when I am about to die, so I can get your instructions, I can get your advice. So in the purport, Srila Prabhupada says, the great sage Shukadeva Goswami was certainly inspired by Lord Krishna to appear voluntarily before Maharaj Pariksit, the great devotee of the Lord, just to give him the teachings of Srimad Bhagavatam. Krishna arranged, Pariksit should hear Bhagavatam, so that Pariksit is benefited, by Shukadeva Goswami speaking the Bhagavatam, and not only Pariksit, through Pariksit, many many millions of people, and all future generations of people, are benefited. So, Prabhupada says,

---
### spiritual master, Krishna's mercy, devotional service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/gLgxrCFkVVs?t=774
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "To get the assistance and help of a bona fide spiritual master means to receive the direct help of the Lord himself."
**Summary:** This segment emphasizes the importance of the spiritual master as a representative of Krishna, highlighting that receiving help from a bona fide spiritual master is equivalent to receiving direct help from the Lord.

One can achieve the nucleus of the devotional service of the Lord by the mercy of the spiritual master and the personality of Godhead. This is what Chaitanya Mahaprabhu says, in his teachings, Guru Krishna Kripaaye Paaye Bhakti Lata Bheech. The spiritual master is the manifestation or manifested representative of the Lord to help one achieve ultimate success. Nothing is disconnected in the life of a devotee. Everything is in relationship with Krishna. One who is not authorized by the Lord cannot become a spiritual master. It's not that somebody is a very learned scholar, therefore he is Guru. Or somebody has got some mystic, magical, mystic powers, and therefore he is Guru. No. None of these things are qualifications. They may have mystic power, they may not have mystic power. They may be very learned, they may not be learned. Formally I mean. Formally no learning. But important point is, the spiritual master is necessarily authorized by the supreme personality of Godhead. And he acts as the representative of the Supreme Lord. So, Srila Shukadeva Goswami is an authorized spiritual master. And thus he was inspired by the Lord to appear before Maharaj Parichar, and instruct him in the teachings of the Srimad Bhagavatam. One can achieve the ultimate success of going back to Godhead if he is favored by the Lord's sending of the spiritual master. So, one can achieve the ultimate success of going back to Godhead, if he is favored by the Lord's sending. So, the conclusion is, that to get the assistance and help of a bona fide spiritual master means to receive the direct help of the Lord himself. So all of us, who have got Srila Prabhupada as our spiritual master, we are directly getting the help of Krishna himself. This is what we should understand and realize, and accept, and take advantage. I'll stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

---

## SB 1.19.37-38 | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | 05-06-2023 | Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### invocation, prayer
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/gGr8vctXBOg?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Om Agyana Timiraandhasya Gyananjanishalakaya Chaksharunmilitam Yena Tasmai Shri Gurave Namaha"
**Summary:** The lecture begins with traditional Vaishnava prayers and mantras, invoking blessings and paying respects to the spiritual master and Krishna.

Om Agyana Timiraandhasya Gyananjanishalakaya Chaksharunmilitam Yena Tasmai Shri Gurave Namaha. Namah Om Vishnupadaya Krishna Preshtaya Bhutale Shri Mate Bhakti Vedanta Swamin Nithinamine. Namaste Saraswate Deve Gauravani Pracharine Nirvishesha Shunyavadi Pashchatya Deshatarine. Jaya Shri Krishna Chaitanya Prabhu Nithyananda Shri Advaita Gadadhara Shri Vasadi Gauravakta Vrinda. Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare. Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya. Narayanam Namaskritya Naram Jai Manarottamam Deveem Saraswateem Vyasam Tato Jaya Mudhirayet.

---
### death, spiritual master, perfection
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/gGr8vctXBOg?t=49
**Verse References:** SB 1.19.37
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "You are the spiritual master of great saints and devotees, Yoginam Paramam Gurum."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the verses to be discussed, focusing on Parikshit Maharaj's question to Shukadeva Goswami about the path to perfection, especially for one nearing death.

In this session we will be discussing from two verses, that is 37th and the 38th verses of the 19th chapter of the first canto of Shri Madbhagavatam. The 37th verse is as follows: Ataprishami Samsiddhim Yoginam Paramam Gurum Purushasyehayatkaryam Priyamanasya Sarvatha. Now Parikshit is asking from Shukadeva Goswami a very very specific question. What is that question? That is a translation of this verse, the 37th verse. You are the spiritual master of great saints and devotees, Yoginam Paramam Gurum. The same thing was said by Suta Goswami at the beginning of the Bhagavata. Suta Goswami offered prayers to Shukadeva Goswami and he said to him, This is the question that was asked to Shukadeva Goswami before he began speaking Bhagavatam to the sages of Naimisharanya. There he described that Shukadeva Goswami is the spiritual master of great saints and devotees. So Parikshit is telling, I am therefore begging you to show the way of perfection for all persons and especially for one who is about to die, Purushasyeha yatkaryam mriyam anasya sarvatha. mriyam anasya means one who is about to die. His own position, He is going to meet with death in seven days. So he is preparing for quitting the body, but he is preparing by asking the greatest of the transcendentalists, the spiritual master of great saints and devotees. He is asking such a personality, kindly show the way of perfection for everyone including me who is about to die.

---
### spiritual master, material world, Krishna consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/gGr8vctXBOg?t=183
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "A spiritual master is only meant for one thing in everyone's life: He is meant for showing the path of perfection for everyone in this material world who is entangled in the material existence."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on Prabhupada's explanation of the verse, emphasizing the role of the spiritual master in guiding individuals towards perfection and highlighting Krishna's purpose in creating the material world for those who desire to enjoy independently.

So in the purport Prabhupada explains, Unless one is perfectly anxious to inquire about the way of perfection, there is no necessity of approaching a spiritual master. That means a spiritual master is only meant for one thing in everyone's life. He is not meant for anything else. He is meant for showing the path of perfection for everyone in this material world who is entangled in the material existence, because that is Krishna's mission in this material world. That is what Krishna wants. Why this creation? Actually there is no need for Krishna to create because there is a whole spiritual world, perfect, complete, full of transcendental bliss, full of transcendental knowledge, full of everything required for our perfect existence, but foolish persons that we are, we left everything and come here. And why have we left that and come here? Because I want to enjoy independent of Krishna. This is utter foolishness because I want to enjoy independent of Krishna, because there is no such thing that you can enjoy independently, because that is not your position, that is not your actual position. There is no existence for you without the help of Krishna, without support of Krishna. There is no existence. So Krishna is so kind that because we are adamant, he makes arrangement. He is all powerful, so he makes arrangement. What arrangement does he do?

---
### material desire, spiritual master, illusion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/gGr8vctXBOg?t=320
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "This is not what a Guru is meant for, for material happiness. Material happiness there are millions of ways, concocted ways, illusory ways. Spiritual master is not meant for that."
**Summary:** The speaker continues to explain Krishna's arrangements for those seeking independent enjoyment, emphasizing the futility of material pursuits and contrasting them with the true purpose of a spiritual master, who guides towards spiritual perfection, not material happiness.

He creates this material world and after creating for us to try and enjoy independently, he is making elaborate arrangements, because we have got so many varieties of desires, how I like to enjoy independently. So many arrangements Krishna has made. So to get us out of this madness, trying to enjoy in so many different ways, unsuccessfully, Krishna is actually also making arrangement to help us to get out of this madness. So Krishna himself comes for that purpose to extricate us, to get us out of this entanglement, and he sends his bonafide spiritual masters, the pure devotees for the same purpose that he comes. He also sends them from his personal abode. So Prabhupada says a spiritual master is not a kind of decoration for a householder. Some people for a fashion, they say, we also have a Guru. I have got this Guru, and what for you have got a Guru? Oh he gives me blessings by which I will be materially very very happy. This is not what a Guru is meant for, for material happiness. Material happiness there are millions of ways, concocted ways, illusory ways. Spiritual master is not meant for that. So Prabhupada says generally a fashionable materialist engages a so called spiritual master without any profit. Any profit means without any real benefit, spiritual benefit. The pseudo spiritual master flatters the so called disciple and therefore both the master and his ward, that is the disciple, go to hell without a doubt.

---
### guru, disciple, materialism, Srimad Bhagavatam
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/gGr8vctXBOg?t=493
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Maharaj Pariksit is the right type of disciple because he puts forward questions vital to the interest of all men, particularly for the dying man."
**Summary:** The speaker describes the dangers of approaching unqualified spiritual masters for material gain, contrasting this with Parikshit Maharaj's sincere inquiry about ultimate welfare, which forms the basis of Srimad Bhagavatam.

You see this material world is a place which is full of material actions and reactions. So foolish disciples, they approach even greater fools who pose as spiritual masters and only ask from them, how can I be materially happy? How can I get some material wealth? How can I get some material sense gratification? And the foolish spiritual masters, so called spiritual masters, they also either in ignorance or even sometimes those who are not ignorant also, by way of cheating, they try to give some material wealth, give some material happiness, material wealth, material solutions to their problems. Where is the bona fide guru to be found in this world who will give ultimate solution to all problems? Such spiritual masters are very very rare in this material world and so we have to understand this point. So Prabhupada says about Pariksit Maharaj, Maharaj Pariksit is the right type of disciple because he puts forward questions vital to the interest of all men, particularly for the dying man. Pariksit's questions are very very very vital, they are of the utmost importance. You can ask a spiritual master so many different questions, but the essential questions which are relevant for our ultimate welfare, for getting us out of this material entanglement, those questions is not generally asked by common people. So about Pariksit Maharaj Prabhupada says, the question put forward by Maharaj Pariksit is the basic principle of the complete thesis of Srimad Bhagavatam. See Pariksit asked one or two questions in the beginning, but the answer to those two questions led to Shukadeva Goswami speaking the entire Srimad Bhagavatam. So you can imagine how vital those questions are. Now let us see how intelligently the great master replies. This is how Prabhupada is ending the purport of this particular verse.

---
### hearing, chanting, remembering, worship
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/gGr8vctXBOg?t=677
**Verse References:** SB 1.19.38
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Please let me know what a man should hear, yad srotavyam, what he should chant, yad japyam, what he should remember, smartavyam, and worship, bhajaniyam."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the 38th verse, highlighting Parikshit Maharaj's intelligent inquiry about what one should hear, chant, remember, worship, and what one should avoid.

How Shukadeva Goswami will answer these vital questions. The 38th verse: yad srotavyam atho japyam yad kartavyam nrbhit prabho smartavyam bhajaniyam va bruhiyadva viparyayam. Very intelligent question asked by Pariksit Maharaj to Shukadeva Goswami. Already he has asked in the previous verse, what question has he asked? Kindly show the way of perfection. Now he is asking, what is he asking now in the 38th verse? The translation is, please let me know what a man should hear, yad srotavyam, what he should chant, yad japyam, what he should remember, smartavyam, and worship, bhajaniyam, what is worship about for him, and yadva viparyayam, and also kindly tell me what he should not do. He is asking both positive and negative, what he should do and what he should not do. This is very very intelligent question.

---

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Always remember Krishna and never forget Krishna. Simply by doing that, you will get all perfection."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the importance of knowing what to do and what not to do for achieving perfection, summarizing Vedic injunctions as 'vidhi' and 'nishedha,' and emphasizing the paramount importance of always remembering Krishna and never forgetting Him.

Please explain all this to me. So we should understand Pariksit is most intelligent king of his time. So he is asking, of course he is asking about his own welfare, but he is also asking about the others welfare. He is also asking about others welfare. So it is very important that we should know for achieving the ultimate perfection specifically what we should do and also what we should not do. That is why in the Vedas there are so many instructions, but they all can be broadly classified into two different set of instructions: what is to be done and what is not to be done, vidhi nishedha. This is explained elsewhere by Srila Prabhupada. All the Vedic injunctions are can be classified in two headings: vidhi means what you should do and nishedha means what you should not do. And in the Bhagavatam it is said among all these different instructions, the most important positive injunction that you must do is always remember Krishna. What is the most important positive injunction of the Vedas? Always remember Krishna. And what is the most important negative injunction? Never forget Krishna. It is very interesting, both are the same. Both are the same now. Always remember Krishna means never forget Krishna. Never forget Krishna means always remember Krishna. Why is it said like this? Because the only thing you need to do to achieve perfection is to always remember Krishna and never forget him. Simply by doing that, you will get all perfection. Now how to do it, that's another matter, but this is the injunction, the most important injunction. So if somebody is not able to directly take up this always remembering Krishna, then there are other methods by which they are brought to that point and that is the entirety of the Vedic literature. I will stop here. Thank you very much Hare Krishna.

---

## SB 1.19.39-40 | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | 06-06-2023 | Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### Srimad Bhagavatam, Structure, Krishna Leela
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/E4K0TdH5U7s?t=33
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The 10th canto exclusively describes Krishna Leela."
**Summary:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami introduces the structure of the Srimad Bhagavatam, explaining the division into 12 cantos, chapters, and verses, highlighting the 10th canto's focus on Krishna Leela.

Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya Narayanam Namaskritya Naram Chai Vanarottamam Devi Saraswati Vyasam Tato Jayam Udi Rayet. In this session, we will be discussing two verses, the 39th and the 40th verses of the 19th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. These are the last two verses in this chapter, and with the end of this chapter, even the first canto comes to an end. So these are the last two verses of this entire canto itself of the Srimad Bhagavatam. As you might be aware, Bhagavatam is divided into 12 cantos, skandha in Sanskrit. And each of those cantos have got many chapters, and each chapter has got many verses. So totally, the number of verses is 18,000 in the Srimad Bhagavatam, and the number of cantos is 12. And there are different number of chapters in each canto, just like this first canto has 19 chapters. The second canto has 10 chapters, whereas the 10th canto has got 90 chapters. The biggest canto. Why? Because the 10th canto exclusively describes Krishna Leela. The other cantos describe the pastimes of the other incarnations of the Supreme Lord, and even the pastimes of the activities of the devotees of the Supreme Lord, including the devotees of Krishna like the Pandavas. So 39th verse.

---
### Renunciation, Vedic Culture, Cow Protection
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/E4K0TdH5U7s?t=148
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "A pound of milk fresh from the milk bag of a cow is sufficient to feed an adult with all vitamin values."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the meaning of the 39th verse, discussing the role of renounced saints and sages who briefly visit householders to beg for milk, highlighting the Vedic culture of cow protection and the nutritional value of milk.

The meaning of this 39th verse is, O powerful Brahmana! It is said that you hardly stay in the houses of men long enough to milk a cow. Shukadeva Goswami has appeared in that assembly. Now what is the duration for which if Shukadeva Goswami goes to somebody's house. So Prabhupada explains this in the purport. Saints and sages in the renounced order of life go to the houses of householders at the time they milk the cows early in the morning and ask some quantity of milk for subsistence. Even though somebody will be completely renounced from everything material, as long as this body is there, for the bodily maintenance, at least devotees, they beg for some milk. And from whom do they beg? From the householders who keep cows. That's the Vedic culture generally. Vedic culture means the householders generally keep at least one cow or a few cows, and they have some land for growing some food. Very easily all economic necessities can be met by having some land for cultivation of food grains, vegetables, growing some flowers or fruits, and also having one or more cows. So Prabhupada says the householders are able to not only get enough for themselves, but if some saintly person comes begging, bhavati bhikshan dehi, they want very little, little milk. So the householders can easily give that little milk. So Prabhupada says a pound of milk fresh from the milk bag of a cow is sufficient to feed an adult with all vitamin values, and therefore saints and sages live only on milk. Very very easy. They take some milk, drink that, and that's enough to give them all necessary nutrition for their subsistence, for engaging in devotional service exclusively, without the need for gathering some grains, cooking it, preparing some foodstuff. Either oneself or somebody else has to prepare nothing. Directly take the milk, especially when milk is being taken from the cow, the cow is being milked, just take that milk, drink it, finished, over. So Prabhupada says in the Vedic culture, even the poorest of the householders keep at least 10 cows. This is Vedic culture, each delivering 12 to 20 quarts of milk, and therefore no one hesitates to spare a few pounds of milk for the mendicants. But.

---

I don't know the exact conversion of these quarts to pounds. Of course pounds, generally one pound is about half a kilo, kilogram. And I think quarts means something like some 15 or 20 quarts make a pound, something like that. So, when they have ten cows, each of those cows can give so much milk, and they have plenty of milk to even spare for, even the poorest of householders is considered poor householders. They can spare some milk for the saintly persons who come begging. Anybody can give. So, Srila Prabhupada says, it is the duty of householders to maintain the saints and sages like the children, like their own children. They give some milk at least to those who come begging for some milk, milk or food. So, a saint like Shukadeva Goswami would hardly stay at the house of a householder for, he would hardly stay for more than five minutes. He would hardly stay for more than five minutes. That means, he would not even stay for five minutes at the house of a householder. So, what does this mean? In other words, such saints are rarely, very rarely seen in the houses of householders. Very rarely. If at all they approach a householder, little milk, less than five minutes. When they are milking the cow, just take little milk and go on. So, therefore, Maharaj Pariksit therefore prayed to Shukadeva Goswami to instruct him as soon as possible. So, Raupar is giving us a lot of instructions here. Householders should understand saintly persons when they come begging, they are not going to stay for long. So, to take advantage of the saintly persons coming for begging is the real business of a householder. The real duty of a householder.

## SB 1.19.4 | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | 03-05-2023 | Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### acceptance, detachment, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/alHRLofKaW4?t=50
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The king accepted this as good news, for it would be the cause of his indifference toward worldly things."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the verse and explains how King Pariksit welcomed the news of his imminent death as a means to detach himself from worldly affairs.

In this session, we will be discussing the fourth verse of the 19th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. The meaning of this fourth verse is, while the king was thus repenting, he received news of his imminent death, which would be due to the bite of a snake bird, occasioned by the curse spoken by the sage's son. The king accepted this as good news, for it would be the cause of his indifference toward worldly things.

---
### detachment, birth and death, spiritual existence
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/alHRLofKaW4?t=107
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "One can stop the repetition of birth and death only by going back to Godhead."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on King Pariksit's devotion and introduces Srila Prabhupada's explanation of spiritual happiness as freedom from the cycle of birth and death, emphasizing the importance of returning to Godhead.

This suggests that the king was actually a very great devotee. He was realized sattva. He is welcoming this curse, which is going to be the cause of his death by the bite of a snake bird. So who would welcome death? It's our natural tendency to live on. Nobody wants to die. But here, Pariksit Maharaj, he is repenting, and he warns that the sinful action of his is counteracted by the appropriate punishment. And when he heard it, that he was cursed by the sage's son, he thought that this is the right punishment for me. And it's an extreme punishment. It's not a small punishment. But we will see Prabhupada explaining how the king accepted this particular news as good news. In the purport, Prabhupada says, real happiness is achieved by spiritual existence or by cessation of the repetition of birth and death. So spiritual happiness, material existence, is freedom from birth and death, and material existence is characterized by repetition of birth and death. One who is born, death is certain, and one who dies, rebirth is certain. So this repetition of birth and death is the characteristic of material existence. One can stop the repetition of birth and death only by going back to Godhead.

---
### going back to Godhead, spiritual kingdom, material world
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/alHRLofKaW4?t=240
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Who's goal in life should be, to stop the repetition of birth and death and how can that be done by going back to godhead."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the meaning of 'going back to Godhead' as returning to Krishna's abode and mentions Srila Prabhupada's magazine 'Back to Godhead'. He emphasizes that the goal of life should be to end the cycle of birth and death by attaining the spiritual kingdom, even the topmost planet, Brahma Loka, cannot provide freedom from birth and death.

What does going back to Godhead mean? Going to the personal abode of Krishna, the Supreme Lord, God's own abode. It's called the Absolute pure within. Going back to Godhead. Sri Prabhupada started one magazine sometime in 1944 on the order of his spiritual master, and he gave the title for that magazine, Back to Godhead. So that magazine is particularly meant for all human beings, who's goal in life should be, to stop the repetition of birth and death and how can that be done by going back to godhead by preparing oneself in such a way that at the end of this life when we have to leave this body, we will not take another material body but we will go to the spiritual kingdom of God in our spiritual body. Further, Srila Prabhupada explains in a material world, even by attaining the topmost planet, Brahma Loka, one cannot get rid of the conditions of repeated birth and death. This is the statement in the eighth chapter of the Bhagavad-gita.

---
### birth and death, material attachment, path of perfection
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/alHRLofKaW4?t=335
**Verse References:** BG 8
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Therefore those who are materially poverty are better candidates than those who are materially prosperous."
**Summary:** The speaker continues to explain that the cycle of birth and death exists throughout the material universe, even in Brahma Loka. He emphasizes the importance of following the path of perfection to become free from material attachments and enter the spiritual kingdom, suggesting that those who are materially poor are better candidates for spiritual advancement.

Means we undergo repetition of birth and death, so beginning from the topmost planet, Brahma Loka, down to the lowest, Patala, all are places where repetition of birth and death takes place, though there are different conditions on different planets. Higher planets have a longer duration of life. There are better facilities for comfortable living. There is better arrangement for sense enjoyment. People are more pious in the upper planets, but still one cannot avoid a repetition of birth and death even by going to the topmost planet in this material existence, that is Brahma Loka. So Prabhupada says, even if you go to Brahma Loka, we do not get freedom from birth and death. And knowing this, one should accept the path of attaining perfection. Generally, people don't take up this path of perfection of going back to Godhead. The path of perfection frees one from all material attachments, and thus one becomes fit to enter into the spiritual kingdom. See, there's a qualification who can go to this spiritual kingdom of God: those who are free from all material attachments. And the material attachments we can become free only if we follow the path of perfection. Therefore, those who are materially poverty are better candidates than those who are materially prosperous. This is explained in many places in the scriptures.

---
### material prosperity, detachment, spiritual progress
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/alHRLofKaW4?t=477
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Material prosperity is like a fever."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of fever to describe material prosperity as a diseased condition that hinders spiritual progress. He emphasizes that the goal should be to return to a healthy, spiritual condition by detaching from material attachments and striving to go back to Godhead.

Material prosperity is like a fever. Fever means the body, healthy body is in a diseased condition. So when somebody is diseased, they should cure the disease and return to the healthy condition, which is the normal condition. But instead of curing the disease and thereby reducing the fever, if somebody is doing things by which the fever keeps on increasing, then that is counterproductive. Similarly, material prosperity is like the diseased condition of human society. Why? Because in this material existence, we are not meant for making arrangements for living comfortably here or settling down in this place, no. This is certified as a place of suffering and misery under all circumstances. So therefore, we should endeavor to go back to God and prepare ourselves by becoming free from all material attachments. So materially poverty-stricken people, they never make arrangements, they cannot make arrangements for staying comfortable, because they are materially not prosperous. Maharaj Pariksit was a great devotee of the Lord and a bona fide candidate for entering into the kingdom of God, but even though he was so, his material assets as the emperor of the world were setbacks to perfect attainment of his rightful status as one of the associates of the Lord in the spiritual sky.

---
### detachment, curse as a blessing, spiritual preparation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/alHRLofKaW4?t=576
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "As a devotee of the Lord, he could understand that the cursing of the brahmana boy, although unwise, was a blessing upon him."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that material prosperity can lead to attachment, hindering spiritual progress. He highlights how Pariksit Maharaj viewed the curse as a blessing, as it would lead to detachment from worldly affairs and prompt him to prepare for death by renouncing his kingdom and associating with saintly persons.

If somebody is materially prosperous, there is a possibility that they have attachment for such material wealth or material belongings. In the course of dealing with this material world, we do develop attachments, and these attachments are impediments on the path of spiritual progress. As a devotee of the Lord, he could understand that the cursing of the brahmana boy, although unwise, was a blessing upon him. So how was this cursing the brahmana boy to die a blessing for Pariksit Maharaj? Because this curse would become the cause of detachment from worldly affairs, both political and social. Why? Because if he knows he is going to die within seven days, he is going to prepare for death by renouncing his kingdom and going to a sacred place to associate with saintly persons to prepare himself to become detached and go back to Godhead. Now, Srila Prabhupada explains something very important here.

---
### duty, regret, spiritual power
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/alHRLofKaW4?t=669
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Shamika Muni sent news to the king that foolish Shungi, his son, misused his spiritual power by cursing the king unwarrantedly."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that Shamika Muni, after regretting his son's curse, conveyed the news to the king as a matter of duty, so that the king could prepare to go back to Godhead. The speaker emphasizes that the curse was an improper and unjust punishment for the king's minor offense.

Shamika Muni also, after regretting the incident, conveyed the news to the king as a matter of duty so that the king would be able to prepare himself to go back to Godhead. If you remember, in the previous chapter, it was explained that Shamika Rishi learnt that his son had cursed the king, the emperor. Just for a small fault of the emperor, this Shamika Rishi's son, Shungi, had cursed the king to die, which was not at all the proper punishment. For a very minor mistake, a severe punishment was given. It was unjust. It was improper, immature on the part of the boy. But still, he conveyed the news to the king so that the king could prepare. Shamika Muni sent news to the king that foolish Shungi, his son, misused his spiritual power by cursing the king unwarrantedly. Why did he say this? Because the incident of the king's garlanding the Muni was not sufficient cause for being cursed to death. But since there was no way to retract the curse, the king was informed to prepare for death within a week. Shamika Rishi is not an ordinary Muni, not an ordinary Brahmana. That will be explained by Srila Prabhupada here.

---
### self-realization, bhakti yoga, detachment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/alHRLofKaW4?t=777
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Both were very very advanced spiritually."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that both Shamika Muni and King Pariksit were self-realized souls, advanced in spirituality. Shamika Muni was a mystic practicing Ashtanga Yoga, while Pariksit Maharaj was a devotee practicing Bhakti Yoga. Both understood that they were not the body but spirit souls belonging to the spiritual kingdom of God and were not afraid of death. The king did not want to shame the Muni further by begging for forgiveness and decided to prepare for his imminent death and find the way to go back to Godhead.

Both Shamika Muni and the king were self-realized souls. Both were very very advanced spiritually. Shamika Muni was a mystic. Mystic means he had practiced Ashtanga Yoga. So he was a Ashtanga Yogi. And Maharaj Pariksit was a devotee. Maharaj Pariksit had practiced Bhakti Yoga. So he was a Bhakti Yogi. Therefore there was no difference between them in self-realization. Both were self-realized. That means they understood, they realized, I am not the body, I am spirit, soul. I don't belong to this material world. I belong to the Supreme Lord in the spiritual kingdom of God. Neither of them was afraid of meeting death. Shamika Rishi was not afraid of death, being self-realized. Neither Pariksit Maharaj was afraid of death, being also self-realized. Maharaj Pariksit could have gone to the Muni to beg his pardon, but the news of imminent death was conveyed to the king with so much regret by the Muni that the king did not want to shame the Muni further by his presence there. Pariksit Maharaj understood that Shamika Rishi has sent word to him that his son has foolishly cursed the king. Therefore the punishment is, according to Pariksit Maharaj, proper. So no need to further go and beg any forgiveness from the Muni. He decided to prepare himself for his imminent death, to find out the way to go back to Godhead. That became his immediate concern. Pariksit's immediate concern. And you will see in the future verses how he prepared and what was Krishna's arrangement for his preparation to go back to Godhead. I'll stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

**Summary:** The speaker introduces the fifth verse of the 19th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam, focusing on Maharaj Pariksit's preparation for death by concentrating his mind in Krishna consciousness after being cursed to die within a week.

In this session we will be discussing from the 5th verse of the 19th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam: Atho Vihayemam Amum Chalokam Vimarshitau Heyataya Purastat Krishnangri Sevam Adhimanyamana Upavishatprayam Amartyanadyam. The meaning of this 5th verse is Maharaj Pariksit sat down firmly on the banks of the Ganges to concentrate his mind in the Ganges. To concentrate his mind in Krishna consciousness rejecting all other practices of self-realization because transcendental loving service to Krishna is the greatest achievement superseding all other methods. So this verse is directly describing what did Pariksit do to prepare for death having got the news that he was cursed to die within a week. He was cursed to die within a week by a snake bite.

### bhakti yoga, self-realization, Goloka Vrindavana

**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

So he renounced his kingdom and he went to a sacred place on the bank of the river Ganga, sat down firmly to concentrate his mind in Krishna Consciousness. Why? Because he's a devotee and he knows that he should not become a devotee. And he knows that bhakti yoga is the best system for self-realization so he rejected all of the practices of self-realization because he knew that loving devotional service to Krishna is the greatest achievement, nothing else can compare with that. So in the purport Srila Prabhupada explains for a devotee like Maharaj Pariksit none of the material planets even the topmost brahma loka is as desirable as Goloka Vrindavana the abode of Lord Sri Krishna the primeval Lord and original personality of cottage.

### Vaikuntha, Goloka Vrindavana, spiritual sky
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/tKs82AxeW6o?t=544
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "All these planets, that means Vaikuntha lokas and Goloka Vrindavana are eternally situated in the spiritual sky."
**Summary:** The speaker describes the spiritual sky with its Vaikuntha planets and Goloka Vrindavana, contrasting them with the temporary nature of material planets and emphasizing the eternal existence of the spiritual realm.

In the capacity of servitor, friend, parent or conjugal lover of the Lord, either in one of the innumerable Vaikuntha planets or in Goloka Vrindavana, the planet of Lord Sri Krishna. So, this statement, it's being explained. There are many, many, many, many planets in the spiritual sky beyond the material creation. And those planets are of two categories. The Vaikuntha planets is one category of spiritual planets where the Supreme Lord as Narayana is residing with his devotees. And there is another planet called Goloka Vrindavana also in the spiritual sky where Lord Sri Krishna resides with his devotees. Narayana is four-handed Lord. And Sri Krishna is two-handed Lord. But both are the supreme personality of Godhead in different forms. All these planets, that means Vaikuntha lokas and Goloka Vrindavana are eternally situated in the spiritual sky. The characteristic of material planets, whether Brahma loka or Indra loka, Chandra loka. Bhu loka, Patar loka, Sutala loka, all material planets are not eternal. Bhutva bhutva praliyate. They are constantly created, maintained and destroyed. Including Brahma loka. And so are the living beings. The living beings are created, maintained and destroyed also. So the planets in the spiritual sky, the Vaikuntha lokas and Goloka Vrindavana are eternally situated. There is no creation, there is no destruction, there is no need for maintenance. That we have to understand, we have to realize, we have to accept. We have to appreciate this fact.

But in contrast, Prabhupada says a devotee like Maharaj Pariksit does not care for any of these material planets, whether moon planet or Brahmaloka. He does not care for any of these material planets, whether moon planet or Brahmaloka. So when he was assured that he is going to die within seven days, on a fixed date he was assured that he is going to die, he became determined in the transcendental loving service of Lord Krishna by complete fasting in the bank of the transcendental river Yamuna. Now earlier it was mentioned that Pariksit Maharaj was sitting down firmly on the bank of the Ganges. And here in the purport Shrita Prabhupada is telling Pariksit Maharaj sat down on the bank of the river Yamuna. So Prabhupada explains in a later purport, actually even though some places scriptures mention that Pariksit sat down on the bank of the river Ganga, but actually he sat down on the bank of the river Yamuna. ### Ganges, purification, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/C9dY8rJoCV0?t=42
**Verse References:** SB 1.19.6
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Consequently everyone who is destined to die must take shelter of this river."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the 6th verse of the 19th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam, focusing on the glorification of the Ganges river and its purifying qualities due to its association with the lotus feet of Lord Krishna and Tulasi leaves.

Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya Narayanam Namaskritya Naram Chai Vanarottamam Deveem Saraswateem Vyasam Tato Jayam Udi Rayet. In this session we will be discussing from the 6th verse of the 19th chapter, chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. Yavailasach Shri Tulasi Vimishra Krishnangri Renu Abhyadikambu Netri Punatiloka Nubhayatra Seshan Kastamna Sevet Marishyamanah. The meaning of this 6th verse is as follows: The river Ganges, by which the king sat to fast, carries the most auspicious water, which is mixed with the dust of the lotus feet of the Lord and tulasi leaves. Therefore that water sanctifies the three worlds inside and outside, and even sanctifies Lord Shiva and other demigods. Consequently everyone who is destined to die must take shelter of this river. So something about the glorification.

### Yamuna, Krishna, Tulasi
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/C9dY8rJoCV0?t=775
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Lord Krishna's lotus feet are always besmeared with the Tulasi leaves, and thus as soon as the lotus feet contact the water of the Ganges in the Yamuna the rivers become at once sanctified."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes that Maharaj Pariksit took shelter of the Yamuna because it carries the dust of Lord Krishna's lotus feet and Tulasi leaves, explaining that Krishna's lotus feet are always adorned with Tulasi leaves, which sanctify the river upon contact.

So therefore it is said here it is especially mentioned herein that Maharaj Pariksit took shelter of that particular river which is beautifully flowing carrying the dust of the lotus feet of Lord Krishna, mixed with Tulasi leaves. Now how are dust of lotus feet of Krishna and Tulasi leaves both in Yamuna? We heard how dust of lotus feet of Krishna is in Yamuna because Krishna fell down into the river Yamuna when Vasudeva was crossing over. Now also Tulasi leaves are present in Yamuna because Lord Krishna's lotus feet are always besmeared with the Tulasi leaves. And thus as soon as the lotus feet contact the water of the Ganges in the Yamuna the rivers become at once sanctified. The Lord however contacted the river Yamuna more than the Ganges. Krishna performed his pastimes in Vrindavan on the bank of the Yamuna so he was more in contact with Yamuna than the Ganga. According to the Varaha Purana as quoted by Srila Jiva Goswami there is no difference between the water of Ganges and the Yamuna because both these waters are washing the lotus feet of the same Supreme Lord.

**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "When he stretched his leg it pierced the coverings of the universe and the water of the Karna ocean leaked into this universe, it washed the feet of Lord Vaman Dev and that became the river Ganga which flows through all the planets from the topmost planet Brahmaloka to the bottom most Pataloka and purifies the entire universe by the sacred water."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the origin of the Ganges, stating that it originated when Lord Vamana's foot pierced the universe, and the water that washed his feet became the Ganges, which purifies the entire universe, and that both the Ganges and Yamuna wash the lotus feet of the same Supreme Lord.

What is the pastime of the river Ganga appearing in this world? When the Lord appeared incarnated as Vamana he stretched his leg to measure three steps of land. When he stretched his leg it pierced the coverings of the universe and the water of the Karna ocean leaked into this universe. It washed the feet of Lord Vaman Dev and that became the river Ganga which flows through all the planets from the topmost planet Brahmaloka to the bottom most Pataloka and purifies the entire universe by the sacred water. Because that water contains the dust of the lotus feet of the Supreme Lord Vaman Deva. So therefore Varaha Purana says there is no difference between Ganga and Yamuna. Both are washing the lotus feet of the same Supreme Lord whether Vamana or Krishna it is only one Supreme Lord. One and only one Supreme Personality of Godhead.

Why did he choose to fix his mind on the lotus feet of Lord Krishna? Because Krishna alone can award liberation. That's why Krishna's another name is Munivrata. That's why Krishna's another name is Mukunda, one who can give Mukti, liberation. Even Lord Shiva admits, nobody else can give liberation. Mukti Pradata Sarveshaam Vishnureva Nasamshayaha. Shiva says nobody else can give liberation other than Vishnu. So Vishnu, Krishna, only they can give liberation. Only they can give liberation. That is the supreme personality of Godhead. That is the supreme personality of Godhead. And in order to become liberated, one has to become free from all kinds of material association and material attachments. ### Vishnu Tattva, Krishna, liberation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/qOOwbq9SZLc?t=230
**Verse References:** BG 9.4
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna is the fountainhead of the principle of Vishnu Tattva means Krishna is the source of all other incarnations, all other forms of the Supreme Lord."
**Summary:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami explains Shrila Prabhupada's explanation of Pariksit Maharaj retiring to the Ganges, the relationship between Krishna and Vishnu as Vishnu Tattva, and Krishna as the source of all incarnations.

...Sixth verse, Shrila Prabhupad explains the significance of Pariksit Maharaj retiring and going to a holy place on the bank of the sacred river Ganga. Shrila Prabhupada says in the Sixth verse, the water of the Ganges sanctifies all the three worlds, including the gods and the demigods, because it emanates from the lotus feet, the personality of Godhead Vishnu. Lord Krishna is the fountainhead of the principle of Vishnu Tattva and therefore shelter of his lotus feet can deliver one from all sins including an offense committed by a king unto a Brahmana. Here Srila Prabhupada explains what's the relationship between Krishna and Vishnu, actually they are one person called the principle of Vishnu Tattva. You should understand that the supreme personality of Godhead Krishna is an unlimited personality, so he is not limited to one form. His original form is that of Krishna and he has many, many, many countless other forms which are forms of his own personality like the form of Vishnu in this material world or the form of Narayana in the spiritual world or the form of Balarama, form of Sankarsana, Pradyumna, Aniruddha, Keshava, Madhava, Govinda, unlimited forms because he is unlimited personality. He has unlimited forms, he is unlimited, but one should not mistake the form of the living being to be the form of the Lord, no, the form of the living being is never the form of the Lord. The living being is a very, very, very, very, very tiny living being, Anu Atma and Krishna is Vibhu Atma. He is unlimited. He is a super soul and we are all individual, tiny, very, very infinitesimal spirit soul. So there is a difference. Both are spiritual by nature, the living entity and the Supreme Lord, but the Supreme Lord is unlimited person and we are limited, very, very, very, very tiny like comparing an atom to diamond. The whole universe, it's like that, the difference is like that. We can't measure how big is this universe. It's billions and trillions of times the size of an atom. So the living entity should never be equalized or even compared with the Supreme. So, Krishna is the fountainhead of the principle of Vishnu Tattva means Krishna is the source of all other incarnations, all other forms of the Supreme Lord. From Krishna emanate Narayana, Sankarsana, Pradyumna, Aniruddha, Vasudeva, Vishnu, and Vishnu. There are three forms of the Supreme. One is the Supreme. One is the Supreme. That's the Supreme. That's the Supreme. That's the Supreme. That's the Supreme. Keshava, Madhava, Govinda, all the Vishnu forms, all the Narayana forms, emanate from Krishna. That's the meaning of Krishna telling in the Bhagavad Gita, Aham sarvasya prabhavo matta sarvam pravartate.

**Summary:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami discusses the arrival of great sages at Pariksit Maharaj's location, emphasizing the significance of Pariksit's renunciation and his status as an ideal emperor and pure devotee.

The meaning of this eighth verse is, at that time, when Pariksit Maharaj sat to fast until death, preparing himself for quitting the body, purifying himself, all the great minds and thinkers, accompanied by their disciples and sages, who could very sanctify a place of pilgrimage, just by their presence. They arrived there, where Pariksit Maharaj was sitting, for fasting and purifying himself. They arrived there on the plea of making a pilgrim's journey. Sushila Prabhupada explains this in the purport. When Maharaj Pariksit sat down on the bank of the Ganges, the news spread in all directions of the universe. It's a very, very, very great incident, because he was Hindu. He was a great devotee of the Lord. And he was the ideal emperor, ruling the whole earth planet. He was a very powerful king. He was a descendant of the Pandavas. Pandavas were pure devotees of Krishna. So was Pariksit, the grandson of Arjuna, also a pure devotee of Krishna. So, when such a great personality died, when such a great personality decides to renounce his kingdom, it is not a small incident. It even is important for the great personalities living in higher planets. The personalities of the higher planets know the aim of life is to purify oneself. And to prepare oneself to go back to Godhead. All the great personalities know this. But they are unable to themselves engage in a specific activity for complete 100% purification.

### devotional service, pilgrimage, purification
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/qOOwbq9SZLc?t=691
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The absolute truth is the personality of Godhead Krishna and this can be realized only by those who follow the process of pure devotional service."
**Summary:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami explains the rarity of pure devotees, the sages' visit to Pariksit Maharaj, the burden of sins on pilgrimage sites, and the sages' intention to hear the Srimad Bhagavatam from Shukadeva Goswami.

Because among them, different sages have different understanding of what is the best means for attaining the absolute truth. Because to understand what is the absolute truth is not at all easy if one simply studies the Vedas. It becomes different kinds of spiritual practices. The absolute truth is the personality of Godhead Krishna and this can be realized only by those who follow the process of pure devotional service. Such pure devotees are very very very rare in this whole universe. Including the upper planets. Including Brahma Loka, Mahar Loka, Jana Loka, Tapo Loka. Including the heavenly planets. It is very rare to find a 100% pure devotee like Pariksit Maharaj or it is very rare to find a 100% pure devotee like Shukadev Goswami. So when they receive news, Pariksit Maharaj is preparing to quit his body, they actually came to meet Pariksit Maharaj. But when they were asked, where are you going? Where are you traveling? They made an excuse that we are going to a holy place to take a bath to become purified. But what is their qualification? They themselves are personalities who can purify a place by their presence. They don't require to become purified. Neither they require to take a bath in a holy place. No. So the significance of such great personalities visiting a holy place is also described by Prabhupada. The places of pilgrimage become overburdened with the sins of common people. The common people go to a place of pilgrimage, take a bath, and they leave behind all their sinful reactions. So as millions of people go, let's say, a Kumbha Mela. So many millions of people go there to purify themselves by taking a bath at the auspicious time in the holy place of Prayag, Prayag Raj. So they leave behind all their sinful reactions. The holy places become overburdened with these sinful reactions of common people. But when such sages who are very very powerful personalities, they visit overburdened places of pilgrimage, they sanctify the holy places by their mere presence. So the sages who came there, they were not interested in their own purification. They were already pure. But they came there to meet Maharaj Pariksit who was a great personality. Now they could foresee, Prabhupada says, that the Srimad Bhagavatam would be spoken by Shukadeva Goswami. Therefore they came there to hear the Bhagavatam spoken by Shukadeva Goswami, which was going to be spoken by Shukadeva Goswami to Maharaj Pariksit. I'll stop here. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

### Chavana, Bhrigu Muni, Weakness, Devotional Service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/HHaTdJsX-qE?t=240
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "That's the difference between great personalities and ordinary people."
**Summary:** The speaker begins describing Chavana Rishi, son of Bhrigu Muni, and discusses the weaknesses that even great personalities can exhibit due to material influence, emphasizing the importance of rectifying mistakes and avoiding disrespect towards such figures. He stresses the need for caution and engagement in devotional service to avoid sinful activities.

The first sage being described by Prabhupada is Chavana, a great sage and one of the sons of Brugu Muni. Brugu Muni was himself a great personality. So, his son was another great sage Chavana. This Chavana Rishi was born prematurely when his pregnant mother was killed. His testified himself to his father's the family about how he became kidnapped. See even among great personalities, there is some weakness of enviousness, weakness of greed, weakness of pride. This is the nature of material existence you should understand. So, briefly Prabhupada is describing, he was born prematurely, Chavana is one of the six sons of his father Bhrigu Muni, great personalities, because of their practicing severe austerities, diligently studying the Vedic literature, cultivating spiritual knowledge, and living a very austere life, teaching this most important branch of knowledge, which is spiritual knowledge, Atmavidya, to other aspiring students, all these great works are done by these great sages. But, not all of them are always in this material world, living a pure life, sometimes overcome by some bad influence, or overcome by some weakness of the heart, they may make some mistake, they may commit some mistake. But, because they are great personalities, they immediately take steps to rectify their mistakes. That's the difference between great personalities and ordinary people. So, we should never show them disrespect, or ignore them, or neglect them, just because they commit a mistake, or they have done something wrong in the past because of some weakness, or because of being influenced by the overwhelming, overpowering material energy. So, it's a lesson for us, of course, we are very very ordinary persons compared to such great personalities. So, we are constantly subject to the influence of this most powerful material energy. And we often practice this in ourouls. So, we are constantly subject to the influence of this most powerful material energy. And we are prone to make mistakes again and again and again and again. So therefore, if we want to stop committing sins, if we want to rectify our old habits, we should be extra cautious to always strenuously try to avoid bad association. To strenuously try to constantly engage in pious activities and which are the best type of pious activities, that is pious activities of the nature of devotional service.

### Bhrigu Samhita, Astrology, Creation, Elements
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/HHaTdJsX-qE?t=658
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "This Bhrigu Samhita is a great astrological treatise authored by Bhrigu Muni and it contains so many details about the science of astrology."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses Bhrigu Muni's authorship of the Bhrigu Samhita, a treatise on astrology, and his explanation of the creation process, detailing how the elements of air, fire, water, and earth are generated from ether.

that Bhrigu Muni he instructed Bharadwaja Muni about astronomical evolution and he is the author of the great Bhrigu Samhita. This Bhrigu Samhita is a great astrological treatise authored by Bhrigu Muni and it contains so many details about the science of astrology. This Bhrigu Muni is such a great personality. He explained how air fire water and earth are generated from ether. At the beginning of the creation, there is only the material energy in the inactive state. So when material energy is activated by the glance of Maha Vishnu, then material energy becomes activated and first of all there is a trap. He explains this and says that the will of the Father is an intensity of energy and there is a trap. So when material energy is activated by the glance of Maha Vishnu then material energy becomes transformation of Pradhana to Mahatatva. Mahatatva is the active principle of the material energy that Mahatatva transforms into false ego and false ego transforms into other elements like that how these elements get transformed and generated from one element another element is generated. So, from ether, ether is the first among the five elements to appear in the process of creation. So, from ether how air is generated, from air how fire is generated, how from fire water is generated and from water how earth is generated. This Brighumani explained this particular aspect of the process of creation.

### Bhrigu Muni, Science, Eternal Soul, Varnashrama
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/HHaTdJsX-qE?t=771
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Even though our body is temporary, body is changing, body will perish, but I, the real I or you or any living being is eternal."
**Summary:** The speaker continues describing Bhrigu Muni's knowledge of the body's internal workings, his logical establishment of the soul's eternity, and his scientific propounding of the Varnashrama institution, clarifying that it is not the degraded caste system but a system for the welfare of society.

He explained how the air in the stomach works and regulates the intestines. See the scientists modern day scientists, they think they have made lot of advancement in understanding how this internal body works, but actually they know very very little. Whereas these great sages based on the knowledge given in the knowledge given in the knowledge given in the knowledge given in the knowledge given in the knowledge given in the knowledge given in the Vedas and as the knowledge is handed down in parampara from the Supreme Lord to Brahma from Brahma to his sons and disciples in disciplic succession, they know lot more than what these modern scientists know. As a great philosopher, he logically established the eternity of the living entity and that is described in the Mahabharata that we are eternal beings. Even though our body is temporary, body is changing, body will perish, but I, the real I or you or any living being is eternal. That Brighumani has established, logically established by explaining the philosophy. He was a great anthropologist and the theory of evolution was long ago explained by him. He was a scientific propounder of the four divisions and orders of human society known as Varnashrama institution. This Varnashrama lot has been described in the scriptures and even Srila Prabhupada spoke a lot about this Varnashrama institution. It is not the, the so called caste system that degraded modern day caste system. No, Varnashrama institution is a scientific institution for the welfare of the entire human society and the welfare of all creatures. So, some other time we will discuss this. So, this is about Brighumani. I will stop here. We will discuss about the other great sages who have been described in this purport by Srila Prabhupada in the next few sessions. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna.

### Sages, Time, Yugas
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/0midTiF87XQ?t=114
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So even beyond that they live."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the greatness and longevity of sages like Vasishta, Narada, and Parikshit, explaining their classifications as Brahma Rishi, Deva Rishi, and Raja Rishi, respectively, and their presence across different Yugas.

The historical details about the great sages, their personality, how they are so great personalities themselves. Vasishta. Vasishta is the great celebrated sage among the brahmanas, well known as the Brahma Rishi. Rishi means a sage. There are different kinds of rishis. Brahma rishi is a sage among the brahmanas. A deva rishi is a sage among the devatas, devas. Narada is a deva rishi. Vasishta is a Brahma rishi. Pariksit is a Raja rishi. He is a sage amongst great kings. Raja rishi. So like Pariksit is a Raja rishi, Narada is a deva rishi, Vasishta is a Brahma rishi. Now Vasishta is a prominent figure in both the Ramayana and Mahabharata periods. You should remember Ramayana is in Treta Yuga, historical narration of a history of the great incarnation of the Supreme Lord, Lord Rama. Mahabharata is the history of the Pandavas and Kauravas during the Dwapar Yuga, a later age. But still, such personalities as Brahma, Shiva, Vasishta, Bhrgumuni, they all live beyond the duration of one Yuga. A Yuga itself, this Kali Yuga, lasts 432,000 years according to the calculation of time on the Earth planet. So they live beyond even the four Yugas put together. The four Yugas, put together Sathya, Treta, Dwapara, Kali, each previous Yuga to the Kali Yuga is two times, three times, four times the duration of Kali Yuga. So sum total of the four Yugas duration, Sathya, Treta, Dwapara, Kali, is 43 lakhs of years.

### Ramayana, Lord Rama, Kaikeyi
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/0midTiF87XQ?t=374
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "The second boon she asked was very, very, very heartbreaking. It shattered the heart of Dasharatha."
**Summary:** The speaker narrates the story of Lord Rama's impending coronation and the intervention of Queen Kaikeyi, who asked for two boons that led to Rama's exile and her son's coronation.

Mahabharata periods he celebrated the coronation ceremony of the personality of God Sri Rama if you remember the description in Ramayana Lord Rama appeared as a son of Dasharatha and one of the queens of Dasharatha Kausalya was his mother so Rama was about to be enthroned he was the eldest son of Dasharatha he was about to be enthroned as the as the next king of Ayodhya succeeding Dasharatha Maharaj but the day before the coronation Dasharatha's youngest queen Kaikeyi she asked him for two boons which he had promised to give her earlier so the boon she asked which Dasharatha had promised to fulfill she asked that her son be coronated as the king of Ayodhya and not Rama so Dasharatha had agreed to give her this boom and every mother wants Her own son to be the king. Okay. He agreed for that. But the second boon she asked was very, very, very heartbreaking. It shattered the heart of Dasharatha. The second boon she asked is not only that my son should be coronated as the king in place of Rama, but Rama should be in exile for 14 years from today, so that her son can establish himself as the king without any opposition from Rama. This was Kaikeyi's opinion.

### Lord Rama, Promise, Vasishta
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/0midTiF87XQ?t=499
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "To keep his father's promise intact. This is the ideal son of his great father."
**Summary:** The speaker continues the Ramayana narrative, highlighting Rama's willingness to fulfill his father's promise by accepting exile, Bharata's refusal to take the throne, and Rama's eventual coronation, with Vasishta present at the ceremony. He also mentions Vasishta's presence at the battle of Kurukshetra.

This was Kaikeyi's calculation. So Dasharatha was completely heartbroken. But nevertheless, he lived up to his promise. And when Rama came and inquired from Dasharatha, what is the cause for his grief, the day before his coronation, when Dasharatha said, I have given such a boon to Kaikeyi, two boons, and she's asking these two things. Rama readily agreed to keep the promise of his father intact. He readily agreed to Bharata, his younger stepbrother, being coronated as the king of Ayodhya to succeed Dasharatha. And he agreed to go to the forest and live in exile for 14 years. To keep his father's promise intact. This is the ideal son of his great father. So later on, after 14 years, when he came back, Bharata, of course, did not want to be the king. And he would not accept the position of the king of Ayodhya. He ruled on behalf of Rama. And when Rama returned after exile, he handed over charge of ruling the kingdom to Rama. Rama was coronated as the king of Ayodhya. After he returned from exile. And Vasishta was present in that coronation ceremony of Lord Rama. He was also present on the battlefield of Kurukshetra. After that, he was raised on the battlefield of Kurukshetra. After Treta Yuga, the Dwapara Yuga began and towards the end of Dwapara Yuga there was the battle of Kurukshetra. So Vasishta was present during the battle of Kurukshetra.

### Vasishta, Sages, Weakness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/0midTiF87XQ?t=633
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes Vasishta's greatness and long life, connecting him to Hiranyakashipu's history, while also acknowledging the weaknesses of even great sages. He stresses the rarity of finding someone completely free from defects in the material world.

He could approach all the higher and lower planets and his name is also connected to the history of Hiranyakashipu. Hiranyakashipu was in Sakhi Yuga. So this Vasishta is a very very great personality and he has a very very very long duration of life. All these sages, great sages, they have a very long duration of life. There was a great tension between him, that is Vasishta, and Vishwamitra. There was a quarrel between Vasishta and Shabu. As I said in the previous session, they are great personalities, Vasishta, Vishwamitra, Bhrigu Muni, Bharadwaja, but they do have some weakness. They do have some weakness. Now as I said earlier and repeating, because of their weakness we should not disrespect them or we should not look down upon them. You should understand that this material world is a very very difficult place to live and to find somebody who is a spotless in character, who is completely free from all defects and weaknesses is extremely rare. And who is such a personality, who is completely free from all defects? It is, one is the Supreme Lord himself, who is eternally pure, eternally full of all good qualities, and his pure devotees like Narada, the Pandavas, Pariksit Maharaj, Shukadeva Goswami. So these personalities who are pure devotees are very very very very very rare.

Vasishta had one wish-fulfilling cow called Kamadhenu. And Vishwamitra while visiting Vasishta noticed that Vasishta has that Kamadhenu. So he wanted that Kamadhenu. Vasishta Muni refused to spare his Kamadhenu. And Vishwamitra, he killed 100 sons of Vasishta because Vasishta refused to give him the Kamadhenu. Because Vishwamitra was a powerful king.
