### death, Krishna consciousness, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/irlp-_ltNtg?t=41
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "That is Krishna consciousness, situated in devotional service to Krishna, situated in bhakti, then we straight go to Krishna's personal abode for eternal life full of bliss and knowledge."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the topic of Parikshit Maharaj's preparation for death and emphasizes the importance of Krishna consciousness in achieving eternal life.

Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya Narayanam Namaskritya Naram Chai Vanarottamam. Deveem Saraswateem Vyasam Tato Jayam Udi Rayet. Om Namah Bhagavate Vasudevaya Narayanam Namaskritya Naram Chai Vanarottamam. In this session, we will continue to discuss from the same two verses, verses 9 and 10 of the 19th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. These two verses list the names of some of the great sages who were visiting the place where Pariksit Maharaj decided to sit down on the bank of the river Ganga and fast unto death, preparing himself for quitting the body in one week's time. So these great saintly persons from the upper planets of the universe, they could understand the importance of this event of Pariksit Maharaj quitting his body because he was a first class devotee. A great devotee. Pure devotee of the Lord. Therefore, his quitting the body is instructive for everyone all over the universe. That is the greatest test we have to face in life. How to be prepared to quit this body or how to face death in such a way that we don't enter another body, material body. That is the greatest test. But instead, if we are situated in right consciousness, like Parikshit Maharaj was, then we don't take another birth after we leave this body, which is what happens at a time of death. We leave this body. And if we are ready, we are prepared, we are situated in the right consciousness. What is that right consciousness? That is Krishna consciousness. Situated in devotional service to Krishna, situated in bhakti, then we straight go to Krishna's personal abode for eternal life full of bliss and knowledge. In association with Krishna. So, that is what the confidential portions of the Vedas describe to us.

---
### death, devotee, materialism
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/irlp-_ltNtg?t=191
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "But a pure devotee like Parikshit Maharaj has the guarantee that he is going back to Godhead."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the significance of preparing for death in the right consciousness, contrasting the position of a pure devotee with that of materially advanced beings.

So, these great personalities, they know how important it is to actually prepare oneself to quit the body, to leave the body at the time of death, in the right consciousness. So, they are anxious, they are eager to learn by witnessing the passing away of such great personality as Parikshit Maharaj. So, by this you can also estimate what is the position of a pure devotee in the entire universe. Parikshit Maharaj, by virtue of his birth in a family of great kings, Kshatriya family, Pandavas, Pandava family, he was entitled to become the emperor of the entire world, this earth planet. But, compared to the position of an emperor of this earth planet, there are many many great personalities or greater personalities in other higher parts of the universe who have much better power, much more power, much better position, much greater duration of life, bodily beauty and facilities for material enjoyment and lot of knowledge, lot of knowledge, lot of knowledge. And, they also have a lot of good aristocratic society in which they live, etc. The Devatas, the Rishis, the Siddhas, the Charanas, the Vidyadharas, the Kinnaras and so many different grades of higher beings in the upper planets up to Brahma Loka. But none of them are actually by virtue of their actual material position none of them are free from death none of them are by virtue of their material position in a proper situation to face death so that they don't take birth again and go back to Godhead none of them have that assurance simply by virtue of their position as some great devata or great jnani tapasvi yogi brahma rishi raja rishi deva rishi none of them have this assurance or guarantee but a pure devotee like Parikshit Maharaj has the guarantee that he is going back to Godhead.

### Parikshit Maharaj, curse, renunciation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/irlp-_ltNtg?t=527
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Let me now accept this notice of death which has come of its own accord by higher arrangement of providence or as devotees understand by arrangement of Krishna."
**Summary:** The speaker recounts the story of Parikshit Maharaj being cursed to die and his subsequent decision to renounce his kingdom and prepare for death on the banks of the Ganga.

Sri la prabhupada describing the personality of the great sages some of the great sages who assembled in that saintly assembly of great persons where pariksit maharaj had retired after renouncing his Indian religion his kingdom in order to prepare himself to go back to godhead at the end of seven days when he was due to die due to a snake bite due to a curse by the son of a brahmana a sage shamika rishi shamika rishi's son feeling that his father was insulted by pariksit he cursed pariksit to die within seven days by his snake bite because his father was insulted by pariksit taking a dead snake and putting it around the shoulder of his father who was sitting in meditation completely in trance unaware of the external surroundings so pariksit accepted the curse because he was insulted by pariksit he was an intelligent king he knew i have to die someday sooner or later let me now accept this notice of death which has come of its own accord by higher arrangement of providence or as devotees understand by arrangement of krishna the supreme personality caught in the ultimate controller of providence itself so let me accept that opportunity that I now have seven days to prepare myself being exclusively dedicated for such preparation by completely renouncing my kingdom, my family, my wealth, my material connections, everything material and simply sitting down on the bank of the Ganga and associating with saintly persons who naturally live in such holy places, themselves being engaged in spiritual activities meant exclusively for self-realization. So let me associate with them, take their good advice and accordingly follow their instructions to prepare myself to attain self-realization. This was Pariksit Maharaj.

**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "There is always the fear of death even for Lord Brahma what to speak of others so the duration of life is fantastically long but still they have to face death and for that they have to be prepared."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the long lifespans of beings in higher planets, including Brahma, but emphasizes that even they are not free from material miseries and the fear of death. They are always anxious about how to leave their bodies at the time of death to go back to Godhead.

This is a general description but there is a gradation so the mystic power possessed by great sages who are living in the topmost planets like maharloka janloka tapoloka satyaloka the topmost planets in this universe they have a very very very long duration of life some of them have a duration of life as much that of brahma himself some live for several kalpas a kalpa is a day of brahma which is 1000 cycle of four yugas on the earth planet 1000 cycle of four yugas is something like four billion years on the earth so there are saintly persons whose duration of life is four billion years we can't even imagine how long the duration of life they live but still a long duration of life does not mean that they are free from the material conditions of birth and death the material miseries of birth and death that is never absent even in the topmost planet called brahma loka so there is always the fear of death even for lord brahma what to speak of others so the duration of life is fantastically long but still they have to face death and for that they have to be prepared so they are always anxious how to actually leave this body at the time of death so as to be able to go back to godhead become free from the repetition of birth and death in this material world.

Further description vishwamitra is famous as gadi sutta because his father was gadi a powerful king of the province of kanyakubja a province of uttar pradesh although he was a kshatriya by birth he became a brahmana in the very same body by the power of his spiritual achievements. Vishwamitra was a very very great tapasvi he performed severe austerities and he became a brahmana became a brahmana means qualified to become a brahmana because of his tapasya. He picked a quarrel with vasishta muni when he was a kshatriya king. It is described briefly in the description under vasishta you would have heard this in a previous session that vasishta was having one kamadhenu one special cow which would give as much milk as one desires whenever one desires to have milk that's a kamadhenu and vasishta was using that milk given by kamadhenu the cow special cow for his performing daily sacrifice sacrifice means one has to offer huge quantities of ghee in a sacrificial fire of course the kind of sacrifice one does there are varieties of sacrifice one can do as a recommend in scriptures so if one performs a fire sacrifice one requires several ingredients of which the most important ingredient for a fire sacrifice is a cow ghee.

### sacrifice, universal welfare, sadhu
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/rMNcjJTIlDI?t=501
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The more the sacrifice that is performed the better the supply of necessities for the different people of this material world."
**Summary:** The speaker describes the process of making ghee for sacrifices and emphasizes the importance of sacrifice for universal welfare, explaining that the supply of necessities by nature is dependent on the performance of sacrifices. He defines a sadhu as someone who lives for the benefit and welfare of others.

So such cow ghee is obtained by taking the milk of a cow and then turning that milk into yogurt or curds dahi and churning that dahi to get butter and heating that butter to make it into ghee. Wonderful properties and qualities so such huge quantities of ghee are required for performing a fire sacrifice especially the saintly persons the sages they perform fire sacrifice for the sake of universal welfare see the way the universe is made is that our necessities are supplied according to the system of sacrifice that is carried out by the different human beings in different parts of this world and different parts of the universe so the more the sacrifice that is performed the better the supply of necessities for the different people of this material world. We cannot manufacture any of the things that are supplied by mother nature but the supply by nature is dependent on our performing sacrifice so the sages because they desire the welfare of all living beings they are engaged in welfare activity for others that is the definition of the sadhu one of the meanings of the word sadhu means a saintly person who lives for the benefit of others for the welfare of others so therefore a sadhu is always engaged in pious activities which guarantee sufficient supply of all the necessities for all the living entities in this material world by mother nature so vasishtha muni was having the kamadhenu.

### Kamadhenu, quarrel, austerity
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/rMNcjJTIlDI?t=640
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "When he realized his mistake Vishwamitra then he decided to retire from his kingly position and he decided to perform austerities to purify himself of all the reactions so he began very severe austerities."
**Summary:** The speaker continues the story of Vishwamitra and Vasishta, describing Vishwamitra's visit to Vasishta's ashram, his desire for the Kamadhenu, and the resulting quarrel. He explains that Vishwamitra forcibly took the cow, leading to a fight, and that after realizing his mistake, he retired from his kingly position and began performing austerities.

And this vishwamitra as the king was visiting was going to the forest and he happened to come to the ashram of vasishtha muni and he was received very nicely by vasishtha muni given a grand reception so he was curious how is this saintly person living in the forest to give me a grand reception while of course I am a king I am very pleased with the reception I have received but what is the source of wealth by which he has given such a grand reception so he found out this that Vasishtamuni had a kamadhenu so when he learned that Vasishtamuni had a kamadhenu he became very much desirous of getting such a kamadhenu now such kamadhenu is not available anywhere and everywhere so he decided that he is going to take away that kamadhenu from Vasishta and that is how a quarrel began so therefore Srila Prabhupada says he picked up a quarrel with Vasishtamuni when he was a Kshatriya king because Vasishtamuni he requires that cow for his sacrifice but this Vishwamitra forcibly took it away and then there was a big fight and so many things happened and some details are given elsewhere but it is just mentioned that when he realized his mistake Vishwamitra then he decided to retire from his kingly position. And he decided to perform austerities to purify himself of all the reactions so he began very severe austerities and he also performed a great sacrifice in cooperation with Matangamuni and thus was able to vanquish the sons of Vasishta. He became a great yogi and yet, he failed to check his senses and thus was obliged to become the father of Shakuntala the beauty queen of world history. So, he actually fathered a child, Shakuntala daughter.

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker summarizes the story of Vishwamitra, highlighting his visit to Vasishta's hermitage, his desire for the Kamadhenu, the quarrel, his defeat by Vasishta's spiritual strength, and his subsequent decision to become a brahmana through severe austerity.

So, Srila Prabhupada describes here, once as a Kshatriya king, he visited the hermitage of Vasishtha Muni. He was given a royal reception. Vishwamitra wanted Vasishtha's Kamadhenu named Nandini, but Vasishtha would not give it. Vishwamitra stole the cow and there is a quarrel between the sage Vasishtha and the king Vishwamitra. Vishwamitra was defeated by Vishwamitra and Vishwamitra was defeated by the spiritual strength of Vasishtha. Thus, Vishwamitra decided to become a brahmana and before becoming a brahmana, he underwent severe austerity on the bank of Kaushika. **Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Fogw_0WEKn8?t=64
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The lecture discusses the creation process, focusing on Brahma's birth from Vishnu's navel and his role as the first created being in the material world.

We are discussing the different sages who visited Pariksit Maharaj's temple. So, these are the verses. Pariksit Maharaj's are some of the six Tiya. Here she goes. universe at the beginning of creation so brahma is born on a lotus flower that emanates from Garbhodakashai Vishnu Padmanabha Vishnu. He is called Padmanabha. This Vishnu is called Padmanabha because from his navel one lotus flower comes out and on that lotus flower Brahma is born. So Brahma is also called Padmaja, one who is born on a lotus flower. He is the first created being. He is created directly by Vishnu because at the beginning of creation there is no one else other than Vishnu in this material world. Of course in the spiritual world which is eternal supreme lord and all his expansions, his devotees, eternal devotees, everyone is there always, but in the material world it is always created by Vishnu so Brahma is also called Padmanabha.

### creation, liberation, material energy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Fogw_0WEKn8?t=169
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So, creation two things happen. One is creating the material diversities or the varieties from the material energy in terms of the elements, earth, water, fire, air, sky and then the different planets and the varieties of living conditions on the planets, the rivers, the mountains, the forests, etc, etc, all that."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the process of creation, including the creation of material diversities and the rebirth of living beings who were not liberated during the previous destruction.

Created, maintained, and destroyed. So at the time of creation, first of all Brahma is born among the living beings who have to take birth again because they are not liberated in the previous previous time of destruction of this material world. Those who are not liberated, they are lying in a dormant condition in the material energy, mahat tattva. So, then they are again given birth, given bodies, given birth means given bodies by the arrangement of the Supreme Lord called creation. So, creation two things happen. One is creating the material diversities or the varieties from the material energy in terms of the elements, earth, water, fire, air, sky and then the different planets and the varieties of living conditions on the planets, the rivers, the mountains, the forests, etc, etc, all that. All the places of inhabitation of the living entities have to be first created.

**Verse References:** —

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The discussion moves to Brahma creating the Prajapatis, specifically mentioning Narada and Angira as mental sons of Brahma, and Angira being the father of Brihaspati, the priest of the demigods.

And that is done by Garbhodakashayi Vishnu. He creates the different diversities in this material world. Then Brahma is born from Vishnu and Brahma creates the first of the different state. the Prajapati's from his mind from his different parts of the body. So from his mind Narada is born and Angira is also a Rishi who is born from Brahma's mind. So therefore Angira Rishi is described as Manasa Putra mental son of Brahma at the beginning of creation and Narada is another Manasa Putra the son of Brahma. And Angira is also described as the father of Brihaspati the great learned priest of the demigods in the heavenly planets. Brihaspati is another great sage. He is the learned priest of the Devatas that means he advises the king of heaven Indra on all religious matters spiritual matters. He advises King Indra as well as the Devatas. Angira was born of the semen of Brahmaji given to a sender of fire. So the method of birth is extraordinary in these cases.

**Summary:** The speaker describes Angira's continued austerity in Alokananda, a holy place in the Himalayas, and emphasizes the Himalayas' significance as the holiest place on Earth, specifically referring to the land of India.

Because Brahma is empowered to create in different ways. Utatya and Sambhartha are his sons, Angira's sons. It is said that Angira Rishi is still performing austerity and chanting the holy name of the Lord at a place known as Alokananda on the bank of the Ganges, especially in the Himalayas. There are crores of holy places. How many? Crores of holy places. Koti, Koti, Koti. In fact, it is not an exaggeration if we say every spot in the Himalayas is actually a holy place. That is the significance of the Himalayan mountains. That is why this tract of land which is having Himalayas to the north and ocean or seas on the three other sides is actually a very special tract of land. It is a very special tract of land. It is the most holy land on the entire earth planet. This what we now call as India. The most holy place.

Next there is a description of Parishurama. He is the son of Maharishi Jamadagni and Srimati Renuka. He is also known as Renuka Sutta by his mother's name, Renuka Sutta, the son of Renuka. He is one of the powerful incarnations of God. In the Dasha Avatara Stotra his name is mentioned as one of the Leela Avatara's, past time incarnations of the Lord. As a past time what did he do? ### Parashurama, Kshatriyas, righteousness, axe
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Fogw_0WEKn8?t=662
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "He went round simply chopping off the heads of all these miscreant Kshatriyas. He went round the world and simply killed all these Kshatriyas."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that Parashurama eliminated the Kshatriyas because they were exploiting the citizens and not ruling righteously, and he did so with an axe.

When the earth was overburdened by Kshatriyas who were posing as kings but they were actually very very puffed up with power and they were not properly ruling in a righteous way. They were exploiting, they were exploiting the citizens. So Parashurama became very angry at these Kshatriyas and he decided to clean up the earth planet, get rid of such Kshatriyas and therefore he killed them completely, almost completely by going round the earth twenty one times. He just had one Parashu. He is called Parashurama. His name is Parashurama but he is he is named as Parashurama because there are three Ramas mentioned in the Dashavatarasthotra. All three incarnations are known as Rama but depending on the specific quality or characteristic they are distinguished. The first Rama mentioned is Parashurama, the son of Maharshi Jamadagni and Renuka. He is having a a weapon called Parashu. Parashu means an axe, axe as a weapon. With that one Parashu, one axe, he went round simply chopping off the heads of all these miscreant Kshatriyas. He went round the world and simply killed all these Kshatriyas.

### Parashurama, Ramachandra, Balarama, incarnations
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Fogw_0WEKn8?t=778
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The second Rama is Parashurama. The third Rama is the famous Lord Ramachandra. He is called Kodandarama. He had a bow and some arrows and he is to use that bow and arrows as his chief weapon for fighting as a Kshatriya."
**Summary:** The speaker distinguishes between three Ramas: Parashurama (with an axe), Ramachandra (with a bow and arrows), and Balarama (with a plough).

So he is called Parashurama. The second Rama is Parashurama. The third Rama is the famous Lord Ramachandra. He is called Kodandarama. He had a bow and some arrows and he is to use that bow and arrows as his chief weapon for fighting as a Kshatriya. And the third Rama is called Balarama or Halayudharama. His weapon was a plough. With that plough once Balarama when he was insulted by the Kurus, Kauravas, then he decided to teach them a lesson by using his plough. He decided to chip off the Hastinapura city with the Kshatriyas with the Kauravas and put drown that in the Yamuna River. So when he just began to carve out this tract of land called Hastinapura then the Kauravas realized their mistake and they went to him and begged forgiveness and immediately he forgave them and he restored that land back in its place. So this is the these are the pastimes of the Supreme Lord in his various incarnations. So he is called Balarama or Halayudharama.

---
### Parashurama, Kshatriyas, sin, liberation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Fogw_0WEKn8?t=888
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So in order to teach us that killing is not good, so he himself underwent severe austerities in order to free himself from the sin of killing so many millions of Kshatriyas."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that although Parashurama's killing of the Kshatriyas was justified, he still underwent austerities to teach that killing is sinful, even though those killed by God are liberated.

So this Parshurama he killed the Kshatriya community as old 21 times. With the blood of the Kshatriyas he pleased the souls of his forefathers. Later on this is at the Mahendra Parvata because he wanted to teach even though his killing the Kshatriya was justified but still the killing business is very very sinful activity. Killing is sinful. So in order to teach us that killing is not good, so he himself underwent severe austerities in order to free himself from the sin of killing so many millions of Kshatriyas. Actually there is no sin on his part because he himself is the incarnational Supreme Lord. So there is no sin because whoever he kills will be liberated, will be purified. Being killed by God one becomes liberated, purified of all sinful reactions. So there is no sin on the part of the Supreme Lord when the Supreme Lord kills any person in this world. So it is a favor done by the Supreme Lord upon a soul, a sinful soul because by killing that sinful soul the Supreme Lord will put an end to that person's sinful activity and also purify him of sinful reactions and also liberate him from this material bondage. So there is more description. We will hear more about Parchurama in another session. Thank you very much Hare Krishna.

## SB 1.19.9-10 Part 6 | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | 14-05-2023 | Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### death, spiritual journey, Krishna consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/rncKDWYS4Yw?t=34
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "One in knowledge is able to face death perfectly by understanding that time has come to leave this body and move on."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the importance of understanding death as a transition for the spirit soul and the need to prepare for it with Krishna consciousness.

Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya Narayanam Namaskritya Naram Jai Vanarottamam Devim Sarasvatim Vyasam Tato Jayam Udhirayet. In this session, we will be discussing from the 9th and 10th verses of the 19th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. This is a continuation of the discussion of the same two verses which we have done in the past five sessions. We completed part 5 of the discussion of these two verses because it is a long purport. So, this session will be the part 6 of these two verses which we will be discussing. These two verses are listing the great sages who arrived from different parts of the universe to witness the great emperor Mahabhagavata, pure devotee Parikshit Maharaj, put his body in perfect Krishna consciousness. So, this quitting the body, which means when death comes, we have to leave the body. We means each one of us is spirit, spirit soul. So, we have to leave the body and we have to leave this body. So, one in knowledge is able to face death perfectly by understanding that time has come to leave this body and move on. So, in that sense.

---
### spiritual destination, liberation, Krishna's abode
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/rncKDWYS4Yw?t=175
**Verse References:** BG 8.16
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Ultimately liberation means to fix our destination as Krishna's personal abode and travel this spiritual which leads us to that specific destination Krishna Krishna Krishna dhama, Goloka dhama."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that life is a journey with Krishna's personal abode as the ultimate destination, emphasizing liberation as fixing our destination and traveling spiritually towards Krishna dhama, Goloka dhama.

Life is a journey, so it's a journey we have undertaken in order to reach a final destination, just like you travel, whenever you travel, you have a certain destination you want to reach exactly. Like that, if life is understood to be a journey, then our destination should be Krishna's personal abode. In the Bhagavad-gita and several places, including the verse I just cited, Krishna says his abode, personal abode, is such a place having gone where we will never come back to this material world. So, ultimately liberation means to fix our destination as Krishna's personal abode and travel this spiritual which leads us to that specific destination Krishna Krishna Krishna dhama, Goloka dhama, nija.

Material life means, material existence means, anudarshanam, repeatedly we experience these four things, whether Brahmaloka or Maharloka or some Tapoloka, Siddhaloka, Bhuloka, Patarloka, every place in this material creation, in this material universe, in this material world, is a place of repeated birth, disease, old age and death, whereas the spiritual world is that place where there is no misery, no birth, no death, no disease, no old age, no suffering, no scarcity, no deterioration. ## SB 1.19.9-10 Part 7 | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | 15-05-2023 | Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### righteous rule, duty, Krishna's instructions
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/XjzRDBgQSkc?t=46
**Verse References:** BG
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The Pandavas, in order to obey the instructions of Krishna that righteous people should rule this world, then only the people will be guided properly and will actually benefit from the rule of such righteous persons like Maharaja Yudhishthira."
**Summary:** This segment discusses Parshurama's presence at Yudhishthira's coronation, emphasizing the importance of righteous leaders as instructed by Krishna.

In this session, we continue to discuss from the 9th and 10th verses of the 19th chapter of the first canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. We were discussing in the previous session about Parshurama. So, Parshurama has been described as a brahmana who was expert in the military science, Dhanurveda. Therefore, he taught that military science to Dronacharya, who further instructed that military science to the Pandavas and Kauravas. It is described Parshurama was present during the coronation of Maharaja Yudhishthira, and he celebrated the function along with other great rishis. The saintly persons know very well that the Pandavas are the righteous persons to rule the world, not the Kauravas, especially Duryodhana. It is described in the Bhagavad Gita, he was wicked, crooked minded, evil minded. So, he was not the fit person to rule, Duryodhana. So, therefore, the Pandavas, in order to obey the instructions of Krishna that righteous people should rule this world, then only the people will be guided properly and will actually benefit from the rule of such righteous persons like Maharaja Yudhishthira. So, Parshurama celebrated the function of coronation of Maharaja Yudhishthira after the battle of Kurukshetra along with other great sages. The sages are always there to advise, instruct, and guide the Kshatriya kings.

### time, devotee worship, respect
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/XjzRDBgQSkc?t=172
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "If somebody commits an offense at Krishna's lotus feet, one may beg forgiveness from Krishna and Krishna will pardon very easily, but if somebody offends Krishna's pure devotee, Krishna will never forgive, never pardon."
**Summary:** This segment explains Parshurama's longevity due to different time scales on higher planets and emphasizes the importance of respecting Krishna's devotees, even more than Krishna Himself, because offenses against devotees are not easily forgiven.

Now Srila Prabhupada says, Parshurama is so old that he met both Rama and Krishna at different times. He is a very, very old personality. Several millions of years they lived because their time scale is different from the time scale that we are subject to on this earth planet. To give an example, Brahma's one day consists of a day and a night. A complete cycle of a day and a night of Brahma is something like two thousand cycles of our four yugas. That is Brahma's day and night. So that comes to some 8 billions of years. So 8 billions of years is Brahma's just one day and night. So the time scale on different planets is different than the time scale of Brahma. The time scale of different planets is different. So, Parashurama is a resident of a higher planet. So, he doesn't, his body doesn't follow the time cycle, time scale of this earth planet. So, his life span will be very, very, very, very long. It is described that he fought with Rama, that is Lord Ramachandra, but he accepted Krishna as a Supreme Personality of Godhead. He also praised Arjuna when he saw Arjuna with Krishna, because Arjuna is a celebrated devotee of Krishna. If Krishna is respected as a Supreme Personality of Godhead, definitely one must also offer respect to Krishna. Respect to Krishna's pure devotees like Arjuna, because if somebody commits an offense at Krishna's lotus feet, one may beg forgiveness from Krishna and Krishna will pardon very easily. But if somebody offends Krishna's pure devotee, Krishna will never forgive, never pardon. Even if you ask Krishna forgiveness, Krishna will not pardon. So, therefore, it is very important to respect not only Krishna, but Krishna's pure devotees also. And they should be offered as much respect as we offer to Krishna.

---
### devotee worship, spiritual master, humility
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/XjzRDBgQSkc?t=338
**Verse References:** SB 11
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna says you should respect them more than what respect you offer to me."
**Summary:** This segment continues the discussion on respecting devotees, stating Krishna's instruction to Uddhava that devotees are more worshipable than Him. It also explains the exalted position of the spiritual master as non-different from Krishna and worthy of the respect due to all demigods.

In fact, Krishna says the opposite. He says, as far as my devotees are concerned, you should not just respect them as much as you respect me. Krishna says you should respect them more than what respect you offer to me. Krishna says in the eleventh category of Bhagavatam while instructing Uddhava, madbhakta puja abhyadhika. What does it mean? My devotee is more worshipable than me. So Krishna is thoroughly pleased if we worship Krishna's devotee and show respect more than what we show to Krishna. Of course, one should not do that, that you disrespect Krishna or show some disregard to Krishna. It should never be done. But nevertheless, a devotee should be respected. Devotee should be respected at least as much as we offer respect to Krishna, because Krishna is supremely worshipable, a devotee is also supremely worshipable. A pure devotee of Krishna is more worshipable than all the demigods put together. In the eleventh canto, Krishna says, acharya mambhijaniya sarva-devamayo guruhu. That is spiritual master is described as acharya and guru. In this particular verse, and Krishna says, the acharya or the spiritual, bona fide spiritual master is non-different from me, and he is to be respected as the sum total of all the demigods. So he is very, very exalted position. So Parshurama actually glorified Arjuna. He not only glorified Krishna, but he also glorified Arjuna, the pure devotee of Krishna.

### vows, duty, guru-disciple relationship
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/XjzRDBgQSkc?t=476
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** This segment narrates the story of Amba, Bhishma, and Parshurama, detailing Amba's desire to marry Bhishma after being kidnapped, Bhishma's refusal due to his vow of celibacy, and Amba's subsequent appeal to Parshurama to intervene.

Then further Srila Prabhupada says, when Bhishma refused to marry Amba, who wanted him to become her husband, Amba met Parshurama. See the history is given in the Mahabharata that Bhishma wanted to arrange the marriage of his step-brother, Vijitravirya. So in order to get a suitable princess to marry this prince, Vijitravirya, the son of Satyavati and Shantanu. So Bhishma arranged that he went to some place where some princesses were about to be married, and he kidnapped them and brought them to be married to Vijitravirya. Of the three princesses, Amba, Ambika and Ambalika, two of them were married to Vijitravirya. But Amba refused to marry Vijitravirya. Amba's argument was that Bhishma has kidnapped her. So she wanted to marry Bhishma. But Bhishma refused to marry Amba because he had taken a vow that he is going to remain a Brahmachari, a celibate, rest of his life. So he refused to marry Amba. And then Amba insisted that she is not going to marry Vijitravirya, that is Bhishma's stepbrother, but she only wants to marry Bhishma. So when Bhishma refused, then Amba went to Parshurama and requested him to force Bhishma to marry her. So then Parshurama asked Bhishma, you should marry Amba because she has been kidnapped by you. And then as a chaste woman, she will not accept anybody else as her husband other than the one who has kidnapped her. So when Bhishma refused to obey the order of Parshurama, because Bhishma had learned the military science from Parshurama, so Parshurama was one of the gurus of Bhishma, spiritual masters. So Parshurama, as the spiritual master, he ordered Bhishma, you should marry Amba. But Bhishma refused because he had taken a vow of celibacy. So then both of them fought very severely, Bhishma and Parshurama.

## SB 1.19.9-10 Part 8 | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | 16-05-2023 | Daily Evening Bhagavatam Discourse
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### Vedic knowledge, Vanashrama Dharma, Guidance of sages
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/FwUpznOZO8Q?t=56
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Following the Vanashrama Dharma is very, very beneficial for elevation to the higher status in life."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the role of sages in guiding rulers and the benefits of following the Vanashrama Dharma for elevation in life, using Medha Tithi as an example.

In this session, we continue to discuss from the 9th and 10th verses of the 19th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. This is part 8 of the discussion. This is the last session in which we will complete the discussion on these two verses. So we are discussing different sages who appeared there. Srila Prabhupada has given a brief biography of these sages. So the first chapter of the 19th chapter of the 1st canto of Srimad Bhagavatam. So the next sage, Medha Tithi, an old sage of yore, an assembly member of the heavenly king Indra Deva. These devatas, they are always guided by great saintly persons. This is the system. Any ruler, any king, any responsible person in the administration is always guided by learned sages who never occupy the post of ruling or controlling or doing administration, but are well-versed in the science. So they give advice, they instruct, they guide, but they never accept any salary, they never take any remuneration. They don't expect any material benefit out of that. They are doing it as a seva, as a service. So this Medha Tithi was an assembly member of the heavenly king Indra Deva. His son was Kanvamuni, another great sage. This Kanvamuni brought up Shakuntala in the forest. And Shakuntala was left behind. And Shakuntala was left behind by Menaka. This Medha Tithi was promoted to the heavenly planet by strictly following the principles of retired life or vanaprastha. Following the Vanashrama Dharma is very, very beneficial for elevation to the higher status in life.

---
### Bhakti Yoga, Liberation, Gradual elevation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/FwUpznOZO8Q?t=190
**Verse References:** BG 8
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "But the Bhakti Yoga process is directly go back to Godhead by completely concentrating one's attention on Krishna alone."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts the gradual process of elevation through different planets with the direct path of Bhakti Yoga, emphasizing the importance of concentrating on Krishna for liberation.

Of course, the ultimate goal of Satcharita. Such elevation is to come to the point of achieving ultimate liberation by going back to Godhead. But those who adopt the gradual process of elevation of consciousness, they have to gradually go from the earth planet to the heavenly planet to the still higher planet, still higher planet, still higher planet. Bhūloka, Bhūvarloka, Svarloka or Svargaloka. Bhūloka, Bhūvarloka, Svargaloka or Svargaloka. Maharloka, Janaloka, Tapoloka, Satyaloka. This is step-by-step elevation. But the Bhakti Yoga process is directly go back to Godhead by completely concentrating one's attention on Krishna alone. This is explained by Krishna in the 8th chapter. That one who remembers me alone. That one who remembers me alone at the time of death. Such a person when he quits the body. He directly attains shelter at my lotus feet in my personal above. He is directly transformed to Krishnas personal above. So, those who don't know about this Bhakti Yoga process. Or, those who are not having the guidance of the spiritual master or master as the laity. Master who is expert in Bhakti Yoga, such people have to follow the gradual process of elevation, the other process of Jnana, Karma, Ashtanga Yoga, Hatha Yoga, so many other, Sankhi Yoga, so many other yoga systems are there for the gradual process of elevation.

---

Next, Devala, a great authority like Narada Muni and Vyasa Deva, his good name is on the list of authorities mentioned in the Bhagavad Gita. Krishna, sorry, Arjuna, when he acknowledges Krishna's position as a supreme Brahman, Arjuna is saying that Krishna is accepted as a supreme Brahman, the supreme personality of Godhead, by many great saintly persons who are authorities in Vedic knowledge. Narada, the foremost authority. Then, Asita Devala Vyasa, so Devala's name is mentioned as one of the authorities by Arjuna who all acknowledge Krishna is the supreme personality of Godhead. This Devala Rishi, he met Maharaja Yudhishthira after the battle of Kurukshetra, and this Devala Rishi was the elder brother of Dhaumya, the priest of the Pandava family. Like the Kshatriyas, he also allowed his daughter to select her own husband in a Swayamvara meeting. And at that ceremony, all the bachelor sons of the rishis were invited. Next, Gautama, one of the seven great sages of the universe. In every universe, there is a position of Saptarishis. They are elevated to that post, they have their own planet in the upper part of the universe, Saptarishi Mandala. It's called Saptarishi Mandala. And Gautama Rishi is one of the Saptarishis.

**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Persons in the Gautama gotra today are either his family descendants or in his disciplic succession."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the concept of 'gotra' in Vedic culture, focusing on Gautama gotra and its two types of descendants: family descendants and those in disciplic succession. He then introduces the story of Ahalya, wife of Gautama.

Sharadwaan Gautama was one of his sons. Now, Srila Prabhupada clarifies one very important point. Persons in the Gautama gotra today are either his family descendants or in his disciplic succession. Like those in the Vedic culture, they say, to which gotra do you belong? To which lineage? Lineage of ancestors. So, those belonging to Gautama gotra, generally a rishi's name is associated with the gotra. Gotra means this descendant of a particular sage. So, two kinds of descendants. Those who are family descendants are the brahmanas. Just like Gautama Rishi, he had his sons and his sons had their sons, their sons, their sons, their sons. Like that in the brahmana lineage, present day brahmanas who are actually Gautama gotra descendants, they are coming from the Gautama Rishi himself as the descendants. And those who are Kshatriyas and Vaishyas who claim to be of Gautama gotra, they generally are having Gautama Rishi as their priest, as their preceptor, as their guide and saintly teacher. So, two kinds of descendants, those in the disciplic succession who follow Gautama Rishi and those who are family descendants, seminal descendants and the descendants who are having the spiritual guidance given by Gautama Rishi. Gautama Rishi was the husband of the famous Ahalya. Ahalya is a personality who was delivered by Rama or Ramachandra. So, it is described here that Ahalya turned into a stone when Indradeva, the king of heaven, molested her. You see, among the devatas, there is always this enviousness

---
### Ahalya, Indra, Morality, Kripacharya
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/FwUpznOZO8Q?t=625
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "This is the instruction in the scriptures that another's wife should be respected on the level of one's mother."
**Summary:** The speaker narrates the story of Ahalya and Indra, emphasizing the importance of respecting another's wife. He then transitions to discussing Kripacharya, Gautama's grandson, and his role in the battle of Kurukshetra and later as a guide for Yudhishthira.

because there is so much material desires for enjoyment. Sometimes they become envious. Ahalya was the wife of Gautama Rishi. But because Indra wanted to enjoy with her, because she is a chaste lady, she will refuse to satisfy Indra. This is the instruction in the scriptures that another's wife should be respected on the level of one's mother. So, there is a question of enjoying. So, similarly, Indradeva, he should not have even approached the wife of Gautama Ahalya even though she might have been very beautiful. But he nevertheless approached her. When she refused, he molested her. He forced her. So, then she, by her chastity, she turned into a stone so that Indra could no longer do any more harm to her. And then she was delivered by Rama. When Rama touched her, she was delivered. When Rama was in exile. Ahalya was delivered by Lord Ramachandra. Gautama was the grandfather of Kripacharya who was one of the heroes of the battle of Kurukshetra. Kripacharya was fighting on the side of the Kauravas. But Kripacharya was the brother-in-law of Dronacharya. Kripacharya's twin sister married Dronacharya. So, Dronacharya's wife's brother is Kripacharya. So, Kripacharya did not die in the battle of Kurukshetra, even though he was fighting on the side of the Kauravas. So, later on, Kripacharya actually became the guide for Yudhishthira. There was no enmity as such. They would fight on one side or the other side. There was no enmity actually. So, Kripacharya agreed to become the, because he was a very learned sage, so he became the guide for Yudhishthira after Yudhishthira was coronated as the emperor of the world after the battle of Kurukshetra.

---
### Maitreya Rishi, Vidura, Brahmavidya, Parampara
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/FwUpznOZO8Q?t=788
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "You have to hear from a Self-realized soul. That is the only way and there is no other way."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces Maitreya Rishi and his role as the spiritual master of Vidura, emphasizing the importance of hearing from a self-realized soul to acquire Brahmavidya and highlighting the parampara system.

So, Kripacharya's illustrious lineage is Kripacharya's grandfather was Gautama Rishi, the great Gautama Rishi. Then the last sage being described here is Maitreya Rishi, a great Rishi of yoga. He was the spiritual master of Vidura. In fact, most of the Bhagavatam, a good portion of the Bhagavatam is describing the conversation between Vidura and Maitreya, which is cited by Shukadeva Goswami. When Shukadeva Goswami was speaking the Bhagavatam to Pariksit Maharaj, to answer some of Pariksit's questions, Shukadeva Goswami would cite a similar question was asked by someone else in the past. This is called parampara system. Nobody has to manufacture any answer for any question. The entire body of spiritual knowledge is descending from Krishna. Krishna first instructed Brahma, Brahma instructed Narada, Narada instructed Vyasa, Vyasa instructed Deja. Shukadeva Goswami and other personalities. So Vidura inquired from Maitreya Rishi when on pilgrimage. He met Maitreya Rishi in Badrikashram. And he inquired from Maitreya Rishi. Of course, Vidura himself is enlightened and learned, pure devotee, Mahabhagavata. He is one of the Mahajanas. But in order to teach us how we have to make inquiries from the right authority, from the right source, we have to acquire this knowledge of the Self, Brahmavidya. You cannot study Brahmavidya or become knowledgeable in the Brahmavidya simply by studying books, simply by doing some meditation and all that. You have to hear from a Self-realized soul. That is the only way and there is no other way. There is no substitute for that. So Vidura heard from Maitreya who was Self-realized. And that conversation is elaborately described in the later cantos of the Bhagavad Gita. So Maitreya Rishi is famous. As the one who instructed Vidura in this spiritual subject matter, Brahmavidya.

---
### Maitreya Rishi, Dhritarashtra, Duryodhana, Advice
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/FwUpznOZO8Q?t=955
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Maitreya advised Dhritarashtra to keep good relations with the Pandavas."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes the discussion on Maitreya Rishi by recounting his advice to Dhritarashtra regarding the Pandavas and his interaction with Duryodhana, leading to a curse. He also mentions Maitreya's religious discourses with Vyasadeva.

Maitreya advised Dhritarashtra to keep good relations with the Pandavas. And Dhritarashtra was plotting and planning to kill the Pandavas. Actually, Dhritarashtra was not planning to kill the Pandavas. It was Duryodhana who wanted to kill the Pandavas. So Dhritarashtra, instead of stopping his sons from attempting to kill the Pandavas, he supported his sons. That is when the others intervened. Like Maitreya Rishi, he advised Dhritarashtra not to ill-treat the Pandavas because Dhritarashtra was a caretaker. So not to ill-treat his brother's sons, his nephews, but rather to keep good relations. Duryodhana disagreed and thus Maitreya Rishi cursed Duryodhana. Maitreya also met Vyasadeva and had religious discourses with Vyasadeva.

## Sri Jayananda Prabhu Goes Back to Godhead | HH Stoka Krishna Swami
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### devotional service, Krishna consciousness, preparing for death
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/93mZn4btO8c?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Because he was engaged always in Krishna Consciousness, so he went back to God."
**Summary:** The speaker describes how Jayananda Prabhu, knowing he was dying of cancer, focused on Krishna consciousness through chanting, hearing, and association with devotees to prepare for death and ultimately return to Godhead.

Jayananda Prabhu was always engaged, after coming in touch with Prabhupada, in devotional service, as Prabhupada instructed him. Towards the end of his life, when he realized he had cancer, he knew that very soon he was going to die, quit his body. He did not want to take treatment in the hospital, but he came back to the temple and he requested devotees to help him to always remember Krishna. He diverted his mind away from the pain and the complications of the disease towards hearing and chanting about Krishna, about the holy name of Krishna, all of the pastimes of Krishna, and engaging with devotees in the temple. In this way, he was preparing to quit the body. Death actually came, he was completely absorbed in Krishna Consciousness. Prabhupada confirmed because he was engaged always in Krishna Consciousness, so he went back to God.

---

### incarnation, Varaha, purpose
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2nz6Wqgi8ac?t=39
**Verse References:** SB 1.3.7
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "There cannot be any incarnation without a particular function. Every incarnation has got a very specific function and such functions are always extraordinary."
**Summary:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami introduces Lord Varahadeva as an extraordinary boar incarnation, citing Srimad Bhagavatam 1.3.7, explaining the Lord's purpose in lifting the Earth from the lower regions of the universe, a feat impossible for any ordinary being.

A very extraordinary incarnation of the Lord in the form of a transcendental boar incarnation. I will be reciting a verse from the Bhagavatam. Bhagavatam contains a nice description of Lord Varahadeva's appearance and pastimes. The first canto third chapter entitled Krishna is the source of all incarnations. In this chapter there is a description of the Varaha incarnation in the seventh verse: Dviteeyam tu bhavayasya Dviteeyam tu bhavayasya Dviteeyam tu bhavayasya Rasatala Gatam Mahim Uddharshan Upadatta Yajnesha Savukaram Vapuhu. The meaning of this verse is, the supreme enjoyer of all sacrifices, Yajnesha, accepted the incarnation of a boar, Savukaram Vapuhu, the second incarnation, Dviteeyam tu, and for the welfare of the earth, he lifted the earth from the nether regions of the universe. So in the purport Srila Prabhupada explains regarding this appearance of the lord as a boar. The indication is that for each and every incarnation of the personality of Godhead, the particular function executed is also mentioned. So this verse says why did the lord appear as Varaha? To lift the earth from the lower regions of the universe. There cannot be any incarnation of the lord to lift the earth from the lower regions of the universe. There cannot be any incarnation without a particular function. Every incarnation has got a very specific function and such functions are always extraordinary. Lifting the entire earth planet out of the depths of the Garbhodaka ocean simply by lifting it on the snout. You can imagine how big the Varaha form of the Lord would have been simply to support the earth between the two tusks on the snout and lift it effortlessly as a Leela and place it back perfectly in the orbit. That is what the Lord did. So, such a Leela. Extraordinary activities are impossible for any living being to perform. This is very important. That nobody else can do it. No yogi, no jnani, no karmi, no devata, no asura. Nobody can do it. The incarnation of the Bohr was to take the earth out of Pluto's region of filthy matter. The bottom of the universe. From there, the earth was to be lifted up. Picking up something from a filthy place is done by a Bohr. And the all-powerful personality of Godhead displayed this wonder to the asuras who had hidden the earth in such a filthy place. Particularly the asura by name Hiranyaksha.

---
### Hiranyaksha, earth, transcendence
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2nz6Wqgi8ac?t=254
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "We should never think that this form of the Lord as Bohr is similar to the Bohr form in this material world."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on Hiranyaksha's role in hiding the Earth and the necessity of Lord Varaha's appearance to rescue it, emphasizing the transcendental nature of the Lord's form and activities, distinct from mundane perceptions.

He had hidden the earth in the filthy place. And the earth is the place of residence for the human beings and many other varieties of living entities. But at the beginning of this Manvantara, when the earth was to be populated, then the earth itself was in the bottom of the Garbhodaka ocean, Garbha ocean. So to pick it up was required because the earth is the place of residence for so many living beings in different life forms. There is nothing impossible for the personality of Godhead. And although he played the part of a Bohr, by the devotees he is worshipped. Nobody worships a hog. But the Lord, when he appears in this particular form, he is always worshipable in all forms because all forms are transcendental. We should never think that this form of the Lord as Bohr is similar to the Bohr form in this material world. No. The form of the Lord is always transcendental. It is very beautiful. It is very very extraordinary. So devotees worship the Lord even in the form of Bohr. And the Lord is always staying in transcendence. He always remains in the spiritual platform. He never becomes affected by the material nature. He never is influenced by material nature. On the other hand, he is always the controller of material nature. And he remains the Supreme Lord under all circumstances, eternally, always, in any form he may appear. Just for the sake of his Leela, he assumes different forms.

---
### incarnations, Brahma, Hiranyaksha
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2nz6Wqgi8ac?t=394
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Only under certain conditions do the planets float as weightless balls in the air."
**Summary:** The lecture transitions to a verse from the second canto, seventh chapter, spoken by Brahma, describing Lord Varaha lifting the Earth and killing Hiranyaksha. It also discusses the eternal conflict between demons and demigods, identifying Brahma as the first Devata and Hiranyaksha as the first Asura.

Then, in one more place, in the second canto, seventh chapter, which describes the scheduled incarnations, the first words are spoken by Brahma. Yathrodhyata shiti taloddharanaya vibhrat kraudim tanum sakalayajnamayim anantah antar maharnava upagatam adhidaityam tam damstraya adhrim iva vajradharo dadhara. The meaning of this verse is, Lord Brahma is speaking, when the unlimitedly powerful Lord assumed the form of a Bohr as a pastime, just to lift the planet Earth, which was drowned in the great ocean of the universe called the Garbhodaka. The first demon, Hiranyaksha, appeared and the Lord pierced him with his tusk. This Hiranyaksha had hidden the Earth in the bottom of the Garbhodaka ocean. So the Lord came to lift this Earth. And when Hiranyaksha prevented the Lord from rescuing the Earth, then there was a fight between the Lord, Lord Varaha and Hiranyaksha. And Hiranyaksha was delivered simply by the Lord piercing with his tusk, Hiranyaksha's huge powerful body. So in the purport to this verse, Srila Prabhupada says, since the beginning of creation, the demons and demigods or the Vaishnavas are always two classes of living beings to dominate the planets of the universe. The demons, Asuras and Suras. Sura means the Devatas. They are all Vaishnavas. They are all Vishnubhakta. Vishnubhakta spruto daiva. That is the definition. So among the Devatas, Lord Brahma is the first Devata. And among the demons in this universe, Hiranyaksha is the first demon in this universe. Brahma and Hiranyaksha. The first Devata and the first Asura.

---
### planetary balance, exploitation, Garbhodaka ocean
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2nz6Wqgi8ac?t=542
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "The modernized drilling of the earth to exploit oil from within is a sort of disturbance by the modern demons."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the conditions under which planets float, likening modern oil exploration to the disturbances caused by Hiranyaksha, potentially disrupting the Earth's balance and highlighting the Lord's role in maintaining it.

Only under certain conditions do the planets float as weightless balls in the air. The planets are gigantic bodies. They float in the air only under specific conditions. And as soon as these conditions are disturbed, the planets may fall down in the Garbhodaka ocean, which covers half the universe. The universe is like a huge shell. And half the shell is covered by water called Garbhodaka ocean. And the planets are above. That is what is explained here. The other half is a spherical dome. Within which the innumerable planetary systems exist. The floating of the planets in the weightless air is due to the inner constitution of the globes. And the modernized drilling of the earth to exploit oil from within is a sort of disturbance by the modern demons. So much oil exploration is going on. So much oil is being taken out of the earth. But then it does disturb the conditions under which the earth is floating in the atmosphere. So this disturbance by modern demons can result in a greatly harmful reaction to the floating condition of the earth. So it is not humanly possible. Neither it is possible by any Devata or anybody else other than the Supreme Lord to maintain this balance of the earth remaining in the atmosphere above the Garbhodaka ocean. And the earth also moving in its orbit perfectly. That is facilitated by the Lord.

### Hiranyaksha, gold, Dasavatara Stotra
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2nz6Wqgi8ac?t=667
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The earth was detached from its weightless condition and fell down into the Garbhodaka ocean due to the mischief of this Asura, Hiranyaksha."
**Summary:** The speaker connects Hiranyaksha's exploitation of gold to the Earth's fall into the Garbhodaka ocean, then introduces Jayadev Goswami's Dasavatara Stotra, specifically the verse describing Lord Varaha holding the Earth on his tusks.

A similar disturbance created formerly by the demons headed by Hiranyaksha. Hiranyaksha is described to be the great exploiter of the gold rush. It means he was, Hiranya means gold. So he was interested in gold. Why? Because Asuras want to enjoy unlimitedly, sense gratification. And they require huge amounts of money for that. Therefore this Hiranyaksha was always after gold. And therefore he is called Hiranyaksha, the exploiter of the gold rush. And the earth was detached from its weightless condition and fell down into the Garbhodaka ocean due to the mischief of this Asura, Hiranyaksha. The Lord as maintainer of the whole creation of the material world therefore assumed the gigantic form of a boar with a proportionate snout and picked up the earth from the water of the Garbhodaka. The Lord went inside the water and picked up the earth from the bottom of the Garbhodaka ocean. Sri Jayadev Goswami, the great Vaishnava poet, has sung as follows. Jayadev Goswami has written a song called Dasavatara Stotra. In that Dasavatara Stotra, the third verse describes the incarnation of the Lord as Varaha. Vasati dashana shikhare dharanitava lagna Shashini kalanka kaleva nimagna Kesava drita shukara roopa jaya jagadishahare. This is the third verse sung by Jayadev Goswami in glorification of Supreme Lord.

### Jayadev Goswami, incarnation, imagination
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2nz6Wqgi8ac?t=791
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "The incarnation of the Lord is not the concocted idea of fanciful men who create an incarnation out of imagination."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the meaning of Jayadev Goswami's verse, comparing the Earth on Lord Varaha's tusks to the moon with spots, and emphasizes that the Lord's incarnations are not mere human imagination but occur under extraordinary circumstances.

Jayadev Goswami says, O Keshava, O Supreme Lord, who assumed the form of a boar, O Lord, the planet earth rested on your tusks and it appeared like the moon engraved with spots. So here is a comparison When the Lord lifted the earth, the earth was resting on his tusks, then the Lord appeared very very beautiful. Shashini kalanka kaleva nimagna. So therefore Srila Prabhupada says, Such is the symptom of an incarnation of the Lord. The incarnation of the Lord is not the concocted idea of fanciful men who create an incarnation out of imagination. The incarnation of the Lord appears under certain extraordinary circumstances. Like the above mentioned occasion. Above mentioned occasion is when there was a need for the Lord to annihilate this demon Hiranyaksha. Then the Lord appears as Varaha and he kills this demon Hiranyaksha. The incarnation performs a task which is not even imaginable by the tiny brain of mankind. We have all got brains, but it is beyond our comprehension how the Lord can appear in the form of a boar or Varaha. How he can perform so many pious activities. We cannot imagine what all the kind of activities he performs. Extraordinary, extraordinary. But for him all activities are leela, pastimes. There is no exertion on his part. Because he is unlimitedly powerful. The modern creators of the many cheap incarnations may take note of the factual incarnation of God as a gigantic boar. So these modern people who think that they can concoct some idea about Varaha in their own way. No, that is not Varaha. Varaha is the specific form of the Lord who is incarnated in order to bless the people of this world. So

### Varaha, Hiranyaksha, Brahma
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2nz6Wqgi8ac?t=999
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "When a Devata or a devotee has got a problem naturally they turn towards the Supreme Lord Krishna for solving their problem."
**Summary:** The speaker reiterates the specific form and purpose of the Varaha incarnation, highlighting the killing of Hiranyaksha and then transitions to a description from the third canto of Srimad Bhagavatam about how the earth fell into the water and Brahma's contemplation.

That Varaha incarnation not simply appeared but actually lifted the earth with a suitable snout to carry the planet earth. He appeared. So this is the description of Lord Varaha lifting the earth in two different verses. Now it is also described here when the Lord appeared to pick up the earth the demon of the name Hiranyakya tried to create a disturbance in the methodical functions of the Lord and therefore he was killed by being placed by being pierced I am sorry he was killed by being pierced by the tusk of Lord Varaha. Now in the Bhagavatam third canto there is another description of the appearance of Varaha. Here in the third canto it is mentioned in the 14th chapter it is described that the Lord appeared as Varaha for a specific purpose to lift the earth. Now how did the earth fall down into the water? So it is said here one two different reasons are given in this narration. The the creator Brahma he thought while I have been engaged in the process of creation the earth has been inundated by deluge. Inundated means flooded. By what? By what? A flood. There was a flood and has gone down into the depths of the ocean. It has gone down to the depths of the ocean. What can we do who are engaged in this matter of creation? When a Devata or a devotee has got a problem naturally they turn towards the Supreme Lord Krishna for solving their problem. So similarly Brahma is praying to Krishna. So he is telling it is best to let the Almighty Lord direct us. Krishna is seated in our heart. He gives us knowledge. He gives remembrance. He gives us forgetfulness. So when he directs a devotee it need not be that he has to only personally do that. No. He can do it personally because he is seated in everyone's heart as Paramatma. But he also empowers somebody else to do this work. So in accordance with the Lord's empowerment certain devotees certain great saintly persons they perform extraordinary activities. So when Brahma was thinking like this suddenly a small form of bores came out of his nostril. The measurement of this creature which came out of the nostril of Brahma was as small as the upper portion of a thumb. Upper portion of a thumb. Very very small.

### Brahma, Varaha, Vedas
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2nz6Wqgi8ac?t=1239
**Verse References:** —

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers

**Summary:** The speaker continues the story of Varaha's appearance from Brahma's nostril, describing its rapid growth and the bewilderment of Brahma and other sages, who eventually recognize the boar as Vishnu and greet him with Vedic chants.

While Brahma was observing him the bore became situated in the sky. Bore became situated in the sky. And the bore was appearing as a as gigantic as a great elephant. It is small when it was born. But it became big like a huge elephant. Now struck with wonder at observing the wonderful bore like form in the sky Brahma along with other Brahmanas like Marichi as well as the Kumaras and Manu began to argue began to argue in various ways. He argued that who is this who has come in the pretence of a bore. So Brahma says is this some extraordinary entity come in the pretence of a bore? It is very wonderful that he has come from God and as a result of this the the empowered person can do something which may appear to be like an extraordinary thing. But actually even an empowered person cannot do anything extraordinary like God that is not possible for us. So God only has to do it. So first of all this bore was seen to be no bigger than the tip of the thumb and within a moment he was as large as a stone. My mind is perturbed. Is he the supreme personality of God and Vishnu? See Brahma is not like the bore. Therefore immediately Brahma was not able to recognize but he came to know that this could be Vishnu. So while Brahma was deliberating with his sons the supreme Lord who appeared as the bore in the form of a mountain he roared very loudly like a great mountain. Formerly there used to be mountains which used to fly and which used to roar. Now we don't have experience but this was an extraordinary bore who is very powerful in the world. The inhabitants of Brahma loka or the next three lokas Janaloka Tapoloka they all became alerted and it was very very loud sound. So that sound which was the all auspicious sound of the All Merciful Lord they chanted auspicious chants from the three Vedas.

### Varaha, Hiranyaksha, Yajnas
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2nz6Wqgi8ac?t=1666
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "All Yajnas are meant for satisfying the Supreme Lord and he is the master of all Yajnas, all sacrifices. He is a recipient of all the offerings in the sacrifice."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes the story of Lord Varaha, describing his descent into the Garbhodaka ocean, his fight with and killing of Hiranyaksha, and the subsequent placement of the Earth in its orbit, emphasizing the Lord's role as the enjoyer of all sacrifices (Yajnas).

Protect the devotees who are always harassed by demons so the lord went diving into the giant mountain sorry into the water of the garba ocean and the ocean water became as if divided into two high waves and the ocean was praying to the lord oh lord of all sacrifices please do not cut me into two kindly protect me so the ocean was very afraid of the gigantic boar going diving inside the garba ocean so the lord as the varaha form have penetrated the water with his hooves which are like sharp arrows and found the limits of the ocean although it was unlimited this ocean is garbotion is very very very very huge but still the lord went down and found the bottom and he saw the earth resting place of all living entities lying as it was in the beginning of creation and he personally lifted it very easily he did that by placing the earth on his tusks and got it out of water now when he was coming out of the water because this hiranyaksha had hidden the earth in the bottom of the universe hiranyaksha tried to stop the lord then there was a fight between the supreme lord in his boar incarnation and hiranyaksha so the lord killed hiranyaksha by piercing that demon's body with his tusk and in this way hiranyaksha was killed and the earth was placed in its proper orbit so then the sages with great respect they offered wonderful prayers recognizing the supreme lord to be transcendental and also the enjoyer of all sacrifices, Yajneesha, this is very important. All Yajnas are meant for satisfying the Supreme Lord and he is the master of all Yajnas, all sacrifices. He is a recipient of all the offerings in the sacrifice. And by doing such sacrifices, anyone who performs sacrifice is benefited personally by the Supreme Lord.
