### Brahma, Vishnu, authority
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/6az3lopn0ho?t=278
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "You have to accept the authority of Vishnu, who is replying to Brahma."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the context of the verse, where Brahma, after performing tapasya, sees Vishnu and asks questions, emphasizing the authority of Vishnu's answers as the source of Brahma's birth and enlightenment.

One of the ways is to go by the verse itself. You see the context of this verse. Brahma has been created. Brahma is born. He doesn't know the source of his birth. And he gets an instruction that he has to perform tapasya. He hears the two letters ta and pa. And his intelligence, he utilizes and understands that he has to perform tapasya. He performs tapasya. And as a result of his performing tapasya, he has darshan of, he gets to see Vishnu. And after seeing Vishnu, he understands or is able to directly see, Oh, Vishnu is the source of my birth. And then he asks certain questions to Vishnu. And Vishnu is giving the reply. Now when Vishnu is giving the reply to Brahma, obviously, Vishnu being the source of Brahma, his answers to Brahma are authoritative. You have to accept the authority of Vishnu, who is replying to Brahma. After Brahma has tried to find the source of his birth, being unable to find, he performs tapasya. And as a result of tapasya, he has become enlightened. And he is now able to see the source of his birth. And then hear from Vishnu, the source about who is Vishnu. Therefore, Prabhupada says in the purport here, in another feature of this verse, no one can deny the personalities of both the Lord and Brahma. That Brahma as a person is standing in front of Vishnu. And Vishnu is present in front of Brahma as a person. That is confirmed in this verse itself. That the personality of Vishnu and Brahma cannot be denied.

---
### spiritual form, eternal beings, predominator and predominated
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/6az3lopn0ho?t=435
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So that singular is the Supreme Lord, and the maintained are the living entities."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the spiritual nature of Vishnu and Brahma's personalities, referencing the Kathopanishad to explain the difference between the singular Supreme Lord and the plural living entities, emphasizing the Lord as the maintainer and the living entities as the maintained.

Now, their personality is spiritual, or at least the God's personality is spiritual. He is clearly explained in the Kathopanishad verse, which is quoted by Prabhupada, whether we have a material form or not. In any case, according to the Kathopanishad verse, there are eternal conscious beings. And there are two categories, singular and plural. So in the previous paragraph, Prabhupada had explained that there is a difference between these two categories, because even though the similarity is nitya and cetana, the difference is, the singular is actually maintaining the plural. So that singular is the Supreme Lord. And the maintained are the living entities. It is very clear from the verse of the Kathopanishad. And that is corroborated here, that in the ultimate issue, both the predominator and predominated are persons. Predominator is the Lord, who is the maintainer. And the maintained are the predominated, as is evident from Rama, Rama's own position.

### impersonalism refuted, eternal form, matter vs. spirit
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/6az3lopn0ho?t=558
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "But we cannot accept Vishnu's form as temporary, for the simple reason that form existed before the creation, remains during the creation, and continues to exist even after everything is destroyed."
**Summary:** The speaker refutes the impersonalists' view of temporary forms, asserting that Vishnu's form is eternal, existing before, during, and after creation, and therefore spiritual, contrasting it with the temporary nature of matter.

Now the impersonalists, they say, whatever personality is being referred in this verse, or whatever we experience in this world, all these personalities are temporary manifestations, temporary forms. But we cannot accept Vishnu's form as temporary, for the simple reason that form existed before the creation, remains during the creation, and continues to exist even after everything is destroyed. If Vishnu's form existed before the creation, remains during the duration of the whole creation. And as it is, it is there in that directly described. That form is not a material form. That is an eternal form. And we know, anything which is eternal cannot be material. From the Bhagavad Gita, from all the Upanishads, from the Vedas, we understand, matter does not endure, and spirit never ceases to exist. Na sato vidyate bhavo, na bhavo vidyate sata. Very, very clear. Since Vishnu's form is eternally existing, Vishnu's form is necessarily a spiritual form. Vishnu formed spiritually.

---
### nitya and chetana, maintainer, misunderstanding
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/6az3lopn0ho?t=664
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "What is the greatest misunderstanding that exists in this world? Everything sees the body."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that if Vishnu's form is spiritual and we are qualitatively the same as Vishnu (nitya and chetana), then Vishnu is the single maintainer and we are the maintained, refuting impersonalism and highlighting the common misunderstanding of identifying with the material body.

If Vishnu's form is a spiritual form, and we are also in quality same as Vishnu, we are also nitya and chetana. But our relationship for our position means we are maintained by the singular nitya and chetana. Then it becomes very clear that Vishnu is the single maintainer, eternal, conscious person. And we are the maintained, eternal, conscious persons. We are many. Because there is only one Supreme God and there are many living entities. So therefore, the impersonal stress, impersonal features stressed by the less intelligent impersonal list, is refuted by pointing out that Vishnu, who is the creator of Brahma, who existed before the creation, who remains after the destruction of the entire universe, is a person. If that entity who remains before creation or after destruction is a person, then ultimately the absolute within person is refuted. Now for realization of one's own self, if one thinks I am equal to God in terms of quality, then that is helpful, it is conducive to separate ourselves from the ahamkara that I am matter or I am this material body, which is very, very strong, strong feeling or big misunderstanding that is there in every person's consciousness. What is the greatest misunderstanding that exists in this world?

---
### self-realization, spiritual psychology, predominator
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/6az3lopn0ho?t=782
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "But there is always the difference of the predominated and the predominator."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that for self-realization, it's convenient to assume oneness with the Absolute Truth to detach from the material body, but emphasizes the eternal difference between the predominated and the predominator, with God always being the controller and living beings always subordinate.

Everything sees the body. So, to convince anybody interested in self-realization, to convince that person that you are not the body, you are not matter, it is stressed in the Upanishads that you are one in quality with the Lord or the Absolute Truth, who is completely spiritual. So Prabhupada says, for the realization of one's self in spiritual psychology, it may be convenient to assume one's self with the same principle as the Absolute Truth. It is just a matter of convenience. But I am same as the body. I am equal to the Lord. It is a matter of convenience. But there is always the difference of the predominated and the predominator. Vishnu or God, the Absolute Truth, is always the predominator, always the dominator, always the maintainer, always the Lord, always the controller, always the master. And I, or any living being for that matter, or even Brahma, is always about that, always controlled, always maintained, always subordinate. It is very, very clear.

That we know from the scriptures. So if nothing material remains after everything is destroyed, and if Vishnu remains, and he has got a form, then what is the conclusion? Vishnu's form is spiritual. Vishnu's form is eternal. Vishnu's form is non-material. So therefore, the form seen by Brahma is a transcendental form, spiritual form. And, this entire material world which we see is a manifestation of the energy of that Supreme Person. Because He has multifarious energies as explained in the Upanishads and all the other scriptures. And His energies are manifesting different varieties, especially in this material world. So those varieties are made of the energy. ### Vedas, Vasudeva, Narayana, transcendental
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/6az3lopn0ho?t=990
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Nārāyaṇa is transcendental to this material creation."
**Summary:** The speaker quotes Vedic statements and Cangaracharya's commentary on the Bhagavad Gita to emphasize that Vasudeva and Narayana existed before creation, and that Narayana is transcendental to the material creation, which originates from avyattāt (unmanifest material energy).

vāsute govā idaṁ agra āsīt This is the very statement. Now the same thing Krishna has done in ahaṁ evāsaṁ evāgre I existed before the creation. And same thing is told in the Vedas, vāsute govā idaṁ agra āsīt Vāsudeva existed before the creation. na brahma na ca śāntara Brahmā and Śiva did not exist. Another statement, nārāyaṇa āsī na brahmādeśāna Nārāyaṇa existed before the creation, but Brahmā and Śiva did not exist. So, before the creation there was none except Vāsudeva and Nārāyaṇa. Only Nārāyaṇa was there and no one else. And Cangaracharya in his commentary on the Bhagavad Gita, what does he say? He says that nārāyaṇa paro avyattāt It's a bit of Cangaracharya in the Bhagavad Gita. Cangaracharya is an impersonalist. But he says in the Bhagavad Gita, in the beginning, in the commentary in the Bhagavad Gita, nārāyaṇa is transcendental, to this material creation. Because the material creation is coming from avyattāt.

### material nature, gunas, Krishna as the cause
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/6az3lopn0ho?t=1154
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So ultimately, Krishna is the cause of all causes."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that material nature works under Krishna's direction through the guna avataras (Brahma, Vishnu, and Shiva), ultimately establishing Krishna as the cause of all causes and the controller of all controllers.

He is transcendental both to cause and effect of this material. So elsewhere it is explained that material nature is working to produce a transformation or to combine the different elements or to create or maintain or destroy according to the direction of Krishna. So if Krishna is directing, how is He directing? He is directing through the directors of the gunas, called the guna avatāras. Through Brahma, He is directing rajo guna. Through Viṣṇu, He is directing the sattva guna. And through Śrīma, He is directing the buddha. But it is He only who is directing because He is sitting on the heart of Brahma, the heart of Brahma as the super-soul. He himself expanded as Viṣṇu. And Śiva also is receiving direction from Viṣṇu or nārāyaṇa. So therefore, ultimately, even though material nature is immediate cause of this creation and different changes in material nature, even though above the gunas, material nature as gunas is acting, above the gunas are the directors, even beyond the directors is Krishna. Beyond the directors is Krishna. So ultimately, Krishna is the cause of all causes.

---
### Brahma Samhita, transcendental, creation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/6az3lopn0ho?t=1274
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Even though the living entities are of the same quality as God, the living entities are not equal to God in all respects because the living entities are created by God in terms of the living entities getting their material body."
**Summary:** The speaker references the Brahma Samhita to affirm Krishna as the ultimate cause and controller, explaining that while living entities share the same quality as God, they are not equal because they are created in terms of their material bodies.

He is the ultimate controller of all controllers. Therefore, in the Brahma Samhita, He said, Īśvaraḥ paramaṁ viṣṇahāṁ sarva-kāraṇatāṁ ultimate cause of all causes. He is the controller of all controllers. And that same thing is explained here that nārāyaṇa is transcendent to all creation. And the creation is the product of the abhyakta. Abhyakta is a material energy in a manifest state. It becomes manifested as a soul material. So this is explained in Śaṅkara ārācārya. And therefore, the difference between the creator and the created always exists. Both are part in all aspects. Even though the living entities are of the same quality as God, the living entities are not equal to God in all respects because the living entities are created by God in terms of the living entities getting their material body. As spirits only, they are not created. But as living entities in this material world who are getting different types of bodies, they are all created. As living entities who are taking birth in the material world, they are all created.

---
### darshan, incarnation, yoga maya
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/6az3lopn0ho?t=1365
**Verse References:** BG 4.6
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Even when he apparently takes birth, as Kṛṣṇa or Rāma or Vāvana, you cannot say he is born."
**Summary:** The speaker differentiates between the creation of living entities and the Lord giving darshan in his transcendental forms, explaining that even when the Lord appears to take birth as Krishna, Rama, or Vamana, he is not born in the material sense, but employs his internal potency (yoga maya) for his incarnations.

So that creation is different from the Lord giving darshan to Brahma in his form. Vishnu form, nārāyaṇa form, Kṛṣṇa form, any transcendental forms. So when the Lord gives darshan, you cannot say, Oh, he has taken birth now. Even when he apparently takes birth, as Kṛṣṇa or Rāma or Vāvana, you cannot say he is born. Because Bhagavad Gita what does it say? ajopīsaṁ avyayātmā It says when the transcendental body never deteriorates, avyayātmā Avyayātmā means that it does not deteriorate. And ajara, he is unborn. Even though he is unborn and his transcendental body never deteriorates, but still he appears. Sambhavāmi ātmam āhyamā What does that verse say? ajopīsaṁ avyayātmā bhūtānāṁ īśvaropīsaṁ He is the Lord of all created beings. Bhūtā means that which is created. We are all created beings. Brahma is a created being. All of us are created beings. All demigods are created beings. Everyone is created in this world. So bhūtānāṁ īśvaropīsaṁ Bhūtānāṁ īśvaropīsaṁ He is the Lord. But still Prakṛtim Svām He has got a particular nature. Prakṛtim Svām adiṣṭhāya sambhavāmi ātmam āhyamā He has got a specific type of energy which is called yoga māyā or ātma māyā.

Any questions? Do you have any questions? Do you have any thoughts on nature like the nature of Shiva? The Mahārāja and Shiva. But there are some other questions. No. They don't come under the modes. By the grace of the Supreme Lord, Brahmā, Vishnu and Shiva don't come under the influence of the modes. Though Brahmā and Shiva are subject to the influence. They are subject to the influence because their position is such. But by the Lord's mercy, they don't come under the influence. Only then they can do the work of directing. If they themselves come under the modes, then how can they direct? They will not be able to direct. Therefore, by the grace of the Supreme Lord, for the sake of direction, they don't come under the modes. But their position is not that like Vishnu is above the modes, constitutionally, Brahmā and Shiva are not above the modes, constitutionally.

### protection, surrender, illusory energy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/6az3lopn0ho?t=1603

**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker provides examples of Brahma and Shiva momentarily being influenced by the modes but being corrected by Krishna, emphasizing that they are protected by Vishnu's grace due to their surrender to Krishna, allowing them to direct the gunas effectively.

They may come under the modes. It happens sometimes. But they have protection of Vishnu. If Brahmā thought, Krishna is an ordinary one. And what did he do? He stole away Krishna's thumb and covered it up. So immediately Krishna corrected him. And then Brahmā came and said, Oh, it's too cute to do all that. It's all right. It's all right now. Shiva thought, Mohini Murthy is a beautiful, enjoyable woman. Let me try to enjoy her. And then he realized after he tried, Oh, this is my girl. It's not enjoyable for me. Sometimes, Raman and Shiva, even though they may come under the influence of the gunas, still, for the sake of directing the gunas, as directors of the modes, they are protected against fallout or influence by the grace of the Supreme Lord. And that same thing applies for all of us. If we have the mercy of the Supreme Lord, we can expect that we will be protected by the Lord, and we will not. But we will not become entangled in mind with illusory energy. Krishna says that in Bhagavad Gita, māne vaye prapadyante māyā-meta karaniteta Anybody who surrenders to Krishna can cross over the influence of this illusory energy. So Brahmā and Shiva are completely surrendered to Krishna. So they are always protected. They are always protected.

### Shiva, Vishnu, material energy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/6az3lopn0ho?t=1790
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So when there is a need for Vishnu to come in contact with the material energy, for the sake of certain purposes in the material world, then it is Shiva, who is another form of Vishnu, who actually contacts the material energy and makes the creation possible."
**Summary:** The speaker explains Shiva's dual function as both an incarnation of Vishnu and a living being controlled by Vishnu, highlighting Shiva's role in contacting material energy for creation, a role Vishnu never takes on directly.

This is Veda. When Shiva was in the house of Narsimha, what was his position? He was in the house of Narsimha. Am I right? functions which he performs as an incarnation of Vishnu. And certain functions he does as a living being completely controlled and directed by Vishnu. So he's got this dual function because one particular function of Shiva requires that he come in contact with the material energy. Vishnu never comes in contact with material energy. Vishnu always is the controller, director of material energy, but he never comes in contact. So when there is a need for Vishnu to come in contact with the material energy, for the sake of certain purposes in the material world, then it is Shiva, who is another form of Vishnu, who actually contacts the material energy and makes the creation possible. So that form of the Lord, who is an expansion of Vishnu, for the sake of his material creation, who comes in contact with the material energy is called Shiva.

### Shambhu principle, simultaneous, milk and yogurt
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/6az3lopn0ho?t=1904
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Shambhu principle is that he is simultaneously Vishnu and not Vishnu."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the Shambhu principle, explaining that Shiva is simultaneously Vishnu and not Vishnu, using the analogy of milk and yogurt to illustrate how Shiva is both the same as and different from Vishnu.

Prabhupada says Shambhu principle. Shambhu principle is that he is simultaneously Vishnu and not Vishnu. As the expansion of Vishnu, he is of the quality of Vishnu, of the stature of Vishnu. For the sake of the creation, when he comes in contact with the material energy, he is like a Jeeva. So he is both. Just like the example was given in the Brahma Samhita. Yogurt, dahi, is same as milk and different from milk. Because dahi, same as milk, is a product of milk only. But still, dahi has got its own different quality than milk. When it comes to the action of milk versus curds or yogurt, you see, they have got different effect. So that is because milk, when it comes in contact with some acid, it turns into yogurt. Milk transformed is yogurt. So when milk is transformed into yogurt, you cannot say that yogurt is same as milk in all respects. But then, what is the transformation that has happened? It is milk only, in another form. Milk only. So, simultaneously, same as and different from.

## The Different Stages of Sannyasa | HH Stoka Krishna Swami
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### sannyasa, stages of life, renunciation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/MGxEICE74F4?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "According to the strict rules of sannyas ashram, one should not stay in a place more than three days."
**Summary:** This segment describes the four stages of advancement in sannyasa: Kutichaka, Bahudaka, Parivrajaka, and Paramahamsa, detailing the practices and characteristics of each stage.

There are four stages of advancement in sannyas: Kutichaka, Bahudaka, Parivrajaka, Paramahamsa. Kutichaka is the beginning stage of sannyas. Prabhupada says generally they construct a cottage outside the village or outside the usual place of residence. And they live there and from home they receive some simple food so that they are not bothered about preparing food, gathering the ingredients, etc. They can fully devote themselves for practicing tapasya, for studying the scriptures, for practicing austerity, etc. Bahudaka means he will collect a little, little, little from many homes by going and begging, bhiksha. And this is also called Madhukari. Madhukari means the business of a bumble bee. A bee collects the nectar from flowers, a very little from each flower, but it gathers so much that it is able to make a whole honeycomb. When one becomes even further advanced, then one starts travelling. Then one starts learning and preaching. Parivrajaka does not stay in one place. According to the rules of sannyas ashram, strict rules, one should not stay in a place more than three days.

---
### sannyasa, Kali Yuga, preaching
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/MGxEICE74F4?t=99
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "In Kali Yuga it is said sannyas is forbidden, but even the followers of Chaitanya they accept the essence of this principle of sannyas which is traveling and preaching."
**Summary:** This segment explains that while strict sannyasa is difficult in Kali Yuga, the essence of traveling and preaching is still embraced, with some minimum rules accepted to facilitate preaching.

As the Paramahamsa is about all varnas and all ashramas, he's a pure devotee. Devotees who are fully devoted to serving Krishna without caring for any personal comforts, they are actually paramahamsas, so these strict rules of sannyas are not possible to be followed in Kali Yuga. Therefore, in Kali Yuga it is said sannyas is forbidden, but even the followers of Chaitanya they accept the essence of this principle of sannyas which is traveling and preaching and some minimum rules of sannyas are accepted but not all the rules so that the preaching can be done without much difficulty and that's our main business.

### devotion, humility, scholarship
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/t0n-zxIsnfQ?t=86
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "If by scholarship one does not gain such devotion, then how will that learning help him?"
**Summary:** This segment describes Srivas Thakur's role in teaching that Lord Chaitanya is conquered by the love of his devotees, his birth and family, and an interaction with a young Nimai Pandit where he emphasizes the importance of devotion over mere scholarship.

Through Srivas Thakur, Lord Chaitanya taught that he is conquered by the love of his devotees and how anyone who is dear to his devotee also conquers his heart. Srivas Pandit appeared at Biyani Bazar in Shilha district as the second son of Sri Jaladhar Pandit. It is mentioned in Chaitanya Charita Amrita that Srivas had three brothers: Sri Rama, Sri Pati, and Sri Nidhi. But Jaladhar Pandit had an older son named Nalina Pandita who disappeared when the family lived in Biyani Bazar. When Srivas was 12 years old, Jaladhar took his family to Navadvipa and remained there. Because young Nimai Pandit seemed to have grown arrogant by his scholarship, one day Srivas told him, "Why do you people undertake study so that they might understand what is devotion to Sri Krishna? If by scholarship one does not gain such devotion, then how will that learning help him? It simply becomes a tedious endeavor which in the end is nothing but a waste of time. If you have actually learned something, then begin your worship of Sri Krishna now." Nimai laughed and replied, "By your mercy certainly that will come to be. If you are all kind enough to me, then definitely I shall attain devotion to Sri Krishna's lotus feet."

---
### initiation, mercy, detachment, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/t0n-zxIsnfQ?t=188
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "He loves me so much that he does not even express his gratitude. He does not even express his grief over the death of his own son."
**Summary:** This segment narrates Mahaprabhu's journey to Gaya for initiation, emphasizing the need for blessings and guidance from great souls. It then recounts the story of Srivas Thakur's son's death during kirtan and his extraordinary detachment and devotion, leading to Mahaprabhu's benediction of eternal prosperity for his household.

Shortly thereafter, Mahaprabhu journeyed to Gaya where he accepted initiation from Sri Eswara Puri. The Lord did so to teach that, without the blessings and guidance of great souls and without the prayers of great Vaishnavas, beseeching Krishna to extend his mercy to us, there is no hope of our really receiving the mercy of Krishna. Lord Krishna was born in Mathura but performed his most intimate pastimes in Vrindavan. Similarly, Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu was born in the house of Jagannath Mishra, yet he performed his most intimate pastimes in the house of Srivas Thakur. One night while Lord Gauranga was engaged in Kirtan, the son of Srivas Thakur who was suffering from fever left his body. The lady started weeping very loudly. Srivas Thakur on hearing the news, quickly ran into the room and found his son's dead body. He told the ladies to give up this crime, that there should be no grief, that even a most sinful person who dies while hearing the holy name and glories of Krishna will be liberated. He told them, "My son is so fortunate to have left his body hearing the harinam and kirtan of Lord Chaitanya and his devotees. Now if you wish to cry, do it silently in your own heart. But do not tell anyone what has happened because if you disturb the ecstatic dancing of my Lord Gauranga, then I will tie a pot around my neck and drown myself in the Ganges today." After speaking all these words, Srivas Thakur went back into the kirtan and with a big smile on his face began dancing with Lord Chaitanya and Vaishnavas. The kirtan went on throughout the entire night. When Mahaprabhu came to know of the passing of Srivas' son, he cried tears of love and said, "How could I ever leave the association of such a pure devotee as Srivas Thakur? He loves me so much that he does not even express his gratitude. He does not even express his grief over the death of his own son." Mahaprabhu gave this benediction to Srivas: "There will never be poverty in your house. If you simply remain indoors, whatever you require for your worship will come to your door."

---

It is still being worshipped. Okay, another prana mantra. It is there with the translation. So let us repeat this: Srivas Pandita, Dhimanyaha, Pura, Narado Munihi, Parvatakhyo, Munivaro, Yaha, Asi, Narada Priyaha, Sri Rama Panditaha, Sriman, Tatkanishtha, Sahodaraha, Namna, Ambika, Vraja, Vraja, Dhatri, Dhatri, Sthanyadhatri, Sthanyadhatri, Sthitapura, Sthitapura, Sthitapura, Saiveyam, Saiveyam, Saiveyam, Malini, Malini, Namni, Namni, Srivasagrihini, Srivasagrihini, Mata, Mata, Mata or Mata? Śrīvās is Nārada who plays such an important part in Kṛṣṇa's līlā. Nārada Muni's friend Parvata Muni appeared as Śrīvās's younger brother, Ramāya. Śrīvās's wife Mālini Devi was Kṛṣṇa's vet nurse in Vraja, Ambika.

## The Illusion of Safety in the Material World | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | ISKCON Bangalore
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### illusion, danger, spiritual world
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Bn6BfCkgD6U?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Unless we come out of illusion, we are unable to realize that we should not stay in this material world even one moment more."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that the material world is inherently unsafe and that our perception of safety is an illusion, urging listeners to strive for the spiritual world.

If we carefully study the scriptures, we understand there is no safe place which is free from all dangers in this material existence. Material existence means anytime there can be death. So, therefore, it is only because of the influence of the illusory energy that we think that sometimes I am safe, sometimes there is danger for my life. Actually, dangers are always existing. But where is absolute safety in this material world? There is no place which is absolutely safe. So, unless we come out of illusion, we are unable to realize that we should not stay in this material world even one moment more. We should immediately get out of material existence and go to our original position, the spiritual world, Krishnaloka.

---

## The Pastimes of Durga Devi | His Holiness Sri Stoka Krishna Swami | Gaura Mandala Parikrama 2024
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### Durga Devi, Rukmini, Krishna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/pVIYZU85DOU?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Ordinary people pray to Durga for material wealth, fame, profit, strength and so on. Rukmini, however, desired to have Krishna for her husband."
**Summary:** The speaker describes the deity of Dhakeshwari as Durga Devi and explains her role in the pastime of Rukmini's marriage to Krishna, highlighting Rukmini's special prayers for Krishna as her husband.

So we are in this most holy place where there is this deity of goddess Dhakeshwari. She is Durga Devi and who is Durga Devi is very nicely explained by Prabhupad in the Krishnabook. This is the instance where Rukmini, who was kidnapped by Krishna on the request of Rukmini. So she was arranged to be married to Shishupala, but she wrote to Krishna that I don't want to marry him, I want to marry you. Of course, Rukmini is eternally the consort of Krishna. She is Akshara Mahalakshmi. She is a goddess. But this is the pastime of Krishna. So she is participating in the pastime and her marriage was anyway arranged and Krishna had to kidnap her, take her away by force before she was actually given away in charity to Shishupala. So the description is here. Then just before the marriage ceremony, it was a tradition that the bride Rukmini, she will go and worship Durga, goddess Durga. So it is described here. Rukmini offered her prayers to the deity of Durga Devi. My dear goddess Durga, I offer my respectful obeisances unto you as well as to your children. Ordinary people pray to Durga for material wealth, fame, profit, strength and so on. Rukmini, however, desired to have Krishna for her husband. And therefore, she prayed that the deity be pleased with her and bless her with that benediction. Since she desired only Krishna, her worship of the demigods is not condemned.

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Praying to the demigods to achieve the favour of Krishna is not irregular."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that the Gopis worshipped goddess Katyayani to get Krishna as their husband, and Rukmini thought of demigods in respect to Krishna, emphasizing that praying to demigods to achieve Krishna's favor is not irregular, referencing the Brahma Samhita and Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu's recommendations.

Then, Srila Prabhupada also says in the same chapter, the Gopis worshipped goddess Katyaini to get Krishna as their husband. Similarly, Rukmini was thinking of the various types of demigods, not for material benefit, but in respect to Krishna. Praying to the demigods to achieve the favour of Krishna is not irregular. And Rukmini was fully absorbed in thoughts of Krishna. In the Chaitanya Charita Amrita, Srila Prabhupada says about this mention of the demigods in the Brahma Samhita. Srila Prabhupada says, In the Brahma Samhita, there are mantras offering obeisances to Lord Shiva, Lord Brahma, the Sun God, Lord Ganesha, as well as Lord Vishnu, all of whom are worshipped by the impersonalists as Panchopasana. Vaishnavas can also worship all these demigods, but only on the principles of the Brahma Samhita, which is recommended by Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu. We may note in this connection, the mantras for worshipping Lord Shiva, Lord Brahma, Lord Durga, Sun God, and Ganesha, as described in the Brahma Samhita. So what are these mantras in the Brahma Samhita? All of you repeat after me, since we are in the temple of Durga Devi, so we will offer this prayer to Gurga Devi as given in the Brahma Samhita. All of you are familiar with this particular prayer in the Brahma Samhita. Please repeat after me, Srishti Sthiti Pralaya Sadhana Shaktireka Chaayeva Yasya Bhuvanani Vibharti Durga Ichchana Rupam Api Yasya Cha Cheshta Tesa Govindam Adipurusham Tamaham Bhajami. The meaning of this is, the external potency Maya, who is of the nature of the shadow of the chit potency, is worshipped by all people as Durga, the creating, preserving and destroying agency of this mundane world. I adore the primeval Lord Govinda in accordance with whose will Durga conducts herself. And in the purport to this verse, in the Brahma Samhita, translated and purports given by his divine grace, Srila Bhaktisiddhanta Saraswati Thakur, he very nicely explains.

---
### Durga Devi, Material Desires, Devi Dhama
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/pVIYZU85DOU?t=470
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Generally people worship Durga Devi with some material desires."
**Summary:** The speaker explains Durga Devi's role as the creator of Devi Dhama, consisting of 14 planetary systems, and her ten arms representing ten-fold fruitive activities, noting that people generally worship her for material desires and that she rides a lion representing her heroic prowess and tramples Mahishasura, representing the subduer of all vices.

Very concisely he explains, the world in which Brahma takes his stand and hymns the Lord of Goloka. Brahma is worshipping Krishna by chanting these prayers. But here, Srila Bhaktisiddhanta Saraswati Thakur explains, this world is called Devi Dhama, consisting of 14 worlds, 14 planetary systems. And Durga is its creator. She is the siding deity of the entire material universe. She is ten-armed. If you closely look here, there also you can see. She is having ten hands, representing the ten-fold fructive activities. Generally people worship Durga Devi with some material desires. And those desires that she can fulfil for this fructive people are ten kinds of different divisions of these benefits she can bestow. She rides on the lion, representing her heroic prowess. She is always riding a lion, that you can clearly see also. She tramples down Mahishasura, representing the subduer of all vices. Even though Durga is worshipped by so many asuras and so many sinful people. But she never is kind to them. She always deals with them very very sternly. So she subdues all vices by her power. She is the mother of two sons, Kartikeya and Ganesha, representing beauty and success. So people always worship materialistic people, Ganesha. For what? For success. So Ganesha represents success in this material world. And Kartikeya, you clearly see there, he is dressed like a very handsome prince. Correct? So he represents mundane beauty. So materialistic persons worship Kartikeya for bodily beauty. She is placed between Lakshmi and Saraswati, representing mundane opulence and mundane knowledge.

---
### Material vs. Spiritual, Lakshmi and Saraswati, Destructive Time
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/pVIYZU85DOU?t=661
**Verse References:** BG 11
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Lakshmi as the daughter of Durga Devi, she represents material opulence. Whereas Lakshmi in the spiritual world is actually the personification of spiritual opulence."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts material and spiritual opulence represented by Lakshmi and Saraswati, explaining that Durga Devi is armed with twenty weapons representing pious activities enjoined by the Vedas and holds a snake representing the beauty of destructive time.

In the material world, Lakshmi as the daughter of Durga Devi, she represents material opulence. Whereas Lakshmi in the spiritual world is actually the personification of spiritual opulence. Spiritual wealth. And similarly, Saraswati in the material world represents material knowledge. That's why in school, when we were studying at least, they used to tell us, worship Saraswati, pray to Saraswati, then you can do well in your exams of this mundane education system. So that Saraswati, whom they were referring to, ordinary people refer to, is the goddess of material knowledge. But Saraswati in the spiritual world is actually representing transcendental knowledge, not material knowledge. She is armed with twenty weapons. Of course, in this form of Durga, ten hands, she is having ten weapons. But, she is actually armed with twenty weapons, representing the various pious activities enjoined by the Vedas for suppression of the vices, suppression of all sins. Twenty different pious activities are given in the Vedas and her twenty weapons represent those twenty pious activities. She holds the snake, representing the beauty of destructive time. Snake is also called Kala Sattva. Means, Kala is always destroying. Krishna says this in the Bhagavad Gita, 11th chapter. Kalosmi Lokakshayakrut. Shaya means destroy, destruction. So, Kala simply destroys. So, that is the symbol which she is holding. Symbolically, she is holding the snake, representing destructive time.

---
### Durga as Prison House, Karma, Purification
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/pVIYZU85DOU?t=815
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "As soon as we forget Krishna, we are imprisoned in this material world."
**Summary:** The speaker describes Durga as a prison house for those who forget Krishna, explaining that the wheel of Karma is the instrument of punishment and that Durga's duty is to purify penalized Jeevas by giving them suitable punishment for their reformation, all in accordance with Govinda's will.

Such is the, such is Durga, possessing all these manifold forms. Durga is possessed of Durga. Durga is possessed of Durga, which means a prison house Durga means the prison house. When Jeevas, begotten of the marginal potency, Tatastha Shakti, forget the service of Krishna, they are confined in this mundane prison house, the citadel of Durga. As soon as we forget Krishna, we are imprisoned in this material world. The wheel of Karma is the incident of the material world. The wheel of Karma is the incident of the material world. The wheel of Karma is the instrument of punishment at this place. Kala Chakra is also called sometimes the wheel of Karma. The work of purifying these penalized Jeevas is the duty devolved upon Durga. Even though Durga appears to be very cruel, but actually she is serving Krishna in a very peculiar way. So, she is purifying the Jeevas by giving them suitable punishment for their reformation. She is incessantly engaged, incessantly means continuously engaged in discharging the same by the will of Govinda. Ichchanu roopam appears as the one who carries out all her activities exactly in accordance with the will of Govinda.

### Krishna's Potency, Yogamaya, Durga's Role
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/pVIYZU85DOU?t=934
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna has no two potencies called external potency and internal potency. Krishna has only one potency that is his internal potency."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that Krishna is the superintending Lord above material nature and that Durga's actions are in accordance with His will, further elaborating on the pastimes of Krishna and Yogamaya, and the roles of Yashoda and Kamsa in relation to devotees.

Krishna is the superintending Lord above material nature, above the planet. When luckily the forgetfulness of Govinda on the part of the imprisoned Jeevas is remarked by them by coming in contact with self-realized souls like Prabhupada and their natural aptitude for the loving service of Krishna is aroused, Durga herself then becomes the soul of Krishna. So in the past time of Lord Krishna appearing as the son of Vasudeva and Devaki Krishna orders Yogamaya his internal potency to appear as his sister in his past time as a devotee to Krishna. So Krishna ordered him to hide him in the house of Nandamara and exchange him for the daughter born of Yashoda. So in the hands of Yashoda Yashoda has the power to deliver the devotees as internal potency. That means she facilitates the devotional service of the devotees. She provides for all the requisite facilities for the devotees. So in the hands of Nandamara she has the power to bring the devotees to her home. So in the hands of Yashoda she has the power to bring the devotees to her home. She has the power to bring the devotees to her home. She has the power to bring the devotees to her home. So in the hands of Kamsa she has the power to bring the devotees to her home. So in the hands of Kamsa she has the power to bring the devotees to her home.

### Internal vs. External Potency, Illusion, Surrender
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/pVIYZU85DOU?t=1151
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Yogamaya reminds everybody about Krishna. Durga Devi actually puts everybody under illusion because of which they think they are the Lords."
**Summary:** The speaker clarifies that Krishna has only one potency, His internal potency, which acts as Durga Devi, the external potency, for conditioned souls, and that while Yogamaya reminds everyone about Krishna, Durga Devi puts everyone under illusion, but when devotees surrender to Durga Devi, she becomes the agency for delivering them.

Krishna has no two potencies called external potency and internal potency. Krishna has only one potency that is his internal potency. The internal potency in the spiritual world and for devotees forgetful souls, conditioned souls, rebellious souls, asuras, she acts as Durga Devi external potency. The characteristics of external potency is just the opposite of the internal potency. Yoga Maya reminds everybody about Krishna. Durga and Krishna Yogamaya facilitates devotional service. Durga Devi actually puts everybody under illusion because of which they think they are the Lords. Krishna is not the Lord. There is no separate Lord. Everyone thinks I am the Lord. Under the control of Durga Devi. But because of the illusory energy, the illusion that Durga Devi puts them under, they think that they are independent. They think they are the Lords or they think they can become the Lord in case somebody else is overpowering them. So, Durga Devi acts as the external energy, the deluding energy only in this material world and she is originally Yogamaya only. Therefore, for devotees in the material world, when we become devotees or when we surrender to Durga Devi, the same Durga Devi who is responsible for putting all the conditioned souls under the illusion, all the forgetful souls under the illusion, she becomes the agency for delivering the devotees.

---
### Obstacles in Devotion, Material Desires, Token Reaction
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/pVIYZU85DOU?t=1317
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna gives a very very small reaction, a token reaction as Prabhupada says. It is very much reduced. That little reaction is to remind us that we should give up all our material desires and simply act as a master and prepare ourselves to quickly get out of this place and go to the spiritual world."
**Summary:** The speaker addresses the perception that Durga Devi creates obstacles for devotees, explaining that these obstacles are actually token reactions from Krishna to remind devotees to give up material desires and focus on spiritual progress, referencing Srila Bhaktisiddhanta Saraswati Thakur's teachings on obtaining guileless grace through selfless service to Krishna.

Sometimes devotees think that Durga Devi is anyway meant for putting all the obstacles or she puts obstacles on the path of their progress when they want to lord over or they want to enjoy. She creates all kinds of burdens. So, devotees sometimes think that when I am trying to do devotional service, if there is any obstacle, they think the cause is Krishna who has taken charge of the devotees, who gives a token reaction just to remind you that these desires which you are seeking fulfillment of even after becoming devotees is actually an obstacle for your path of progress in your life. You have to give up these material desires. Krishna gives a very very small reaction, a token reaction as Prabhupada says. It is very much reduced. That little reaction is to remind us that we should give up all our material desires and simply act as a master and prepare ourselves to quickly get out of this place and go to the spiritual world. Krishna is above personal world. So here Srila Bhaktisiddhanta Saraswati Thakur says that everyone should obtain the guileless grace with the selfless service of Krishna.

---
### Demigod Worship, Krishna's Supremacy, Proper Respect
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/pVIYZU85DOU?t=1451
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Never consider demigods equal to or greater than Krishna but at the same time we should not fail to show proper respect to the demigods."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes by stating that devotees can expect the mercy of Durga by worshipping Krishna, emphasizing that demigods are servants of Krishna and should be respected as devotees of Krishna, but never considered equal to or greater than the Supreme Lord.

As devotees we can expect the mercy of Durga by worshipping Krishna. And Prabhupada says whenever devotees visit the temples of either Durga or Shiva they are servants of Krishna. They are not equal with Krishna. Therefore even if one goes to a temple of the demigods one should not accept the deities as they are accepted by the impersonalists. All of them are to be accepted as personal demigods but they all serve the order of the deities. So if one can offer respect even to an ant why not to the demigods? Prabhupada is telling you that if one can offer respect even to an immortal being then one must always know however that no demigod is equal to or above the Supreme Lord. Only Krishna is the Supreme Personality of Godhead and all others including the demigods such as Lord Shiva Lord Brahma Goddess Durga are surely eternal servants of the Lord. Never consider demigods equal to or greater than Krishna but at the same time we should not fail to show proper respect to the demigods and what is that showing proper respect? We bow down to them as personalities who are devotees of Krishna and demigods in this material world. For they are placed in that position only by Krishna control us subordinate control us to Vishnu.

## The Rarity Of Pure Devotional Service | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | ISKCON Bangalore
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### devotional service, rarity, detachment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Rwr89To9lH4?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "It is very, very, very, very rare to find among these Vishnu Bhaktas in the upper planetary systems, one pure devotee of the Supreme Lord Vishnu."
**Summary:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami describes how living entities wander the universe and how even those in the mode of goodness and detachment often lack pure devotional service, making it difficult for them to recognize a pure devotee like Narada Muni.

According to his wish and will, living entities are wandering all over the universe. This Brahmanda Brahmana wandering all over the universe is going on. But Narada Muni is not subject to Brahmanda Brahmana by force of the material nature. He travels, he goes from one place to another, simply because he is preaching pure devotional service. And Narada Muni doesn't find easily candidates who will readily accept his preaching. It's ironical that the residents of those upper planetary systems are situated in mode of goodness, predominantly. So they are situated in knowledge, they are situated in detachment. Still, because they are bereft of pure devotional service, they cannot recognize Narada Muni's actual situation. So they are attached to either the process of karma, jnana or tapasya. But it is very rare to find somebody who has accepted the path of pure devotional service. Even among them, those who accept the path of devotion, their devotion generally is mixed devotion. It is very, very, very, very rare to find among these Vishnu Bhaktas in the upper planetary systems, one pure devotee of the Supreme Lord Vishnu. One pure devotee of the Supreme Lord Vishnu. One pure devotee of the Supreme Lord Vishnu. One pure devotee of the Supreme Lord Vishnu. How rare?

---
### pure devotee, liberation, perfection
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Rwr89To9lH4?t=114
**Verse References:** SB 1.2.6
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "But even if you were to search among millions of Mukta purushas and siddha purushas, to find one Narayana parayana is very, very, very rare."
**Summary:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami quotes the Bhagavatam to emphasize the extreme rarity of finding a pure devotee (Narayana parayana) even among millions of liberated souls (Mukta purushas) and perfected beings (siddha purushas).

Bhagavatam it is declared: Muktanam api siddhanam mukta siddha purusha. Mukta means liberated souls. They are materially detached. They don't have any desire for material enjoyment in this world. Siddhanam, those who are perfected beings, they have completely controlled their senses and they engage their senses exclusively in the business of self-realization. Such siddha purushas, among them, Muktanam api siddhanam Narayana parayana sudurlabha. Among them, to find one Narayana parayana, one pure devotee, is sudurlabha. Durlabha means very difficult to find. Sudurlabha means most difficult to find, almost impossible. And among how many Mukta purushas and siddha purushas, is it difficult to find one Narayana parayana? Kotishvapi mahamune kotishu, crores, millions. But even if you were to search among millions of Mukta purushas and siddha purushas, to find one Narayana parayana is very, very, very rare.

---

## The Result of Chanting Hare Krishna | Soulful Japa
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### holy name, spiritual senses, manifestation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/HQrUoI2IA-o?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The holy name of Krishna, his body, his pastimes cannot be understood by the blunt material senses; they are manifested independently."
**Summary:** This segment explains that the holy name, Krishna's form, and pastimes are self-manifest and cannot be understood through material senses but through sincere service and faith.

There is another verse which says in Chaitanya Charitamrita, "Atayiva krishnera nama deha vilasa prakritendriya grahiyanayi hai svaprakasha." The holy name of Krishna, his body, his pastimes cannot be understood by the blunt material senses, prakritendriya grahiyanayi. Then how do you know? Hai svaprakasha, they are manifested independently when you sincerely serve the shakti-avesha nama with attention, with transcendental faith in his unmanifest presence before you. He will purify us, and he on his own will manifest that Chaitanya rasa vighraha nama to our consciousness, to our spiritual senses, not material senses.

## The Teachings and Influence of Lord Buddha | HH Stoka Krishna Swami
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### incarnation, divine appearance

Tathaskalau sampravrttye sammohaya suradvisham buddho namna anjana sutaha kikateshu bhavishyati. Suta Goswami is speaking in the future tense. Bhavishyati means will appear in the future. Lord Buddha is a powerful incarnation of the personality of Godhead. Buddha is an empowered incarnation, shaktiavesha avatara.

---
### non-violence, Vedic sacrifice, atheism
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/k46-SZm7HY0?t=26
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Lord Buddha preached non-violence, taking pity on the poor animals."
**Summary:** This segment describes the historical context of Buddha's appearance, highlighting the prevalence of animal sacrifice and atheism, and Buddha's subsequent preaching of non-violence.

Of course for us now, Buddha appeared in the past because 5000 years have elapsed since Kali Yuga began. So Buddha came about 2500 years back. He appeared in the province of Gaya, Bihar as a son of Anjana. At the time when Lord Buddha appeared, the people in general were atheistic and preferred animosity. They preferred animosity over the animal flesh. On the plea of Vedic sacrifice, every place was practically turned into a slaughterhouse, and animal killing was indulged in unrestrictedly. Lord Buddha preached non-violence, taking pity on the poor animals. He preached that he did not believe in the tenets of the Vedas, and stressed the adverse psychological effect of non-violence.

---
### Vedic interpretation, animal sacrifice, mantra power
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/k46-SZm7HY0?t=77
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "When the Vedas are not accepted through the authoritative disciplic succession, the casual readers of the Vedas are misled by the flowery language of that system of knowledge."
**Summary:** This segment explains the Vedic perspective on animal sacrifice, emphasizing the importance of proper interpretation through disciplic succession and the testing of mantra power.

He preached that he did not believe in the tenets of the Vedas, and stressed the adverse psychological effect incurred by animal killing. When the Vedas are not accepted through the authoritative disciplic succession, the casual readers of the Vedas are misled by the flowery language of that system of knowledge. They have no depth of knowledge. Now animal killing is specifically allowed in the Vedic sacrifices to test the power of the mantra being chanted by a particular Brahman.

---
### animal sacrifice, revival, moral discipline, delusion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/k46-SZm7HY0?t=122
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Lord Buddha deluded the atheists because such atheists who followed his principle did not believe in God but they kept their absolute faith in Lord Buddha who himself was the incarnation of God."
**Summary:** This segment clarifies the Vedic understanding of animal sacrifice as a process of revival and explains how Lord Buddha's teachings, while seemingly atheistic, served to instill moral discipline and faith in him as an incarnation of God.

What is done is an old cow or an old bull is offered in sacrifice but after that sacrifice the animal is revived. The soul in the animal's body is getting a new body. Less intelligent men of the age of Kali who had no faith in God followed the principle of Lord Buddha and for the time being they were trained in moral discipline and non-violence, the preliminary steps for proceeding further on the path of God realization. Lord Buddha deluded the atheists because such atheists who followed his principle did not believe in God but they kept their absolute faith in Lord Buddha who himself was the incarnation of God.

---
### Vedic purpose, God realization, faith
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/k46-SZm7HY0?t=175
**Verse References:** BG 15.15
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "He made the faithless faithful to him."
**Summary:** This segment explains that the purpose of the Vedas is to lead one to God realization and highlights Lord Buddha's mercy in making the faithless faithful to him, ultimately leading them towards belief in God.

According to the Bhagavad Gita Vedascha Sarvair Ahameva Vedyah. The whole system of the Vedas is to lead one gradually to the path of the Supreme Lord. To know the Supreme Lord, the individual soul, the cosmic situation and the relation between all these items. When the relation is known, the relative function begins. Thus the faithless people were made to believe in God in the form of Lord Buddha. That was the mercy of Lord Buddha. He made the faithless faithful to him.

---

## The effect of touching Krishna Nama | Soulful Japa
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### Krishna Nama, Association, Attention
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/HnJilmUifAo?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "There is no question of any other alternative for our mind than to receive him, swagadam, welcome him and our attention should embrace that sound."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of welcoming Krishna through chanting Hare Krishna and focusing our attention on the sound, leading to a deeper connection.

So that is what we yesterday discussed, the importance of, even though we are in the material world, we are in a dreamlike situation, having forgotten Krishna, we are real, the body is unreal, we are real, our attention is real. So Krishna wants what? Not our body, auto japa, he wants to give us association. So after having invited him on our tongue, welcomed him, Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare, we cannot ignore him. So if you simply know that you are welcoming him, you are welcoming him. There is no question of any other alternative for our mind than to receive him, swagadam, welcome him and our attention should embrace that sound. And that embrace, when you chant and hear that very sound, you are actually embracing.

### Shakti Avesha Nama, Taste, Satchidananda
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/HnJilmUifAo?t=71
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Similarly for us, immediately to feel that I like the association of Satchidananda, the Satchidananda is Nama on my tongue."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that chanting and hearing the sound of the Holy Name allows one to experience the taste of Satchidananda, but our spiritual senses are currently dull, hindering our appreciation.

That is why that taste comes. Because in Shakti Avesha Nama, first you will get taste of Shakti, you get empowered without tiring. By the mercy of the Lord, you will be able to go on chanting, wanting to chant more and more. Just like if you like somebody's association, then you want more and more of that association, is it not? Similarly for us, immediately to feel that I like the association of Satchidananda, the Satchidananda is Nama on my tongue. Where our spiritual senses, our spiritual mind and attention is all dull now. I told you, dreamy attention. Because of that we are not able to appreciate the presence of Satchidananda, Nama.

---
### Contamination, Chitta, Rubber Gloves Analogy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/HnJilmUifAo?t=138
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Limitation is us. Limitation is our Chitta."
**Summary:** Using the analogy of rubber gloves on a hot plate, the speaker illustrates how our contaminated mind (Chitta) acts as a barrier, preventing us from fully experiencing the taste of Shakti Avesha Nama, and that the limitation lies within us.

We are unable to. Yet the fact remains that something is flowing into us. Another example I have given several times is about if you wear rubber gloves on your hand and you keep it on a hot plate, you won't feel the heat. But if you hold it for some time, slowly you will feel the heat. Why? Because the heat goes through the rubber gloves and then you start feeling it. In the same way, even though Shakti Avesha Nama is on my tongue, if I chant and hear, I am not getting the full taste. Please understand. Because still the rubber gloves is there. My mind is contaminated. My Chittam is contaminated. Through the contaminated, through the gloves only I am touching. My attention is coming through that and touching. So I will definitely not feel the full. Somebody asked if I have Satchidananda, why immediately I am not enlightened. So limitation is us. Limitation is our Chitta.

---
### Embrace, False Ego, Spiritual Awakening, Shuddha Nama
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/HnJilmUifAo?t=213
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "This false ego is like the rubber gloves. Going to go on melting. Finally real ego is there."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that through the 'hearing embrace' of chanting and hearing, the warmth of the Nama gradually melts away our false ego, leading to the awakening of our spiritual senses and the ability to chant and hear the Shuddha Nama of the Lord, ultimately connecting us to the Dhamma and spiritual realization.

And then we touch the Nama of the Lord on the tongue. That is called the hearing embrace or the attention embrace. And bead after bead we will feel this warmth. I don't want to mislead you by saying that okay you will become ecstatic. Bead after bead if you chant and hear that very sound. No, it will take time. But heat will come. Some heat will come. And you go on. What is that called? Embrace. Continue the attention embrace. What will happen? More and more heat as it comes, more duration you hold, more heat reaches your head. Slowly it will also melt the rubber. This exact action of the Shakti Ayesha Nama. It will melt. It will dissolve our false ego. Dissolve our illusions. And then our pure ego will manifest. When the pure ego touches with its attention. Now real ego. Real ego is through false ego we are touching the Nama. So what is the result? This false ego is like the rubber gloves. Going to go on melting. Finally real ego is there. Real ego is there. Then real mind is there. Spiritual mind is there. It awakens. Then spiritual tongue awakens. Spiritual mind, senses, ears awaken. You can chant and hear the Shuddha Nama of the Lord. Unmodified. Un是啊. Only the Shuddhas can do it. You can do it. It doesn't really matter. When you connect to the Shuddhas it doesn't matter. Any spiritual environment is the same. As long you connect to them it is the same. Everything is in harmony. An sudden spiritual realization takes place. When you connect to the Dhamma you feel all things. As long you connect to the Dhamma you feel all things. When you connect to the Dhamma you feel all things as well. When you connect to the Dhamma, you feel all things need to be part of each other.

## Understanding Death and Rebirth | HH Stoka Krishna Swami
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### immutability of the soul, death and rebirth, duty
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/BOLAMYUT9Dk?t=0
**Verse References:** BG 2.22, BG 2.27
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "As a person puts on new garments giving up old and useless ones, the soul similarly accepts new bodies, material bodies giving up old and useless ones."
**Summary:** The soul is immutable and undergoes transmigration, accepting new bodies upon the death of the old, and Krishna advises Arjuna not to lament in the discharge of his duty.

It is immutable, it does not undergo any change. Whatever changes we see are changes of the body or even the changes that we perceive in a person are the changes in the mind, but the soul is different from the body and the mind, so the soul is unchanging, it is immutable. As a person puts on new garments giving up old and useless ones, the soul similarly accepts new bodies, material bodies giving up old and useless ones. So Krishna explains that when the body becomes old and useless, that is when we give up this body and we accept another new material body. That change of body is called death and rebirth, punar janma. Krishna also says for one who has taken birth, death is certain and for one who dies, birth is certain. Therefore he tells Arjuna, you should not lament in the unavoidable discharge of your duty as a Kshatriya. You should not lament in the unavoidable discharge of your duty as a Kshatriya.

---

## Why Lord Krishna Accepts the Smallest Offering? | HH Stoka Krishna Swami | Gaura Mandala Parikrama
**Speaker:** HH Stoka Krishna Swami

### deity worship, Saligram Shila, Krishna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/LKEdvValcxA?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Of all the Deities that you can worship, all types of Deity worship you can do, the easiest is to worship the Saligram Shila."
**Summary:** The speaker explains why Advaita Acharya chose to worship Krishna in the form of Saligram Shila, highlighting its self-manifest nature and the ease of worship.

In the people, other than the Supreme Lord Sri Krishna Himself, comes and delivers the people. So, for this reason, he began to worship Krishna, particularly he chose to worship Krishna in the form of Saligram Shila. Why? There are several reasons, but two important reasons I'll mention. Saligram Shila is self-manifest, a self-manifest Deity in this world. Saligram Shila is Krishna appearing in the form of a stone with certain markings, and you directly take the Saligram Shila and begin worshipping. You don't have to do what is called as Prana Pratishta. You don't have to install this Deity. This is a very, very special Deity of Krishna. Eight forms and eight different substances, we can make the Deity according to Shastra. And then after making the Deity according to scriptural directions, we have to install. Saligram Shila, no installation. Directly you take and start worshipping. So Saligram Shila is Sakshat Krishna. So therefore, he actually decided to worship the Saligram Shila. The second reason is that... No, the second reason is, it is very, very, very, very easy to worship the Saligram Shila. Of all the Deities that you can worship, all types of Deity worship you can do, the easiest is to worship the Saligram Shila.

### devotion, Saligram Shila, Krishna's reciprocation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/LKEdvValcxA?t=144
**Verse References:** BG 9.26
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "He sells Himself completely to His devotee. He offers Himself to His devotee in exchange for just one Tudasi leaf and a palmful of water offered with devotion."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the ease of worshipping Saligram Shila with devotion, emphasizing Krishna's reciprocation by 'selling Himself' to the devotee for a simple offering of a Tulasi leaf and water.

Of course, as Krishna says, the only requirement for doing any service, devotional service of worshipping the Supreme Lord is our Bhakti. But with devotion, the Saligram Shila is to be worshipped with two items. Tudasi, I will tell you a prayer which Ajay Dacharya offered to Krishna. All of you repeat this prayer. Tudasi, dada, matrena, jalasya, chulakenava, vikrinete, swamatmanam, bhaktebhyo, bhaktavatsalaham. Now this verse was chanted by Advaita Acharya to worship Krishna in the form of Saligram Shila. Saligram Shila is very easy to please by just offering two items. Tudasi, dada, matrena. Just one leaf of Tudasi. One leaf of Tudasi. As Krishna says in Bhagavad Gita, patram, pushpam, phalam, toyam, yome, bhaktya prayatchati. One can offer Krishna with devotion either a leaf or a flower or one fruit or a little water. So these two items, patram and toyam, are particularly mentioned in this prayer to Krishna. Tudasi, dada, matrena, particularly Tudasi leaf. And jalasya, chulakenava, one palmful of water. One palmful of water. Of course, the water should be Ganga Jal. But you can invoke Ganga just by chanting the name of Ganga. Okay? And just these two items, Krishna says, if it is offered with devotion, particularly to the Saligram Shila, Krishna thinks that there is nothing in my possession that is equivalent to one Tudasi leaf and a palmful of water offered with devotion by my devotee. So how does, how can Krishna reciprocate? He can't just receive whatever the devotee is offering with love and devotion and be, you know, just be just neutral about it. He has to give back something. He has to reciprocate. So Krishna considers, what can I give my devotee? My devotee will never accept anything that I give which is actually in my possession. My devotee doesn't want anything. In exchange for what the devotees offer, they don't want anything. So what can I give? And then Krishna thinks, also Krishna thinks, I have nothing in my possession which is equivalent to any of these two items. So, He Himself, vikrinete svamatmanam He sells Himself completely to His devotee. He offers Himself to His devotee. In exchange for just one Tudasi leaf and a palmful of water offered with devotion. This is the way Krishna reciprocates.

### devotee's property, Krishna's affection, surrender
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/LKEdvValcxA?t=382
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna is the property of His pure devotees."
**Summary:** The speaker continues to explain how Krishna becomes the property of His devotees due to His affection, referencing the story of Durvasa Muni and highlighting that only pure devotees can give us Krishna.

So this prayer from the Gautamiya Tantra came to the mind of Advaita Acharya. How to actually make sure that Krishna comes down for this purpose of delivering the whole world. So he decided to worship the Saligram Shila and based on this prayer he was sure that Krishna will definitely come down and answer my prayer if I offer the Saligram Shila with devotion just one Tudasi leaf and a palmful of water. vikrinete svamatmanam bhaktebhyo bhakta vatsalah Because Krishna is very very very very very affectionate to His devotees. Krishna is very affectionate to His devotees. So in exchange for the devotees offering one Tudasi leaf and a palmful of water, Krishna sells Himself to His devotee. When Durvasamani approached Krishna in Vaikuntha so he was seeking protection against the attack of the Sudarshan Chakra. So Vishnu said of course he approached Vishnu in Vaikuntha, Narayana. So Narayana said I am not independent I have sold myself to my devotees I am the property of my devotees. So if at all you want anything from me you have to go to my devotee. I can't give you because I am not independent I am not free. So like that actually this prayer also says Krishna sells Himself to His devotee. So therefore Krishna is the property of His pure devotees. They have got pure devotion to Him. Krishna is the property of His pure devotees. Kuntideva also says this So such Akechana devotees Krishna is their property. Bhaktinath Thakur also says Krishna se tumhara Krishna dite paro tumhara shakti aache. The Vaishnava Acharya Krishna is His property. So Krishna can be given to us if we want Krishna then Prabhupada only Prabhupada can give us Krishna nobody else can give us Krishna. So Advaita Acharya worshipped with this prayer and Chaitanya Mahaprabhu appeared in answer to Advaita Acharya's prayers. Hare Krishna

### Radharani's love, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, internal reasons
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/LKEdvValcxA?t=721
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees
**Key Quote:** "Desiring to understand the glory of Radharani's love."
**Summary:** The speaker begins to explain the three internal reasons for Chaitanya Mahaprabhu's appearance, focusing on Krishna's desire to understand the glory of Radharani's love.

This describes the three internal reasons for Chaitanya Mahaprabhu's appearing in this world. So the prayer itself contains the reasons directly. Desiring to understand the glory of Radharani's love the wonderful Okay, that's the first reason. Desiring to understand the glory of Radharani's love. Now what is special about Radharani's love that even Krishna is so attracted by it and he desires to understand what is the kind of love that Radharani has for me. What is the significance of this? See, Krishna loves Radharani Radharani loves Krishna. Okay, it's mutual, reciprocal. Okay. But Krishna is actually seeing that the pleasure I experience by loving Radharani the happiness I get by loving Radharani Krishna is unlimited Lord. Everything about him is unlimited. Obviously he experiences unlimited pleasure in loving his devotees, particularly Radharani. Okay. But still Krishna feels that Radharani experiences more happiness in her devotional service to me offering her love to me her loving service than I experience in actually loving Radharani or serving Radharani or offering my reciprocation to Radharani. So he is very curious how is it that Radharani is experiencing more happiness more pleasure than me in her devotional service to me. So Krishna is unable to figure this out fathom this understand this. So in order to understand this he takes the position of Radharani as a devotee and he himself worships Krishna he himself serves Krishna he himself offers his love to Krishna. Okay. So the first reason is desiring to understand the glory of Radharani's love.

### Krishna's qualities, Radharani's love, attraction
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/LKEdvValcxA?t=890
**Verse References:** SB 1.7.10
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees
**Key Quote:** "Krishna is full of unlimited wonderful qualities. But who is able to relish those wonderful qualities the best?"
**Summary:** The speaker explains the second internal reason: Krishna wants to understand the special qualities that Radharani alone relishes through her love, despite Krishna possessing unlimited wonderful qualities that attract everyone.

The second one is the wonderful qualities in him that she alone relishes through her love. Krishna is full of unlimited wonderful qualities. But who is able to relish those wonderful qualities the best devotees relish those qualities of course of Krishna. They are full of appreciation and they treasure that Krishna's wonderful qualities. And there is a verse in the Bhagavatam first canto with Chaitanya Mahaprabhu explained in sixty one ways Atma Rama verse. What does that verse say? Atma Ramasya Munayo nirgranta api urukrame kurvanti ahetuteem bhakti ittham bhuta guno harihi Okay, this is a very big explanation is there. Ittham bhuta guno harihi Krishna is able to attract everybody in the whole creation, existence by his unlimited qualities. And because each jiva, each devotee has got something unique in their constitution. So if you are attracted by one particular quality of Krishna I will be attracted by some other quality of Krishna. Somebody else will be attracted by some other quality of Krishna. So there are no qualities which is lacking in Krishna which will attract every single jiva, every single devotee to Krishna. Therefore Krishna is the only one who is all attracted. Krishna is the only one who is all attracted. But in spite of Krishna having all the wonderful qualities by which he attracts everyone still he is wondering what are the special things about Radharani that she alone relishes through her love all those wonderful qualities in Krishna. So he wants to know that. So that is the second reason why he appears as taken in half with him. He takes the position of Radharani and tries to relish those qualities in him in Krishna which he has taken in half with him. He has taken the position of Radharani and tries to relish those qualities in him in Krishna with Radharani which Radharani otherwise alone relishes.

And people don't have devotion in this material world. Atheists doing whatever they do to get some fulfillment of desire including the Asuras. It is Krishna alone as Paramatma who satisfies all his desires. Okay. So Krishna actually satisfies everyone's desires and particularly he is very pleased to satisfy his devotees who never ask him anything who never ask him anything. So Krishna is looking for what does my devotee want what will satisfy him. The devotees don't ask pure devotees don't ask anything from Krishna. So what will Krishna do? Krishna is always looking for an opportunity to serve his devotees in any way he can. Just like devotees are always looking for opportunity to serve Krishna in all possible ways to please him. Same thing. So now Radharani she relishes delicious Okay. Now last last last reason the happiness she feels when she realizes the sweetness of his love that is the third reason. Now Krishna says I'll complete this translation the Supreme Lord Hari richly endowed with her emotions appeared from the womb of Srimati and the Lord and the Lord of the world and the universe as I said in the book of Srimati and the universe and the universe and the universe in this book.
